title: The Amazing Spider-Man #1 (Marvel, 1963). Spectacular first issue as Marvel Comics' most sensational hero is awarded his very own title. Spidey must face the challenge of the entire Fantastic Four in this book as well as the menace of the mysterious Chameleon. Supporting character J. Jonah Jameson makes his debut appearance with vintage art by Steve Ditko and Jack "King" Kirby. Spider-Man's origin is also re-told as timid teenager Peter Parker is bitten by a radio-active spider, empowering him with uncanny power and abilities. Heritage Comics Auctions is proud to offer one of the finest copies of this super-key Silver-Age comic ever to be placed for public auction. An out-and-out beautiful copy that has an immaculate spine and only the most infinitesimal stress line at the upper part of the top staple. Perfectly flat with a very tight spine, exact centering and exceptional cover edges (no "Marvel chipping"). Color inks are sharp and very strong (no smudging) with excellent gloss and outstanding eye-appeal. There are no defects of note save for the most miniscule color flake at the lower right edge of the front cover and the most minor of nicks at the very bottom of the spine. Interior paper is very supple and fresh. An incredible book in every way. At present, Amazing Spider-Man is the undisputed, highest demand title in the entire marketplace. With the earnest expectation of the upcoming motion picture, due out in the Summer, 2002, Spider-Man titles have no equal regarding their potential to rise in value and demand. This Amazing Spider-Man #1 is, without a doubt, one of the best copies in existence! CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $25,000.

John Buscema and Joe Sinnott - Original Cover Art for Silver Surfer #1 (Marvel, 1968). Heritage Comics Auctions is very proud to offer one of the most significant pieces of original artwork ever to be placed at public auction. This classic scene is beautifully elegant with Buscema's idealistic rendering of the Silver Surfer as he soars down from outer space on his cosmic board straight at the on-looker. This first issue would establish the high level of excellence for the entire series, an all-time favorite among collectors. Buscema's work is considered by many as the absolute purest and most classic ever to be done on the Sentinel of the Spaceways. This piece will likely always be an icon, fondly remembered as one of the pinnacles of the Silver Age of comic book artwork. Pencil, pen and inks on heavy illustration paper with title stats intact, but other type stats are not present, as if they were done on a separate overlay (not included). Therefore, this piece is offered in its purest state. Art is in excellent condition with no pieces out, no tears, no creasing and no heavy white-out corrections. There are 4 punch-holes in the paper (2 at top margin and 2 at bottom margin) that do not affect the artwork, and were probably used when photographing the cover for the printing production. Image size measures 9 3/4" X 15".

Special Edition Comics #1 Denver pedigree (Fawcett, 1940). Key issue (published prior to Captain Marvel Adventures #1) is the very first comic book devoted entirely to the "Big Red Cheese" with art by co-creator C.C. Beck. A stunner in every way with superbly rich, vibrant colors such as deep fire-reds, canary-yellows and nice shades of orange. Fully flat and extremely tight with perfectly clean cover edges and a tight spine with no staple stress. An exceptional copy with tremendous overall eye-appeal. One of the finest copies known to exist. CGC NM 9.4 Cream pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $9,200.

Mad #1 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1952). The humor book that started it all! This is a beautiful example of this classic, and historically significant book. From the personal collection of William Gaines, this is copy #3 of 12 from the Gaines File pedigree collection. Great eye appeal, clean cuts, and sharp corners with just the slightest imperfection at the bottom of the spine. According to CGC, this is one of the nicest they have seen. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $6,500.

Brenda Starr #14 (#2) Mile High pedigree (Four Star, 1948). Classic bondage cover, with Jack Kamen art featured in this issue. This stunning copy has intense colors with a near perfect spine, secure staples (no stress at all) and exact centering. An ideal example of why comics from this legendary collection are so highly desired being unsurpassed in structure, quality and paper freshness. This is, most likely, the very best copy of Brenda Starr #14 in the world. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $760.

Jack Kirby and Syd Shores - Original Cover Art for Captain America #108 (Marvel, 1968). An unbeatable combination - Jack Kirby, the undisputed "King" of comics, and his most legendary creation, the star-spangled Avenger, Captain America! In this unforgettable cover, Cap pastes the Trapster with both feet, as a helpless Sharon Carter looks on. Bold, dynamic and action-packed, this was Kirby at the top of his game, ably assisted by the brilliant brush strokes of Syd Shores. There is minimal white-out on this cover, which is standard size, 11.25" x 17.5", and in excellent condition. The title and corner box are both stats, the rest is all original. A beautiful cover, likely to be the centerpiece of any original art collection.

Wonder Woman #1 (DC, 1942). A lovely copy of this key book starring the Amazing Amazon. There is some minor spine stress, and a certain amount of soiling/shadowing on the front and back covers. An extremely attractive and desirable copy overall. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $5,280; VF 8.0 value = $11,400.

War Against Crime #10 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1949). If you're an EC horror fanatic, here's a book you can't pass up. It's the first appearance of the Vault Keeper, and the first appearance of the Vault of Horror. Best of all, it's from the Gaines File pedigree collection. According to the latest CGC population report, only two copies in the world are nicer than this. The most minor abrasions at the top and bottom front spine are the only defects on this tight, flat book. Exceptional eye-appeal. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $2,000.

Amazing Fantasy #15 (Marvel, 1962). The Granddaddy of all Silver Age Keys, featuring the very first appearance of Spider-Man. Leading off with Jack Kirby's "amazing" cover, and the highly imaginative work of Lee & Ditko, this is easily the most important superhero launch since Action #1. A very nice mid-grade copy that shows light spine stress and moderate wear around the edges, but none of the telltale "Marvel chipping." Front cover retains excellent color and clarity. Back cover has a tiny (1/4") tear at the top edge of the spine. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $3,500; VF 8.0 value = $12,825.

The Amazing Spider-Man #1 (Marvel, 1963). After a spectacular showing in Amazing Fantasy #15, Spider-Man gets his own comic book, with a guest-appearance by the Fantastic Four. Probably the third-most important Marvel Silver Age Key (after Amazing Fantasy #15 and Fantastic Four #1), the first issue of Amazing Spider-Man is a red-hot book in just about anyone's estimation. This outstanding example features good clarity and freshness, with reasonably square corners and a perfectly-registered cut, and none of the telltale "Marvel chipping" that mars so many books from this period. Apparent flaws include some very light spine stress (which breaks the ink only in the green text strip just below the Human Torch), moderate dinginess to the back cover, and the bottom staple rolled approximately 1/8" onto the front cover. Given the relative scarcity of this book in high grade, this is a truly "amazing" copy that would be the prize of any Spider-Man collection. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 VF 8.0 value = $8,800; NM 9.4 value = $25,000.

Wally Wood - Original Art for "Clean Start" from Incredible Science Fiction #30 - Complete 8-Page Story (EC, 1955). A classic and famous story by a gifted storyteller. Whether it's weird aliens or expertly drawn scenes from world history, Woody could do it all, as he proves in a mere eight pages. Drawn in a pleasing large format (the art measures approximately 15" x 20.5"), Wood's art really shines here. The pages are in very good condition, but there is evidence that some type corrections have been lost, leaving glue stains on the page. In most cases, these are fairly minor, but there is a rather prominent stain at the top of page 7, and an area at the bottom of the same page where some type has been lost. Most of the type is done on paste-ups, but the art is all original, with very little white-out and minimal blue pencil throughout. Only the first page is shown here. To see the complete story, please visit www.HeritageComics.com.

The Amazing Spider-Man #7 (Marvel, 1963). Early issue has the second appearance of the Vulture with cover and story art by Steve Ditko. Very scarce in the top grades, this is one of the finest copies we have ever seen. Perfectly flat and clean with a very sharp spine and no "Marvel chipping". There is just the slightest nick at the upper right corner of the front cover. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,050.

All-Flash #3 Mile High pedigree (DC, 1941). In a collection known for the high quality of its books, this is certainly one of the nicest. Looking as if it just rolled off the printing press, this book's only visible defect is that it is slightly mis-cut. A small pencil notation appears in the upper left corner, but does not in any way reduce the beauty of this near-pristine comic. A welcome addition to any high-grade Golden Age collection. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,600.

Action Comics #58 Pennsylvania pedigree (DC, 1943). Famous WW II "Slap a Jap" cover as Superman presses out the War Bonds and Stamps promotional posters. Exceptionally killer copy has tremendous color depth and incredible eye-appeal! The best copy we have ever seen! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $800.

Mad #3 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1953). One of the most trend-setting comics ever published, MAD has become a cultural icon. This is a beautiful example of the beginnings of MAD, before it went to magazine size. From the personal stash of MAD's publisher comes this copy, one of the nicest in existence according to CGC's current population report. A lovely copy that, except for a little tanning on the back cover, looks like it did when it came right off the presses. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $900.

Vault of Horror #13 Gaines File pedigree 6/9 (EC, 1950). Remember those comics that terrified you as a kid? This was one of them. Despite the issue number, this is the true second issue of this title (it was WAR AGAINST CRIME for the first 11 issues). It's also memorable for the infamous Morphine story contained within its pages. Additionally, this is one of the scarcest Gaines File pedigree books, as there were only nine copies of this issue in the collection, instead of the usual twelve. According to CGC's most recent census report, a finer copy of this desirable book may not exist. Virtually perfect, this book is a pleasure to look at. CGC NM/MT 9.8 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,015.

Superman #2 (DC, 1939). From the creative minds of Jerry Siegel and Joe Shuster comes the second issue of this legendary comic title. This book shows a fair amount of spine wear and some light cover soiling, along with a small tear and some dust shadowing on the back cover. CGC FN+ 6.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $3,060.

Tomahawk #1 Mile High pedigree (DC, 1950). Debut issue with "Brand-New Adventures of America's Favorite Frontier Hero" with excellent cover/ story art by Fred Ray. This key issue has a wonderful, dark cover with intense deep-blue colors as Tomahawk faces down a group of hostile Indians. A beautiful book in every way with exceptional color strike, perfectly clean cover edges, sharp corners, near flawless spine and the "trademark" Mile High pedigree white paper. CGC notes: "Manufactured with large piece out margin of 2nd page, slightly affects story." This copy is unequalled in structure quality and freshness, as are most comic books from this most legendary of all pedigree collections.From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,500.

World's Best Comics #1 (DC, 1941). Superman and Batman were already big stars by the Spring of 1941, so putting them together on the cover of this classic issue must have seemed like a natural. The only issue of this title (by the next issue, the book would be renamed World's Finest Comics), this was 96 pages of non-stop action featuring Superman, Batman & Robin, The Crimson Avenger, Zatana, Johnny Thunder, and many others. This is a very nice copy of this key Golden Age book, with slight foxing and some minor chipping at the edges of the front and back covers. The spine has slight damage to the top and bottom, but is overall in excellent condition (this is a difficult comic to find in high-grade, as the square binding is often quite worn). There is also a 1" tear on the first page. This is one of the finest copies available of this desirable book; according to CGC's latest census results, there are only two copies that are nicer than this one. An excellent opportunity to own a beautiful copy of this fantastic comic. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $4,080; NM 9.4 value = $8,840.

Mad #2 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1952). An excellent example of this rare book. Flat, tight spine, sharp corners, fabulous presentation. This is copy #3 of 12 from the Gaines File pedigree Collection. Classic Jack Davis baseball cover. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,500.

Mad #4 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1953). From the personal collection of William Gaines comes this exceptional copy of a classic comic. Good luck finding a nicer copy of this book. According to CGC, this is as nice as they've ever seen. Beautiful eye appeal, flat, tight spine, nice gloss...it doesn't get better than this. All wrapped up in a classic Kurtzman cover. CGC NM/MT 9.8 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $900.

Mad #5 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1953). Low distribution, high grade and the Gaines File pedigree pedigree make this an incredibly desirable book! A nearly perfect copy with a tight spine, sharp corners, and just the most minor chipping at the top of the cover. You'll have to search long and hard for a nicer copy of this hard-to-find issue. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,600.

Sensation Comics #1 (DC, 1942). Straight from the pages of All-Star Comics #8 comes Wonder Woman, the Amazing Amazon, in this exceptionally desirable Golden Age gem! Along with a classic H.G. Peter Wonder Woman cover, this issue boasts the debut of future Justice Society members Mr. Terrific and Wildcat, as well as the first appearance of Shelly Moldoff's classic Black Pirate. Overstreet lists this as #25 on his list of Top 100 Golden Age books. This copy has moderate spine wear and chipping along the right edge, as well as a small "55" pencilled in the upper left front cover and a 1 1/2" crease in the lower right front cover. The centerfold is detached at the top staple only. This is a solid copy of this classic comic. CGC VG/FN 5.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $2,160; FN 6.0 value = $6,480.

Strange Adventures #10 White Mountain pedigree (DC, 1951). Great book featuring "Amazing Science-Fiction Tales", starring Captain Comet with a wonderful giant Space-Alien cover and art by Murphy Anderson, Carmine Infantino and Gil Kane. Gorgeous comic has color inks that seem to leap right off the cover with intense fire-reds and brilliant banana-yellows. Completely flat, very tight and fresh book has outstanding eye appeal. A beauty with a date stamp on first page (this date stamp is quite common on White Mountain books, and is one of the prime identifiers for this pedigree). One of the finest copies on planet Earth! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $900.

Brenda Starr (Vol. 2), #10 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1949). Featuring "Romantic Adventures for Teen-agers" as Brenda Starr catches a would-be-assassin in her dressing room. Absolutely stellar book! As near to perfection as you can get with super sharp, rich colors, and completely fresh white "Mile High" paper. Most likely the very best copy in existence, bar none! CGC NM/MT 9.8 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value: $610.

Batman #1 (DC, 1940). A rough but complete copy of this seminal key; the Caped Crusaders swing into action for their very first issue, which features the first appearance of The Joker and The Cat (later Catwoman), as well as the origin of Batman reprinted from Detective Comics. CGC notes 2 pieces off the first 2 pages, which affects art, but not story. Also tape on interior spine and pages (spine appears to be completely split and taped). CGC GD- 1.8 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value: $6,067.

Famous Funnies #209 (Eastern Color, 1953). The cover caption says it best: "Buck is a One Man Army of Greased Lightning"! The Buck Rogers series begins here with a beautiful cover by the legendary Frank Frazetta. A super-killer copy in every way with full, fresh color strike, great gloss and perfect cover edges. An unbelievable copy and the best we have ever seen. CGC NM+ 9.6 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM value = $975.

Superman #14 (DC, 1942). The greatest of all superheroes stands tall and majestic on this fabulous patriotic WWII cover by Fred Ray. Extremely bold colors accent the jet-black background beautifully. One of the finest and most desirable Golden Age covers of all time. Completely flat and very fresh copy has fresh white paper, great overall eye-appeal and just the slightest bit of corner wear. Misplaced staples on this book affect story. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $3,200. c

War Against Crime #11 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1950). One of the nicest copies of this book in existence. This copy comes from the legendary Gaines File pedigree collection, and presents a nearly flawless appearance. Flat and clean, with razor sharp corners. CG NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $1,155.

Alex Horley - Avengers Litho Art (Marvel, 2000). Incredibly dynamic portrait of The Avengers, circa issue #8, with Kang the Conqueror looming menacingly in the background, as the stalwart team springs into action. The vivid colors and kinetic energy of this piece have to be seen to be believed. Originally published as a Dynamic Forces Limited Edition Lithograph. Acrylic on masonite, approximately 18" x 24".

Superman #1 (DC, 1939). This Golden-Age key was the first time any comic book was devoted to a single character, a radical idea in 1939. This landmark first issue features, "The Complete Story of the Daring Exploits of the One and Only Superman." It includes the stories from Action Comics #1-4 by co-creators Jerry Siegel (writer) and Joe Shuster (art) with two new pages added to the origin plus four new pages that were omitted from the Action story. This copy has good colors with extensive amateur restoration including a large piece replaced at the top left corner of the front cover. In addition, the centerfold and back cover have been replaced with color photocopies and the spine has been reinforced. This is a great low-grade copy for the Superman collector on a budget! Condition: PR. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $14,000.

Exciting Comics #26 Mile High pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1943). A truly exciting Schomburg WWII cover highlights this Golden Age beauty. According to CGC, this is by far the nicest copy in existence. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $525.

Johnny Craig - Original Cover Art for War Against Crime #9 (EC, 1949). From the late, great Johnny Craig comes this excellent cover featuring action both in the ring and at ringside. "The Kid" goes for a knockout while the cops close in on two "muggs". A superb, large (approximately 15" x 22") example of a dynamic cover from this talented craftsman. There is no white-out in evidence on this page. The title, logo and text box are all stats, but the word balloons and all art are original. There are several small cocoa-colored stains at the bottom of the page, which do not affect the art at all. Also, there is a small 1/2" tear at the right edge which comes right to the black border of the art, but not inside it. A thrilling original for any collection. As an added bonus, we are also including a CGC VG 4.0 copy of the comic book.

Mad #10 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). The #3 copy from the Gaines File pedigree, this is an outstanding example of this classic laffer. Only apparent problems are a small chip out of the top right corner, and a small color rub on the left edge of the cover, next to the "u" in "Jugular." CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $650.

Jack Davis - Original Art for "Undertaking Palor" from Tales From the Crypt #39 - Complete 8-Page Story (EC, 1953). One of the leading lights from the classic EC stable, Jack Davis could always be counted on for a gripping yarn sure to shock, scare and terrify. This story goes above and beyond, as a group of boys find out the truth about a series of unexplained poisonings. The first page features a wonderful image of The Crypt-Keeper, surrounded by skulls, body parts, and an assortment of ghouls. The art is large-sized, measuring 15" x 22", with notations at the margins, none of which affect the art. There is evidence that several type corrections have fallen off, leaving small glue stains, but none of these are extensive enough to ruin the look of this classic story. The title logo on the first page is a stat, all else is original Davis. Only the first page is shown here. To see the complete story, please visit www.HeritageComics.com.

Greg & Tim Hildebrandt - Spider-Man Trading Card Art (Marvel, 1996). Spectacular image of Spider-Man (in his then-new costume) framed against a giant spider-web, by the legendary team of Greg and Tim Hildebrandt. Originally used as a Fleer Premium Trading Card. Vibrant colors literally leap off the page, and a very iconic pose make this a must-have for any Spider-Man fanatic. 11" x 15", acrylic on board.

X-Men #1 (Marvel, 1963). The beginning of Marvel's Mutant Dynasty! A great high-grade copy, CGC notes "12.00" partially erased on cover and very light stains also on front cover. Even so, the eye-appeal on this book is incredible. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 VF 8.0 value = $3,915.

Mark Schultz - Original Cover Art for Predator: Hell and Hot Water #1 (1997). This amazing piece of artwork is rendered in a sleek, ink-and-drybrush style by the incredibly talented Mark Schultz. Very dynamic underwater image as the Predator dispatches a couple of frogmen as marine life glides placidly by. On a 14" x 20" board, with an image area of app. 11" x 17 1/2". Also includes an 8 1/2" x 11" color guide prepared by Schultz. Schultz does not often sell his artwork, so this is a particularly unique opportunity.

Phantom Lady #15 Bethlehem pedigree (Fox Features Syndicate, 1947). Phantom Lady uses her unique Black Ray to fight crime on the cover of this great issue, which also includes excellent interior art by Matt Baker. Superb copy has rich, sharp colors, very supple white cover background and supple interior paper. Perfectly flat and tight with great eye appeal, this is the best copy we have seen at any time. Rare in this top-grade. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $2,250.

Detective Comics #121 Mile High pedigree (DC, 1947). Absolutely stunning copy! Extremely fresh appearance, with crisp colors. Only visible problems are a very slight amount of wear at the base of the spine, and a slight mis-cut. Truly outstanding. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $650.

Curt Swan and Murphy Anderson - Original Cover Art for Superman's Girl Friend Lois Lane #105 (DC, 1970). Lois is married...but not to Superman! And her new husband is about to go to the electric chair! Will Lois have a "Death House Honeymoon?" A great cover, standard size, 11.5" x 17.5", featuring Lois, Superman, and "Ol' Sparky!" Minimal white-out, mostly around the word balloons, which are stats, as are the title and corner logos, and the Thorn illustration and text at the bottom of the page. An exceptional example from the classic Superman art team of Swan and Anderson.

Showcase #4 (DC, 1956). The book that started the Silver Age! Like a human lightning bolt, the Flash explodes onto the comics scene in this extremely desirable issue. Listed by Overstreet as #2 on his list of Top 10 Silver Age books. This copy shows moderate spine wear and several slight stains on the back cover, but overall a very attractive copy of this key issue. CGC FN+ 6.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $3,450; VF 8.0 value = $12,500.

Wally Wood and Russ Jones - Original Art for "The Mummy" from Monster World #1 - Complete 6-Page Story (Warren, 1964). Wally Wood was well-known as one of the greatest talents in comics, making his name as part of the legendary EC stable of artists. Several years after the demise of the EC horror titles, Wood applied his considerable skill to this adaptation of the classic Universal horror film starring Boris Karloff. With his wonderful sense of pacing and layout, and his uncanny ability to capture Karloff's amazing presence on paper, this story is a rare treat by a true master. It proved to be so popular that it was quickly reprinted in Eerie #11. Measuring approximately 14.5" x 23", this art is in very fine condition, with some yellowing evident, but minimal white-out and no blue-pencil. One page shows panels that seem to be pasted together, but all the art is original, except for the title logo on the first page, which is a stat. This is an excellent example of Wood's post-EC, pre-Marvel period, when he had truly mastered his artistic style. Only the first page is shown here. To see the complete story, please visit www.HeritageComics.com.

Mad #8 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1953). A truly exceptional copy of a truly exceptional comic. The only visible flaws include a very slight spine abrasion and very slight tanning on the back cover. Otherwise, rich colors, sharp corners and excellent page quality make this book a real treasure. CGC's census shows that a nicer copy has not been found. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 9.4 value = $650.

Mad Scatter/Lapel Pin (EC, 1957). Mad Magazine began offering five styles of jewelry manufactured by Astrahan of New York in June 1957, priced at $2 each or $3 for a pair of cuff links. Mad discontinued selling them by early 1958. Only 1,000 to 2,000 pieces were sold, according to the publisher, and all are excessively rare today with well under 100 pieces surviving. The lapel/scatter pins were described by MAD as follows: "Your good suit will look better with a 'What... Me Worry?' Kid lapel pin. It will look best with a lapel, but the pin will help. Or give it to your girl as a scatter pin. If you have no girl, scatter a few. You'll pin one!" Excellent condition. From the Grant Geissman collection.

The Incredible Hulk #3 (Marvel, 1962). The Hulk goes BERSERK in the thrill-packed third issue of his incredible title, starting off with an awesome Jack Kirby cover. In this issue, the Hulk's origin is retold, plus he gets banished to outer space, and meets up with the Ringmaster and Hercules. Whew! They don't make 'em like this anymore! This is a gorgeous copy with bright colors and tremendous eye-appeal. A tiny bit of edge wear, and some light spine stress are the only apparent defects. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $345; NM 9.4 value = $1650.

Strange Worlds #3 (Avon, 1951). Sci-Fi title showcasing "Astounding Super-Science Fantasies". Magnificent copy of this issue has a great double-headed dragon cover with exceptional artwork by the super talented trio of Wally Wood, Frank Frazetta and Everett Raymond Kinstler. Completely flat with a tight, near flawless spine and near perfect centering. There is a light dust shadow at top and left edge of back cover. Exceptional comic in every way! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,700.

Curt Swan and Murphy Anderson - Original Cover Art for Action Comics #384 (DC, 1970). An exceptional cover by two of the artists most associated with the Man of Steel. The fluoroscope reveals a "diabolical costume" with "weird powers" underneath Superman's familiar red and blue outfit...a costume Superman swears he knows nothing about! In excellent condition for its age, there are several spots of white-out and a light glue stain along the bottom of the image. Also, one of the word balloons shows some evidence of what could be rust stains. Title logo is a stat, as are the balloons, but all the rest of the art, which is standard size, 11.5" x 17.5", is original. This piece also comes with the original hand-colored color guide for this cover. An excellent piece from an increasingly hard-to-find era.

Fighting Yank #13 Mile High pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1945). The Fighting Yank to the rescue on this WWII bondage cover by Alex Schomburg. Near flawless book has an immaculate spine with no staple stress and incredibly deep color strike. An ideal example of why books from this legendary collection are so highly desired, unsurpassed in structure quality and paper freshness. Label note reads: "4 sets of Siamese pages." CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

Famous Funnies #211 (Eastern Color, 1954). A killer copy starring Buck Rogers is highlighted by a great Frank Frazetta cover. Perfectly flat, clean and tight copy has full, deep color inks and incredible gloss. Near flawless with a tight spine and an exceptional overall look. CGC NM 9.4 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $975.

Brenda Starr(Vol. 2), #4 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1948). Used in the Senate Investigations on Juvenile Delinquency and featured in Seduction of the Innocent, this comic has a very attractive bondage cover by Jack Kamen with stunning deep sea-blue color inks and fully fresh white paper. Completely flat and tight with very sharp spine, cover edges, corners and near exact centering. Very scarce in this high grade, this is the finest copy we have ever seen. CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $740.

The Avengers #1 (Marvel, 1963). Origin and first appearance of The Avengers (Thor, Iron Man, the Hulk, Ant-Man and the Wasp) with cover and story art by Jack "King" Kirby. Outstanding, perfectly flat and very tight comic has wonderful color depth, excellent gloss and no "Marvel chipping". Spine is sharp and flat with just a bit of wear in a few places. Label note reads: "70 written on back cover in pencil." This is one of the better copies of Avengers #1 we have seen in some time. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $3,200.

Detective Comics #38 (DC, 1940). The introduction of Robin, the Boy Wonder -- "The Sensational Character Find of 1940!" -- heralds this important Golden Age key. The outstanding cover is by Bob Kane and Jerry Robinson, based on the splash page to Robin's introductory story. This is a rough but complete low-grade copy, a perfect book for the collector who can't afford to spend megabucks to complete a run of Detective. Shows extensive wear, minor tears and creasing, and moderate tape repairs on both interior and exterior covers, but the cover image is still very bold, with bright colors and good clarity. An excellent low-grade example of this important issue. CGC G/VG 3.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $3,040; FN 6.0 value = $9,120.

House of Mystery #1 Northford pedigree (DC, 1952). DC's first horror comic features the cover story "Wanda Was a Werewolf". This is a beautiful copy of this tough issue, which has sharp, deep color strike, exact centering, perfectly clean edges and very sharp spine with no staple stress at all. Flat, clean and very tight with some light tanning on interior front and back cover. Very seldom seen above the VG to FN range, this is, without a doubt, one of the top copies of this key issue in existence.From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $2,000.

Superboy #1 (DC, 1949). The adventures of Superman when he was a boy! A very attractive copy of this desirable book. The front cover exhibits some spotting and a moderate amount of spine wear, as well as light tanning, a small stain, and a date stamp on the back cover. Overall strong eye-appeal. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $2,090; NM 9.4 value = $8,000.

Sub-Mariner Comics #1 (Timely, 1941). Timely's aquatic anti-hero in his own title! Placing #26 on Overstreet's list of Top 100 Golden Age Comics, this copy shows moderate spine wear, rusty staples, some dust shadowing, and a small piece of tape at the bottom spine. CGC notes "black ink on cover transfers through to interior cover". A nice copy of a key book. CGC VG 4.0 Light tan pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $2,160; FN 6.0 value = $6,480.

Mad #6 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1953). Classic Kurtzman cover. An excellent comic from Bill Gaines' personal collection, this book shows very minor chipping at the bottom right corner, and a small ink stain on the back cover. Otherwise flat, clean, and sharp with exceptional paper quality. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $650.

Mad #7 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1953). More MADness from EC regulars Wood, Elder, and Severin, and six pages of early cartoons from Kurtzman. This is from the legendary Gaines File, the #3 (of 12) copy. The only apparent flaws are a slightly blunted upper left corner, and staples approximately 1/4" to the right of the spine. Very crisp, with vivid colors. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $650.

Richard Williams - Original Art for Mad #257 - Front and Back Cover (EC, 1985). This lot includes both the front and back cover art to this issue, as Alfred, in his own inimitable style, flashes a nude beach. Each piece measures 21" x 16", and they have been tastefully matted and framed separately. The work is truly superb. A rare two-piece set from an iconic title.

Brenda Starr #13 (#1) Mile High pedigree (Four Star, 1947). Vibrant sun-yellow cover accented with a rich ruby-red logo. Fully flat, tight and fresh with the "trademark" Mile High pedigree white pages. One of the finest copies of this book in existence! CGC VF+ 8.5 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $740.

The Incredible Hulk #4 (Marvel, 1962). Early issue stars the Hulk in two feature-length stories with vintage cover and interior art by Jack "King" Kirby. Stunning copy of this book has very clean and deep color inks, great gloss and perfectly sharp cover edges with no "Marvel chipping". Spine is tight with secure staples and perfect centering. One of the highest-grade copies of Hulk #4 we have ever seen at any time. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,550.

Batman #3 (DC, 1940). Early issue with a classic Bob Kane cover features the first costumed appearance of the Catwoman. Nice clean copy, with some spine stress and some discoloration along the spine and a very small chip out of the back cover. Overall, a beautiful book with great eye-appeal. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $2,350; NM 9.4 value = $9,000.

Neal Adams and Dick Giordano - Original Art for Detective Comics #407, page 11 (DC, 1971). An exceptional page from an exceptional artistic team. Adams and Giordano redefined comics in general, and Batman in particular, with their groundbreaking work in the 1970's. This page is an excellent example of the power and dynamism this legendary team brought to this book. In a stunning close-up panel, Batman unmasks Francine Langstrom, who has been transformed into a Man-Bat! The shocking close-up, and Batman's uncharacteristic look of surprise, make this a superb page for any collection. The page measures approximately 10.5" x 16", and is in excellent condition, with minimal use of white-out and blue-pencil.

The Black Terror #8 Pennsylvania pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1944). A classic Schomburg WWII cover highlights this high-grade beauty. Nearly perfect, this copy shows only some very minor spine stress. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $525.

Mad #11 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). A memorable Basil Wolverton cover leads off the #3 Gaines File pedigree copy of Mad #11, an outstanding example of this issue. Only obvious problems are a very lightly bumped lower left corner, very light wear along the top edge of the cover, and staples rolled very slightly onto the front cover. Exceptional freshness and clarity, the usual hallmarks of a Gaines File pedigree book, are readily evident here. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $650.

X-Men #4 (Marvel, 1964). This classic issue boasts a ton of important elements, including the second appearance of Magneto, as well as the debut of Quicksilver, The Scarlet Witch (who's colored GREEN on the cover!), The Toad, and the Brotherhood of Evil Mutants. To sweeten the deal, this copy has a double cover (outer cover graded 9.0, inner cover 9.4) and is in absolutely beautiful condition! The only apparent flaws are some very minor wear along the top edge of the front cover, a minute amount of spine stress, and staples rolled approximately 1/4" onto the back cover. A really fresh example of this splendid X-book. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $825.

All-Flash #22 Mile High pedigree (DC, 1946). This book could have come right off the press. So nice it looks virtually unread, the spine is stress-free, the colors are deep and strong, and the corners are nice and sharp. There is an arrival date notation in grease pencil at the top of the front cover and a slight mis-cut, but this really doesn't reduce the overall eye appeal of this gorgeous book. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $550.

Captain America Comics #32 Crowley pedigree (Timely, 1943). This exceptional high-grade copy has a wonderful WWII cover by Syd Shores as Captain America dives to save young Bucky from certain death. Also included is a Human Torch back-up story. This copy offers superb colors with an excellent cut, clean cover edges and great eye-appeal. One of the finest copies of this book we have ever seen! CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $537; NM 9.4 value = $1700.

X-Men #94 (Marvel, 1975). The first appearance of the new X-Men in their own title (after their debut in Giant Size X-Men #1) features outstanding artwork by Dave Cockrum, and a very cool cover by Gil Kane and Cockrum. This is an unbelievably gorgeous copy with razor-sharp corners and brilliant color. The only visible flaw is a near-microscopic crease in the top right corner. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $750.

Vault of Horror #39 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1954). Why settle for less when you can have the best? According to CGC's latest census report, there's not a finer copy of this book in existence. A gem of a comic, with a fabulous look. A stunningly subtle Johnny Craig bondage cover highlights this wonderful book. CGC NM/MT 9.8 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $240.

America's Best Comics #10 Mile High pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1944). Classic patriotic cover. Beautiful copy of a very desirable book. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

Brenda Starr (Vol. 2), #12 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1949). Great-looking top-grade book has wonderful deep sea-blue color inks with rich reds and bright yellows. Completely flat and very tight with a sharp spine, no staple stress and perfect centering. Truly, a superior book. The finest copy known! CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $620.

Dark Mysteries #1 Aurora pedigree (Magazine Enterprises, 1951). Debut issue delivering "Thrilling Tales of Horror and Suspense" is high-lighted with cover and story art by the legendary Wally Wood. Very attractive flat, clean and supple book has bright, rich colors and nice fresh interior paper which is quite uncommon for these books. Spine is beautiful with secure staples and no stress marks of note. Bottom edge of front cover has the smallest chip in the black area. Very tough to find in the higher grades; one of the finest we have seen. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $975.

Speed Comics #38 Mile High pedigree (Harvey, 1945). Super-killer copy of this classic WW II patriotic cover that re-enacts the classic US Marine victory scene on the island of Iwo Jima. This comic approaches perfection in every sense of the word and is the finest copy known in existence! CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $400.

Strange Tales #115 Bethlehem pedigree (Marvel, 1963). The senses-shattering origin of Dr. Strange, Master of the Mystic Arts, by the one and only "Sturdy" Steve Ditko! Difficult to find in high-grade, this copy shows a date stamp on the cover in red ink, and slight evidence of wear at the top and bottom spine. None of this mars the incredible eye-appeal of this Silver Age classic. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $500.

Strange Tales Annual #1 (Marvel, 1962). Top-grade copy of this title's first annual that is also believed to be Marvel's first annual for any comic title. Beautiful square-bound comic has alluring teal-green cover background with rich colors and great gloss. Flat, tight and very fresh with perfectly clean cover edges (no chipping, no tears and no creasing) and superb eye-appeal. This is, by far, the very best copy of Strange Tales Annual #1 we have ever seen! Very rare in this grade! CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $575.

Detective Comics #14 (DC, 1938). Rare pre-hero issue is extremely attractive and presentable. Moderate spine wear, but overall a great-looking copy. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $1,700; NM 9.4 value = $3,800.

Batman #9 (DC, 1942). The all-time classic Jack Burnley cover leads off this very solid copy. An early appearance by the Joker and the very first Batman Christmas story highlight this issue. Flaws include moderate spine wear, color chips on the right front cover edge (just right of the price), and what appears to be the letter "p" pencilled in the logo. Tremendous eye-appeal and vivid color. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $971; NM 9.4 value = $3,400.

Batman #56 (DC, 1949). A beautiful high-grade copy that is the highest CGC grade achieved thus far by this issue. Slightly miscut, but a lovely copy nonetheless. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $725.

Mad #9 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). A hilarious Kurtzman cover leads off this classic issue of MAD. This is the #3 Gaines File pedigree copy, and displays bright, luminous colors and an exceptionally fresh appearance. The only visible flaws include a slightly bumped upper left corner and light spine stress. CGC VF/NM 9.0 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $650.

Johnny Craig - Original Cover Art Recreation of Vault of Horror #26 . Johnny Craig, one of the true giants of comics history, rendered this stunning recreation of his classic Vault of Horror cover in oil on canvas. With an image area of approximately 18" x 24", this piece has been beautifully framed and matted and is ready to hang on the wall of your mausoleum, crypt or tomb. From the Grant Geissman collection.

America's Best Comics #14 Mile High pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1945). An unbelievably nice copy from the pedigree that set the standard for quality. This fabulous Golden Age find is near-perfect, its only defect being a slight mis-cut. According to CGC, a finer copy does not exist. CGC NM/MT 9.8 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

X-Men #11 (Marvel, 1965). Key issue introduces the Stranger with artwork by Jack "King" Kirby. Killer book with full colors and cover gloss. Cover edges are perfectly clean (no "Marvel chipping"), with sharp corners and supple interior paper. Very scarce in this top condition. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $300.

Sensation Comics #1 (DC, 1942). A key book from the Golden Age of Comics! This highly desirable issue features the first cover appearance of Wonder Woman (who debuted in All-Star Comics #8), as well as the first appearances of future Justice Society members Wildcat and Mr. Terrific. A real piece of comics history! Low to mid-grade, with moderate spine wear, light to moderate soiling and staining, a 1" tear on the last 16 pages and "Sample Copy" stamped in purple ink on the front cover, this is still a solid copy of an important book. CGC G/VG 3.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $2,160; FN 6.0 value = $6,480.

Captain America Comics #75 (Timely, 1950). Very scarce last Golden Age issue titled "Captain America's Weird Tales" is seldom found in any grade whatsoever. This high-grade book has excellent colors and centering. Flat and clean with no chipping, no tears and no creasing. Back edge of spine has some light stress points. This is the best copy we have ever seen! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF 8.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $505; NM 9.4 value = $1,600.

Startling Comics #41 Mile High pedigree (Better Publications, 1946). This issue is highlighted by a classic Atom Bomb cover by Alex Schomburg. An exceptional copy, this book's only visible flaw is some light dust shadowing along the top cover. Otherwise, flat, clean and sharp, with incredible eye-appeal. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $360.

Batman #41 (DC, 1947). Little green men train their ray-guns on the Dynamic Duo, in the very first sci-fi Batman cover and story; this rousing issue also features an early appearance by the Penguin. This is an outstanding copy with an almost flawless appearance; sharp corners, a near-perfect spine, and dazzling colors give this tremendous eye-appeal. A shiny gem sure to be a worthy addition to any high-grade Batman collection. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $750.

World's Finest Comics #8 (DC, 1942). Superman, Batman and Robin work to help sink "Japanazis" with War Bonds and Stamps. Also, the Boy Commandos begin their series with this early issue that features artwork by Joe Simon and Jack Burnley. A superb copy of this square-bound issue has very bright and clean colors with perfectly clean cover edges and stunning paper quality. Completely flat with just the slightest hint of wear at the spine edge. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,650.

Mad #15 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). A rich turquoise background and a John Tenniel illustration from "Alice in Wonderland" make up the cover of this early issue of America's first satire comic. Sharp, flat, and clean, with outstanding page quality, CGC has not graded a nicer copy of this issue. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $525.

Crime SuspenStories #2 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1950). You'll find "Startling Tales of Tension" in this pristine example of a classic EC crime comic. A slight impact mark at the upper spine and some very minor dust shadows on the back cover are the only marks of age on this tight, flat copy. Gorgeous and eye appealing, this is a worthy addition to any collection. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $560.

Crime SuspenStories #20 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1953). Classic Johnny Craig hanging cover used in Seduction of the Innocent. From the collection of William Gaines, the front cover shows slight creasing at the bottom right corner and minor damage at the top right corner, but an attractive book overall. Vastly underpriced and extremely desirable. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $260.

Crime SuspenStories #22 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1954). Probably the most infamous of all of EC's gory images, this classic Johnny Craig ax decapitation cover was used in the Senate Investigations on Juvenile Delinquency and lead to that event's foremost sound bite. When asked by Senator Estes Kefauver if he thought this cover was in good taste, publisher Bill Gaines responded, "Yes, sir: I do. For the cover of a horror comic." Good taste or not, this copy is in excellent condition, with slight crimping at the upper spine and moderate spine stress. Great cover colors and an excellent cut combine to make this an extremely attractive and desirable copy. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

Tomb of Terror #15 (Harvey, 1954). From the Harvey File copies. Famous exploding face cover that is highly sought-after by collectors with artwork by Lee Elias and Bob Powell. Top-grade copy has incredibly deep colors and gloss with no chips, no tears and no creasing. Fully flat with near perfect printing cut and tremendous eye appeal. Spine is sharp and tight with only a minor bit of stress at lower corner. The highest-grade copy we have ever seen! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $385.

Tales of Suspense #39 (Marvel, 1963). Key Silver Age issue features the origin and first appearance of the Invincible Iron Man, one of Marvel Comics' most enduring and successful characters. Story art by Don Heck and classic cover by Jack "King" Kirby. Very attractive tight and clean copy, has alluring teal-green cover with sharp grays and rich ruby reds. Perfectly flat comic has exact centering and very clean cover edges with no chipping, no creasing and no tears. Slight bit of stress at top corner of spine, minor stress at top staple and a dust shadow at right edge of front cover and bottom edge of back cover. A nicer copy than the grade would indicate. CGC VF 8.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 VF 8.0 value = $2,480; NM 9.4 value = $5,200.

Journey into Mystery #83 (Marvel, 1962). The origin and first appearance of The Mighty Thor, "The Most Exciting Superhero of All Time!!" A lovely copy of this Silver Age Marvel key, it shows a bit of dust shadowing on the right front cover edge, very minor "Marvel chipping" at the bottom front cover, and a small dent at the spine on the back cover. Very attractive, with great eye-appeal. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $975; VF 8.0 value = $2,600.

Tales of Suspense #59 (Marvel, 1964). Captain America's first solo story of the Silver Age, as he joins Iron Man in this classic title. A solid high-grade copy, this book is a real beauty. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $300.

Showcase #4 (DC, 1956). Origin and first appearance of the Silver Age Flash. This is the book that brought superheroes back from oblivion and started the Silver Age! No Silver Age key collection is complete without this one! This copy shows a moderate amount of spine wear, some chipping and tearing along the right edge, and a date stamp in the upper right corner of the front cover. A solid mid-grade copy overall. CGC VG/FN 5.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $1,150; FN 6.0 value = $3,450.

Mad #12 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). Another batch of merriment calculated to drive you Mad, this is the #3 Gaines File pedigree copy of this issue, and you'd be hard-pressed to find a better copy on the market. Square corners and outstanding crispness make this a sterling example of what GF books are all about. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $525.

Richard Williams - Original Cover Art for Mad #248 (EC, 1984). An obvious "homage" to Norman Rockwell,Mad style. An exquisitely rendered image, beautifully matted and framed, this piece measures 21" x 17" and is in excellent condition. Acrylilc on board, this piece is signed by Richard Williams (but in Norman Rockwell's style) in the lower right corner. Perfect for vets and animal lovers everywhere.

Mad Straight Jacket (EC, 1959). First offered in Mad #46 (and never offered again after #53), this is considered by experts to be one of the rarest and most desirable of all Mad collectibles. Bill Gaines has said that about 1,500 were sold (at the exorbitant price of $4.95!!), but far, far fewer than that are known to exist today. This unique piece of apparel includes a Maddening image of Alfred E. Neuman on the back, along with the logo "What - Me Worry? I'm wearing my Mad Straight Jacket". There is also a working padlock included on the jacket. This particular piece is a size Small (Medium and Large were also offered) and is in excellent condition for its age. There is some minor staining on the jacket and some very slight tearing at the lock eyelets, but overall this is a beautiful piece. The colors are crisp and bright, and the silk-screening shows virtually no signs of wear. An excellent piece for the Mad collector who has almost everything!

Crypt of Terror #19 Bethlehem pedigree (EC, 1950). Last issue before title change has a spooky female Voodoo-Zombie cover by Johnny Craig with interior work by Al Feldstein and Graham "Ghastly" Ingels. Very high-grade flat, clean and tight book with full color depth and nice spine. Tough to find in this high grade! CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $2,800.

Captain Marvel Adventures #29 Mile High pedigree (Fawcett, 1943). The "Big Red Cheese" stars in "Battle at the China Wall". This near flawless copy has very fresh interior paper, deep color strike and razor sharp corners. There is some minor stress at the very top of the spine. A truly beautiful book! CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $480.

Star Spangled Comics #17 Mile High pedigree (DC, 1943). A star-studded classic from the Golden Age wrapped in an exciting Simon & Kirby cover! This beautiful high-grade copy has a double cover (the first cover is CGC graded 7.5, the interior cover is CGC graded 8.5). The book shows only the most minor flaws, including a pencil notation of "D 11-20" in the upper left corner of the front cover and a pencilled "6" in the upper center, a very little bit of spine stress, a 2" crease in the upper left front cover and some slight creasing on the back cover. Stunning eye-appeal makes this an extremely desirable book. CGC VF+ 8.5 White pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $379; NM 9.4 value = $1,200.

The Human Torch #38 (Timely, 1954). Tough-to-find last issue for this title has cover art by Sol Brodsky and a Sub-Mariner story by the legendary Bill Everett. Exceptional copy has rich ruby-reds and bright sun-yellows with no chipping, a beautiful sharp spine and secure staples. Completely flat and very supple with terrific overall eye appeal. A real beauty! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $800.

Mad #17 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). The famous upside-down issue of Mad is well-represented here in this near-perfect example. This is the #3 Gaines File pedigree copy, and a very light crease at the top of the spine is the only apparent flaw. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

Vault of Horror #19 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1951). Johnny Craig's cover is an homage to true love. Flat, sharp and clean, this is a nearly-perfect copy of a very collectible book. According to CGC, this is equal to the finest copy they've seen. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

Graham Ingels - Original Art of the Old Witch. Graham Ingels, better known as "Ghastly" to his fans, was the one artist who, more than any other, defined the look of the classic EC horror comics of the 1950's. Later in his life, Ingels created stunning oil paintings on familiar themes, several featuring The Old Witch, the EC horror-host with which he was most associated. This piece is an oil on canvas, with an image area measuring approximately 9.75" x 14.5" and is in excellent condition, having been professionally matted and framed. Below and to the left of the art, in pencil, has been written, "Comp - Oil - Canvas". To the right has been written "Approx. - 20x30". The notations are most likely in Ingels' own hand. Once known for the graphic quality of his illustrations, this piece is wonderfully subtle, creating tension and mood in a new way for Ingels. This masterful piece was used as the back cover illustration of Tales of Terror, by Fred von Bernewitz and Grant Geissman. It is signed by Ingels in the bottom right. From the Grant Geissman collection.

Mad #14 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). A suitably classical cover (who knew Mona readsMad?) for a classic humor comic. This is the #3 Gaines File pedigree copy, and it's a beauty, with a crisp, fresh appearance and outstanding color. Only apparent flaws are a very small chip at the base of the spine, some very light spine stress, slight color rub at the top staple, and light diagonal creases at the top left edge. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $525.

Mad #16 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). Kurtzman's hilarious "newspaper" cover leads off this near-perfect copy of Mad. This is #3 from the Gaines File; razor-sharp corners and amazing clarity make this look as fresh as if it were printed yesterday. Only apparent flaws are the bottom staple rolled 1/4" on to the back cover, and both covers are slightly off-register. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

Vault of Horror #21 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1951). Super high grade Gaines File pedigree Copy of this classic comic. A beautiful book...the grade says it all. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $365.

Thrilling Comics #22 Mile High pedigree (Better Publications, 1941). The Mile High pedigree collection, well-known for high-quality, is superbly represented in this classic wartime book. CGC notes very minor color touch on cover, but this book has been graded as unrestored. There is a pencilled arrival date on the cover, but this is otherwise a clean, flat, and beautiful book. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $425.

The Amazing Spider-Man #9 (Marvel, 1964). The introduction of Electro, "a menace so powerful that Spider-Man's strength is useless against him!" A flat, clean, absolutely spectacular copy of this book with only very minor wear at the top and bottom spine. Deep, rich colors highlight the classic Ditko cover. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,150.

X-Men #1 (Marvel, 1963). Marvel's Mightiest Mutants debut in this landmark issue. A solid mid-grade copy, this book shows light tanning, slight spine stress, and very minor creasing at the bottom right corner. Otherwise tight and very attractive. CGC FN 6.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $1,675.

All-Flash #21 Mile High pedigree (DC, 1945). An exceptionally beautiful copy of this Golden Age gem. Clean and flat with rich colors and sharp corners, this would be a welcome addition to any high-grade collection. The fact that the book is slightly miscut is the only discernible flaw in an otherwise stunning copy. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $550.

Aquaman #1 (DC, 1962). The King of the Seven Seas stars in his own title in "The Invasion of the Fire-Trolls" with cover art by Nick Cardy. This outstanding copy has an alluring blue cover with very clean edges and a sharp spine. Still, one of the most under-valued of the DC Silver Age key books. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $850.

George Perez - Original Cover Art for Wonder Woman (2nd Series) #9 (DC, 1987). The villainous Cheetah shows her true feelings for the Amazing Amazon in this gorgeously rendered cover by classic Wonder Woman artist George Perez. From early in Perez's long run on this title, this piece is in excellent condition, with only slight, subtle use of corrective white-out. The piece, which has been nicely matted, has an image area of approximately 9.75" x 15", and is signed, "To Chris, Best, George Perez". An outstanding example from a gifted artist.

Mad #19 Gaines File pedigree 3/9 (EC, 1955). Beautiful copy with an important pedigree. Sharp and clean, this book shows slight tanning along the edges, but very little else to detract from its outstanding look. Oddly, there were only nine copies of this issue in the Gaines File pedigree collection, as opposed to all other issues of Mad, of which there were 12, making this the scarcest of the Gaines File pedigree Mad's. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

Vault of Horror #30 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1953). A gory severed arm cover highlights this high grade beauty. Part of the Gaines File pedigree set, this is a great looking copy that will enhance any collection. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $285.

The Haunt of Fear #10 Gaines File pedigree 2/12 (EC, 1951). Includes a stunning bullfight cover by Al Feldstein and a biography of "Ghastly" Graham Ingels. An exceptional copy of this classic issue from the files of William Gaines. A very small chip at the bottom spine and a slight bend at the upper spine do not detract from the exceptional eye-appeal of this beautiful book. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $415.

Crime SuspenStories #3 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1951). Classic EC crime/suspense tales (including an amazing story by Harvey Kurtzman), and you can't get much better than a Gaines File pedigree copy. Absolutely no apparent flaws, with razor-sharp corners, vivid color, and amazing crispness. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $400.

Hit Comics #1 Cosmic Aeroplane pedigree (Quality, 1940). The Red Bee opens fire on this cover, illustrated by the talented Lou Fine. Debut issue also includes such back-up series as Hercules, Blaze Barton and Neon the Unknown. Attractive mid-grade copy has superbly deep color strike and exact centering. Flat and supple with some stress along spine edges and at left edge of back cover. No chips, no large tears and no large creases. CGC FN+ 6.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $1,775; NM 9.4 value = $6,800.

Top-Notch Comics #5 Allentown (MLJ, 1940). Beautiful copy of this scarce issue stars the Wizard, the Man with the Super-Brain, which also includes a Shield cameo and the origin and first appearance of Galahad. Classic Wizard WW II cover by E.M. Ashe Jr. and story art by Irv Novick. Exceptional comic has exact printing cut, full bright color strike and sharp spine with just a minor amount of wear at top corner. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $1,400.

Tales From the Crypt #23 (EC, 1951). Beautiful high-grade copy with a classic Feldstein cover. A great look on a like-new book. CGC NM 9.4 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $560.

The Amazing Spider-Man #6 (Marvel, 1963). The "Marvel Age of Comics" is here as Spidey must do battle with one of his most powerful arch-foes, The Lizard. Origin and first appearance of The Lizard with vintage cover/interior art by Steve Ditko. Very flat and tight book with secure staples and sharp color inks (no smudging in the grays). Perfectly clean cover edges (no "Marvel chipping"), sharp corners and excellent centering. Spine is very sharp with only a few, very light stress marks. Comic has no tears, no chips and no creasing. This is perhaps the most difficult early issue to find in the top grades. Seldom seen this nice. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,600.

The Amazing Spider-Man #32 Slabodian pedigree (Marvel, 1966). Spidey stars in "Man on a Rampage!" Cover and story art by Steve Ditko. Super-killer copy of this classic issue has bright, sharp colors, no "Marvel chipping", and an immaculate spine. Rare in this top-grade and a must see comic! CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

Batman #20 (DC, 1943). The Batmobile debuts in this classic issue, represented here with a truly gorgeous copy. Bright, vivid colors and outstanding crispness. Flaws include dust shadowing on back cover and light stress around staples. The letter "l" is pencilled just above the logo, and CGC notes that a small amount of writing was erased from the back cover. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,900.

Jack Kirby - Original Art for "Hole in the Wall" from Alarming Tales #2 - Complete 6-Page Story (Harvey Publications, 1957). Widely recognized as the "King" of comics, Jack Kirby could do it all - from superheroes to westerns to romance. Here he proves his mastery of the "weird tale" genre with a little-known gem from a seldom-seen title. In this six-page story, a man loses his job, but finds so much more through a "Hole in the Wall!" All the trademark Kirby elements are here: the dynamic layouts, the masterful pacing, and the sheer audacity of the "King's" imagination. This is large art, measuring approximately 14.5" x 23" and is in excellent condition. Little to no white-out is used, and blue-pencil markings, so common on art from this period, are entirely absent. This is an excellent example of Kirby's "middle period" work, between the creative years of the Second World War, and his virtual reinvention of the superhero genre in the 1960's and 70's. Only the first page is shown here. To see the complete story, go to www.HeritageComics.com.

Journey into Mystery #1 Aurora pedigree (Atlas, 1952). Very good-looking copy of this scarce first issue has Russ Heath cover art. Flat, clean and very glossy book has tremendous color inks and excellent overall eye appeal. No chipping with some wear along spine including a 1/2" split at bottom and a 1" split at top. One of the best copies of this very tough-to-find issue we have seen. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC FN+ 6.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $900; NM 9.4 value = $3,100.

Mad #21 Gaines File pedigree 3/11 (EC, 1955). This issue features the very first cover appearance of Alfred E. Neuman in a fake ad. This copy shows slight tanning along the edges, but is otherwise very attractive and desirable. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $420.

Mad #1 (EC, 1952). First issue featuring "Humor in a Jugular Vein" and "Tales Calculated to Drive You Mad". Considered to be the first satire comic, this issue has artwork by such talents as Wally Wood, Bill Elder and Jack Davis and would eventually become the most successful satire magazine in comic history. Great colors and nice overall eye- appeal with a minor cover mis-cut, clean cover edges and supple paper. Minor wear at top right corner of book and top of spine. Flat, clean and tight. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $1,560; NM 9.4 value = $6,500.

Mad #1 - #23 Complete Low-Grade Run (EC, 1952-1955). Complete run of Mad #1-23, all issues are in low-grade, except #22. Most are a solid VG, with the following exceptions: #1 is a GD, heavy overall wear; #15 is FR, ripped cover has heavy tape repair; #18 & #19 are GD-, appear VG, but have drill-holes along the spine; #21 is FR, cover detached, chipped, and brittle, with heavy tape repair; #22 is FN+, very nice copy with staples pulling very slightly, and minor overall wear; #23 is GD-, cover torn app. 4" at the spine. Overstreet 2001 value for lot = $1,787. (Total: 23 Comic Books)

Crime SuspenStories #8 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1951). Issue features artwork by Jack Kamen, Graham Ingels and Johnny Craig with a chilling cover by Craig as well. Incredible copy of this issue is nearly perfect in every way possible. Full color strike, perfectly clean edges and razor sharp corners. Spine is immaculate, centering exact with very fresh interior paper. One of the very finest copies in the world. Absolutely stunning! CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $320.

Fighting Yank #12 Big Apple pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1945). America's Bravest Defender has the Imperial Japanese Army subdued on this Hirohito bondage cover by Alex Schomburg. Top-grade book has vibrant color strike and a near flawless spine with full white paper. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $500.

Brenda Starr(Vol. 2), #6 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1949). Here's a high-grade fresh, clean and flat book from the most famous of all pedigree comic collections. Minor stress at top left corner of back cover but no chips or tears. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $610.

Mystery Men Comics #1 (Fox, 1939). Scarce first issue introduces the Blue Beetle and the Green Mask with the first Blue Beetle cover by Lou Fine. Attractive copy has the overall look of a VG with superb color strike, near exact centering and supple off-white to light cream pages. Cover is detached from interior pages with spine having a split from the bottom to the lower staple. Chip out at top corner of spine. The perfect copy for the collector who is searching for a good-looking copy of this key issue without having to pay $3,000 or more. Condition: VG Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $957.

Sub-Mariner Comics #18 (Timely, 1945). Beautiful copy of this issue stars the Prince of Atlantis with an Angel back-up story and cover art by Alex Schomburg. Completely flat and tight book has sharp colors and perfectly clean cover edges with no tears, no chips and no creasing. Centering is exact. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,200.

Frank Robbins - Original Art for "Survival of the Fittest" from Weird War Tales #27 - Complete 9-Page Story (DC, 1974). An excellent example of Robbins' work from the increasingly-collectible Bronze Age. Herein is told the story of a Nazi U-Boat commander who discovers that what goes around comes around. Robbins' off-beat, quirky style is perfect for this tale of terror, with dynamic layouts and an almost Tothish use of blacks. The pages measure approximately 10.5" x 15.5". There is very minor use of white-out throughout, and several pages display blue-pencilled editors markings, but this is an excellent opportunity to own a fabulous example of an artist at the top of his game. A Bronze Age gem not to be missed. Only the first page is shown here. To see the complete story, please visit www.HeritageComics.com.

Wonder Comics #20 Diamond Run pedigree (Better Comics, 1948). Stunning copy of this last issue has a great "Good-Girl" airbrush cover by Alex "Xela" Schomburg and interior story art by Frank Frazetta on The Silver Knight. This near flawless book is superb in every way with full colors, sharp spine and perfectly clean cover edges. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $655.

Top-Notch Comics #7 (MLJ, 1940). Early issue as the Wizard dons a new costume and a Shield x-over into the Wizard story. Very attractive high-grade book has intensely rich colors and perfectly clean cover edges with no chips, no creases and no tears. Flat, clean and very tight with a near flawless spine and no staple stress at all. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,200.

U.S.A. Comics #16 (Timely, 1945). Classic Timely book featuring Captain America, The Whizzer, and The Destroyer. A gorgeous copy sporting a wonderful Alex Schomburg cover with Cap and Bucky in action. Top left corner shows a tiny nick, and top right corner is slightly blunted, but the colors are vibrant, and the book is very fresh overall. CGC notes a rusty staple. CGC VF/NM 9.0 (No page color notation). Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,000.

Mystic #6 (Timely, 1941). A Timely Classic! Beneath a striking Kirby/Schomburg cover you'll find such Golden Age greats as The Destroyer, The Thin Man, and The Black Marvel! This book displays moderate spine wear, a pencilled "M" in the upper left front corner, and some slight soiling on the back cover. Otherwise, a very attractive copy. CGC VF- 7.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $1,000; NM 9.4 value = $3,500.

Exciting Comics #51 Big Apple pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1946). The Black Terror has crime "In Hand" and on the run on this cover by the legendary Alex Schomburg. This issue also features the first appearance of Miss Masque. Killer, near flawless copy has supple off-white cover and interior paper. Exceptional comic!From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $575.

The Amazing Spider-Man #10 (Marvel, 1964). Early issue in the legendary Lee/Ditko run, features the first appearance of Big Man and The Enforcers. Absolutely gorgeous copy with no "Marvel chipping", nice square corners, and vibrant color. About the only thing evident is that the staples are slightly offset, about 1/8" from the spine. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,050.

The Incredible Hulk #5 (Marvel, 1963). This classic early issue features a killer cover by Jack "King" Kirby and the introduction of Tyrannus, the Subterranean Monarch! Clean, flat and sharp, with virtually no "Marvel chipping", this is an exceptional copy of this hard-to-find book. Slight spine wear and some minor dust shadowing at the edge, along with a date stamp on the back cover, are the only noticeable flaws. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $332; NM 9.4 = $1,550.

X-Men #4 (Marvel,1964). A key issue of this landmark series with art by Jack "King" Kirby. This is the issue that introduced the Scarlet Witch, Quicksilver, and the Brotherhood of Evil Mutants to a breathlessly waiting world! Sharp, clean, and flat, this is a superior copy with only the slightest hint of spine stress. A worthy addition to any collection. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $825.

Batman #28 (DC, 1945). A powerful cover and a classic Joker appearance make this a very desirable book for Bat-collectors. There is a slight amount of chipping along the edges, otherwise this book is nearly perfect. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1400.

Sensation Comics #18 (DC, 1943). Starring Wonder Woman with art by H.G. Peter and Sheldon Moldoff. A beauty with superb colors and fully fresh white pages. Flat, clean and tight with a minor bit of wear at top of spine. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $900.

Joe Orlando and Russ Jones - Original Art for "The Mummy's Hand" - Complete 7-Page story (1964). Originally published in Monster World #2, and later reprinted in Creepy #17, this is Orlando's excellent adaptation of the Universal horror film from 1940 with Western star Tom Tyler under the bandages. Orlando, the well-known EC veteran, displays an excellent sense of mood and pacing as he brings this classic creeper to life in seven pages, ably assisted by inker Russ Jones. The title page also includes some Orlando sketches along the bottom. Measuring approximately 14.25" x 22.5", the pages are in excellent condition overall, with the first page showing several small tape/glue stains, but none that unduly affect the art. The top title block on the first page is a stat, but all the rest of the art is purely original. Only the first page is shown here. To see the complete story, please visit www.HeritageComics.com.

Marvel Mystery Comics #74 (Timely, 1946). Superior copy of this later issue has a great Human Torch and Toro cover as they come to the rescue at the Timely Club. Superbly rich blood-red color inks with perfect centering and clean cover edges. Back edge of spine has light tanning and corners have slight blunting. Scarce this nice! CGC VF/NM 9.0. Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $950.

Mad #22 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1955). With a faux-Picasso cover, this is a great example of a nearly perfect book from an historically important collection. A great addition to any collection. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

Vault of Horror #31 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1953). A beautiful, high grade copy from the classic Gaines File pedigree collection, contains an adaptation of a story by, and a biography of, Ray Bradbury. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $285.

The Haunt of Fear #11 Gaines File pedigree 2/12 (EC, 1952). The gruesome cover by "Ghastly" Graham Ingels made this book a best "cellar"! A near-perfect copy, with great color, sharp corners and exceptional page quality. A real gem. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $310.

Brenda Starr (Vol 2), #7 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1949). Good-looking book has rich color strike, fully fresh white pages and good eye appeal. Bottom corner of spine has an impact dent. No other defects of note. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $610.

Artist Unknown - Original Art for "You Can't Cheat A Ghost" from Strange Mysteries #9 - Complete 8-Page Story (Farrell, 1953). Pre-code horror at its very finest. From the gruesome splash to the terrifying finale, this is the kind of story that thrilled and captivated readers at the close of the Golden Age. The style is clean and bold, with an almost cinematic sense of pacing. Measuring approximately 13.5" x 21" on relatively light paper, the art is in very good condition, with some use of white-out throughout, but not enough to detract from the overall excellent eye-appeal of this rare gem. Only the first page is shown here. To see the complete story, please visit www.HeritageComics.com.

The Amazing Spider-Man #17 (Marvel, 1964). Issue features the return of the Green Goblin with a Human Torch cross-over and cover/interior art by Steve Ditko.
