Very attractive copy is fully flat, tight and glossy with just the slightest bit of blunting on cover corners. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $750.

X-Men #8 Massachusetts pedigree (Marvel, 1964). The X-Men face the uncanny threat of "Unus the Untouchable" (first appearance), with cover and story artwork by Jack "King" Kirby. Top-grade, supple copy is perfectly flat, clean and fully glossy with superb eye-appeal. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $375.

All-Star Comics #13 (DC, 1942). The Justice Society is "Shanghaied into Space" in this classic issue of a key Golden Age title. This copy displays some spine wear and several very small tears along the upper back cover, as well as slight damage to the top and bottom spine, but is overall a very attractive and appealing book in high-grade. CGC VF 8.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $726; NM 9.4 value = $2,300.

Tales to Astonish #27 (Marvel, 1962). Classic key issue features the cover story "The Man in the Ant Hill!", the astonishing first appearance of Ant-Man (out of costume) with vintage art by Jack "King" Kirby and Steve Ditko. One of the toughest to find of the high-grade Marvel keys, this book is sought by both superhero and pre-hero collectors. Very attractive copy has deep chocolate browns and sharp gray color inks, good gloss and no "Marvel chipping". Minor stress at top right corner of front cover and a hint of wear beginning to show at spine edge. Flat, clean and tight with good overall eye-appeal. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $788; NM 9.4 value = $4,200.

Detective Comics #10 (DC, 1937). Rare pre-Batman issue of this classic title has cover art by Golden-Age legend Craig Flessel and interior work by Jerry Siegel, Joe Shuster and Flessel. Solid-looking copy has great colors and good eye- appeal with moderate professional restoration including: color touch, pieces added, tear seals, spine reinforced and cover cleaned/ pressed. This issue is seldom seen in any grade. CGC Apparent VF/NM 9.0 Moderate (P) Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $2,250; NM 9.4 value = $5,000.

More Fun Comics #100 (DC, 1944). This title is very scarce in any grade, so a high-grade copy like this is a real treat. Clean, flat and sharp, with great color and an excellent strike, there is a small chip out of the bottom spine and a very minor bend at the top left back cover. Overall extraordinary eye-appeal. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,100.

Mad #13 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). Harvey Kurtzman's humorous "smallest title in the world" cover is even more striking in such high grade, with a velvety field of dark red lending incredible eye appeal to this outstanding book. This is the #3 Gaines File pedigree copy, and has all the crispness and clarity you would normally associate with that elite grouping. Slightly lower CGC grade is likely due to a series of very small flaws, including a small chip in the lower left edge of the back cover, and some very, very light spine stress, also on the back. There is a very light color scuff at the bottom left corner of the front cover and a very light color break at the top left corner. Overall, this is a tremendously appealing example of this classic comic. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $525.

Mad #20 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1955). A comic designed to be snuck into class! From the Gaines File pedigree collection, this copy has few visible flaws, save for a small crease in the upper left back cover. One word: Beautiful. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

Mad #23 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1955). The very last comic issue of this landmark title. Starting with the next issue,Mad switched to magazine format to escape the restrictions of the newly-formed Comics Code Authority. This copy, from the legendary Gaines File pedigree collection, is in excellent condition with few visible flaws. A key issue of this classic title. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

Tales From the Crypt #27 (EC, 1951). A fabulous Wally Wood guillotine cover highlights this high-grade treasure. A near-perfect copy, there is some slight dust shadowing on the back cover, but an impressive comic overall. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $440.

Tales From the Crypt #43 Gaines File pedigree 2/12 (EC, 1954). An outstanding Jack Davis cover leads off this superb entry into the annals of EC horror. Davis, Jack Kamen, and Graham Ingels all lend their talents to tales of terror. Not to mention the fact that, not only is this copy from the legendary Gaines Files , but it's the #2 (of 12) copy to boot! A beautiful example with square corners and excellent clarity. Only visible problems are a bit of spine stress and very light wear along the top edge of the front cover. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $105; NM 9.4 value = $385.

Crime SuspenStories #11 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1952). An eerie cover from EC mainstay Johnny Craig leads off this classic crime book from Entertaining Comics. Another beautiful Gaines File pedigree copy the only apparent flaws are a tiny crease in the top right corner, and a slight mis-fold (cover and staples are slightly off-register). CGC NM+ 9.6 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.6 value = $240.

Neal Adams and Dick Giordano - Original Art for Detective Comics #407, page 6 (DC, 1971). Neal Adams redefined the Darknight Detective in the early 1970's with his landmark run on Detective. This is a page from one of those issues with Batman in three out of six panels. There is a rather extensive use of white-out in the second panel, but the page is very nice overall, and a superb example of Adams' mastery of the superhero genre. Approximately 10.5" x 16".

Startling Comics #46 Okajima pedigree (Better Publications, 1947). A classic Graham Ingels bondage cover highlights this high-grade beauty. Slight discoloration of the back cover spine, some light pencil notations on the front cover, and two date stamps on the back cover do not detract from the overwhelming appeal of this Golden Age gem. CGC VF+ 8.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $285; NM 9.4 value = $900.

Batman #2 (DC, 1940). This outstanding issue features the second appearances of the Joker and Catwoman, led off by a vivid Bob Kane cover. This is a good solid low-grade copy, with a very crisp cover that is, unfortunately, missing a chunk on the right edge about midway down. The back cover is also missing a chunk at the top left. CGC notes slightly brittle pages. Solid overall, this is an outstanding book for the collector who can't afford a top-drawer copy. CGC VG 4.0 Slightly brittle pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $1,120; FN 6.0 value = $3,360.

Boy Commandos #6 (DC, 1944). A great Simon & Kirby cover highlights this WWII classic. On the front cover, there is a pencil mark of "D2-9" in the upper left corner and a pencilled "2" in the upper right, as well as damage to the upper right corner and slight rounding to the lower right corner. CGC notes "'Jack Kirby' written on 1st page in marker", because the book is autographed. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $221; NM 9.4 value = $700.

Superman Golden Age Group (DC, 1946-51). Large lot of Golden Age Superman, most in VG. Includes: #42 GD/VG; #49, 51 VG; #52,54 GD/VG; #55, #56, 57 VG, classic Super-Lois Lane cover; #58,61 VG, store stamp across cover; #62 VG, guest-starring Orson Welles; #63 GD/VG, classic "good girl" cover; #64 and #68 GD/VG. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $978; FN 6.0 value for group = $2,928. (Total: 14 Comic Books)

Startling Comics #50 (Better Publications, 1948). High-grade copy has an eye-catching Good-Girl airbrush cover by Alex "Xela" Schomburg. Tight, clean and flat book has bright colors, clean cover edges, and sharp corners. Near exact centering and fresh interior paper. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $550.

Amazing Adventures #1 (Marvel, 1961). Debut issue has the origin of Dr. Droom (1st Marvel-Age Superhero) with cover by Jack "King" Kirby and interior work by Kirby, Dick Ayers and Steve Ditko. Nice looking book has bright yellow color inks with good gloss, clean cover edges and no "Marvel chipping". CGC notes: "Name/address stamp on 1st page." Some spine stress lines and wear at bottom of spine.From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $321; NM 9.4 value = $1,500.

Mystic #1 (Atlas, 1951). Pre-Code mystery title features "The Most Eerie Stories Ever Told". A wonderful copy of this scarce first issue has great color inks and gloss with clean edges and no chips, tears or creasing. This comic is very flat and sharp with a tight spine, perfect centering and excellent overall eye appeal. The top right corner has an impact dent. CGC notes, "moderate rust on staples." There are no other defects of note. This issue is very scarce in high grade. CGC VF+ 8.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $825.

Tales From the Crypt #42 Gaines File pedigree 2/12 (EC, 1954). A gorgeous copy with a classic Jack Davis vampire cover. Stunning color, flat and clean (as one would expect from a Gaines File pedigree copy), the only noted flaw is a slight mis-cut. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $385.

Crime Does Not Pay #33 (Lev Gleason, 1942). Very rare double cover copy of this highly sought-after issue has the classic grim Hatchet Man cover by the late, great Charles Biro. Great-looking with outer cover VF- 7.5 and interior cover VF/NM 9.0. Never seen a finer copy anywhere! CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $350.

The Amazing Spider-Man #15 (Marvel, 1964). Beautiful copy of this early key issue as Spidey battles Kraven the Hunter (1st appearance) with a special guest appearance by the enigmatic Chameleon. Issue has classic cover and story artwork by Steve Ditko. This book has rich deep purple inks with full gloss and terrific eye-appeal. Perfectly flat, clean and very tight with no "Marvel chipping" at all, sharp corners and nice paper. Tough to find in the higher grades due to its darker cover that tends to accent every tiny defect. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $750.

The Amazing Spider-Man #9 (Marvel, 1964). The most electrifying villain of all, Electro, makes his shocking debut in this Silver Age classic. Only the slightest indications of wear at the spine show that this comic was ever read. Deep blues, brilliant reds, and bright yellows highlight this classic Ditko cover. A book with terrific eye-appeal. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,150.

The Silver Surfer #4 (Marvel, 1968). The Sky-Rider of the Spaceways engages in a titanic clash with the Mighty Thor in this classic issue with fantastic cover and story art by John Buscema. Completely flat and tight comic has full colors and cover gloss, and near perfect centering. One of the most popular of all later Silver Age comics. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $480.

X-Men #35 (Marvel, 1967). Killer copy of this issue features a Spider-Man appearance. Perfectly flat and very tight copy has extremely deep color inks and cover gloss. Near perfect book. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $140.

The Human Torch #8 (Marvel, 1942). Classic key battle issue as the Human Torch "dukes it out" with the Sub-Mariner. Fantastic cover art by Alex Schomburg. Good-looking comic has sharp, bright colors and very fresh white to off-white pages. Cover has been cleaned and pressed and there is very slight color touch on spine edge. Condition: Apparent VF. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $915; NM 9.4 value = $3,200.

Mad Tie Pin (EC, 1957). Mad Magazine began offering five styles of jewelry manufactured by Astrahan of New York in June 1957, priced at $2 each or $3 for a pair of cuff links. Mad discontinued selling them by early 1958. Only 1,000 to 2,000 pieces were sold, according to the publisher, and all are excessively rare today with well under 100 pieces surviving. The tie pins were described by Mad as follows: "Tie always flapping in the breeze? The sturdy alligator clasp of this 'What... Me Worry?' Kid pin will hold back that tie for good. No telling how long people will hold back laughter, though." Excellent condition.From the Grant Geissman collection.

The Black Terror #4 (Nedor Publications, 1943). A great wartime cover by Alex Schomburg with the Black Terror socking a Nazi U-boat commander! This copy shows a small pencil scribble in the "O" in the title, and has slightly rounded corners, but otherwise is very attractive and desirable, with stunning page quality. CGC VF/NM 9.0 White pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $600.

Journey into Mystery #2 Aurora pedigree (Atlas, 1952). Very attractive copy has stellar color inks and gloss with clean edges (only 1 small chip at the bottom edge of the front cover) and very secure staples (no stress). Spine is flat and tight with only a few soft stress lines. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $300; NM 9.4 value = $950.

Crime SuspenStories #10 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1952). Books from the Gaines File pedigree tend to be the Holy Grail for most EC collectors, and here's an amazing example of this classic EC crime comic. This is truly gorgeous! One tiny (1/8") nick at the base of the spine is the only evident flaw. Cover is still crisp and vivid. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $320.

Captain Marvel, Jr. #1 (Fawcett, 1942). Debut issue stars the "World's Mightiest Boy" with superb cover and interior art by Golden-Age great Mac Raboy. Issue re-tells Captain Marvel Jr.'s origin and has a Captain Nazi appearance. Good looking copy of this comic with a beautiful black cover, excellent color strike and perfect centering. Book is flat and tight with some minor wear at cover edges, minor staple stress, and some eraser marks in the "C" of the logo. This is a great Golden-Age key issue that is still quite undervalued in the current market. CGC VG/FN 5.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $1,435.

Red Dragon #1 (Street & Smith, 1947). First issue has an eye-catching burgundy cover, clean edges and tight spine. Bottom staple has a minor stress line and a few, very light dings along back edge. Flat, clean and tight with excellent eye appeal and supple paper. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $268; NM 9.4 value = $850.

The Amazing Spider-Man #50 (Marvel, 1967). Great 50th issue features the classic cover story "Spider-Man No More!" Peter Parker contemplates whether or not to continue his career as Spider-Man just as the master crime-boss the Kingpin (in his first appearance) shows up. Beautiful copy has very rich color inks and fresh white paper. Perfectly flat and very sharp with superb overall eye appeal. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $500.

Detective Comics #62 (DC, 1942). A striking Jerry Robinson cover highlights this Golden Age gem. An impressive copy, only minor flaws are visible, including slight dust shadowing on the back cover, a very slight chip at the bottom spine, and a small pencil mark at the top front cover. A lovely book with deep, rich cover colors. CGC VF- 7.5 Tan to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $695; NM 9.4 value = $2,200.

Murphy Anderson - Original Art for Hawkman #1, page 7 (DC, 1964). Outstanding page of original art by the incomparable Murphy Anderson, as Hawkman and Hawkgirl eavesdrop on the Mayan villain while he ruminates on his history. Anderson was one of the most realistic and detail-oriented of the great Silver Age artists, and this page shows his meticulous work at its finest, especially in the elaborate Mayan costume of the bad guy. Page is "large art," approximately 14" x 22", with moderate white-out and no blue-line corrections.

Startling Terror Tales #11 Northford pedigree (Star Publications, 1952). One of the most sought-after of all horror comics featuring the classic "Ghost Spider of Death" cover by L.B. Cole. Incredible colors with fire-reds and bright yellows make this scene leap right off the cover. Spine is tight with no staple stress. Back cover has some chipping and tears at bottom and top edges. Label note reads: "heavy tanning interior front and back cover". One look at this cover and you will see why this issue is always in such high demand. CGC FN+ 6.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $316; NM 9.4 value = $1,000.

Strange Tales #110 (Marvel, 1963). Key issue features the first appearance of the Master of the Mystic Arts, Dr. Strange, his Mentor, The Ancient One and his man-servant Wong. The fiery Human Torch leads off this issue as he takes on Paste-Pot Pete with cover by Jack "King" Kirby and story art by Steve Ditko. Very nice looking high-grade copy has deep, dark color inks with clean cover edges and no "Marvel chipping". Tight, clean and fully flat with the spine edge having a few soft stresses. Quite scarce in the high-grades due to the book's very dark cover that accents every tiny imperfection. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,400.

Superman Group (DC, 1945-46). Excellent lot of mid-grade Superman books. Includes: #37 FN-, Toyman cover & appearance; #40 VG, Mr. Mxyzptlk cover & appearance; #44 VG, Toyman; #45 VG, famous "China" cover; and #47 VG. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $437; FN 6.0 value for group = $1,311. (Total: 5 Comic Books)

Picture Stories from the Bible #3: Old Testament Gaines File pedigree (DC / EC, 1943). An outstanding copy of this hard-to-find issue. Deep greens, bright yellows and vivid reds on the cover make this an extremely attractive book. A nicer copy would be hard to find. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $190.

Vault of Horror #26 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1952). Classic Johnny Craig cover. Illustrations from this issue were used in Parade of Pleasure. Beautiful copy with a very small chip at the bottom spine, otherwise sharp, flat and clean. A great addition to any high-grade collection. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $365.

Crime SuspenStories #14 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1952). Brutal Johnny Craig cover. An excellent example of a stunning Gaines File pedigree book. Clean and crisp, with only the most minor flaws. According to CGC, there is not a nicer copy of this book. CGC NM+ 9.6 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $240.

Crime SuspenStories #19 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1953). Rich Al Feldstein cover. Great color and sharp corners on a nearly perfect copy from the private collection of Bill Gaines. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

Teen-Age Dope Slaves #1 Aurora pedigree (Harvey, 1952). This book presents a "Shocking Dope Expose"! Popular first and only issue depicts the reality of drug abuse among teen-agers, which was used in the infamous book Seduction of the Innocent. This is a very attractive copy of an issue that is almost non-existent in the higher grades. Great colors and gloss with supple paper and very clean edges. Spine has a few light dings. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $285; NM 9.4 value = $900.

Tales of Suspense #48 (Marvel, 1963). Iron Man dons his classic red and gold armor for the first time in this issue as he battles Mister Doll with cover/story art by Steve Ditko. Gorgeous, super-high-grade comic has rich, sharp color inks, great gloss and super-clean edges with no "Marvel chipping" whatsoever. Near flawless spine, secure staples (no stress) and perfect centering. One of the best copies we have ever seen! CGC NM 9.4 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $325.

Red Dragon #3 (Street & Smith, 1948). Very cool Hooded Skeleton-Ghost cover (by Bob Powell) has the 1st appearance of Dr. Neff, Ghost Breaker. Very high-grade copy of this issue has a great black cover with superb color inks, clean cover edges and supple paper. Back cover has a triangle-shaped dust shadow at top right corner. The best and only copy we have ever seen! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $500.

Amazing Spider-Man Group (Marvel, 1966-71). High-grade group of six Spider-Man gems includes issues #32 CGC VF/NM 9.0 Light tan to off-white pages; #41 (1st appearance The Rhino) CGC VF/NM 9.0 Light tan to off-white pages; #44 (2nd appearance The Lizard) CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white pages; #47 (Kraven appearance) CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages; #51 (First full appearance The Kingpin) CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages; and #98 (Green Goblin battle issue) CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages with small date stamp on back cover. Artwork by Steve Ditko, John Romita and Gil Kane is featured. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value for group = $895. (Total: 6 Comic Books)

Superman Golden Age Group (DC, 1944-52). Big lot of early Golden Age Supermans, most in Good. This lot also contains a Fair copy of #76, featuring the very first Superman/Batman teamup. Lot includes: #30 apparent VG/FN, moderate amateur restoration; #43 GD, extra staples; #46 GD, cover detached; #48 GD, writing on cover; #50 GD, cover detached; #53 GD; #59 apparent VG, minor amateur restoration (glue at staples); #60 GD, baseball cover; #65 GD+; #66 GD+, baseball cover; #67 GD, cover detached, Perry Como appearance; #75 GD, glue on spine; and #76 FR, tape repairs. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $1,346. (Total: 13 Comic Books)

World's Finest Comics #7 (DC, 1942). Superman, Batman and Robin lead the US Navy armed forces into battle on this beautiful Patriotic WWII cover by Jack Burnley. Green Arrow begins his series in this book, which also includes art by Joe Simon and Jack Kirby. High-grade square-bound book has bright yellow cover and full white interior paper. Minor wear at spine area and at cover edges. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF 8.0 White pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $570; NM 9.4 value = $1,800.

Giant Comics Editions Diary Secrets #12 (St. John, 1949). This rare square-bound edition is highlighted by an alluring prostitute cover by Golden-Age "Good Girl" legend Matt Baker. A very solid copy with wonderful color strike and beautiful blue background color inks, accented with vibrant banana-yellows and rich ruby-reds. The book is flat, clean and tight with excellent centering and very fresh white interior paper. There is a piece out of the top upper right edge of the front cover and a 1/2" tear at the right edge. Additionally, there is some general overall wear to the spine area and cover edges, but the overall eye-appeal is excellent. Seldom seen in any condition. Condition: VG. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $88; FN 6.0 value = $260.

Famous Funnies: A Carnival Of Comics (1933). A clean and complete example of this historically significant book. Spine shows wear,with minor damage at the top and bottom spine. Overall this is a very attractive book. CGC VG- 3.5 Cream pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $913; FN 6.0 value = $2,740.

Four Color Comics #386 (Dell, 1952). From 1952 comes a classic appearance of Duckburg's richest citizen, Scrooge McDuck. Considered by Duck collectors to be the true Uncle Scrooge #1. Drawn by "the Duckman", Carl Barks, himself, and an incredibly difficult issue to find in high grade. In fact, according to CGC's latest population report, this is the nicest copy of this book that has been certified! There is some spine stress, and some minor wear along the top of the front cover, otherwise a beautiful copy with a flat, tight spine and a gorgeous overall look. From the classic cover to the classic story, "Only a Poor Old Man", this is the book to own for all you Barks fans! CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-White pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $275; NM 9.4 value = $1,100.

The Amazing Spider-Man #23 (Marvel, 1965). Spider-Man engages in full melee combat with his most deadly arch-enemy in "The Goblin and the Gangsters!" Third appearance of the Green Goblin with cover and story art by Steve Ditko. Superb fresh copy has great colors, paper quality and eye-appeal. Completely flat, clean and tight with no chipping and just a minor bit of wear at lower staple. Back cover has a small date stamp. Spider-Man #23 is one of the most popular of the first 50 issues. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

The Incredible Hulk #1 (Marvel, 1962). A popular Marvel key, the Hulk debuts in his own series. This is a moderately rough copy which shows quite a bit of wear and spine stress, but is complete, and still exhibits excellent color and clarity. An affordable alternative for the Hulk collector. CGC VG 4.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $600; FN 6.0 value = $1800.

Exciting Comics #62 (Nedor Publications, 1948). Golden Age excitement featuring The Black Terror, and a fabulous airbrushed bondage cover by Alex Schomburg (signing as Xela). This is an outstanding example of this rare book, with exceptional color and a crisp appearance. Only visible problem is a date written in pencil on the front cover. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

Superman Group (DC, 1941- 45). A lot of four excellent low-grade issues of Superman, including: #10 VG/FN, appears FN, but has a small drill-hole in upper left corner that runs through entire book; #29 VG, patriotic cover; #31and 36 VG. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $600; FN 6.0 value for group = $1,801. (Total: 4 Comic Books)

Superman Group (DC, 1942-43). Lot of four very early issues of Superman, all in VG. Includes: #16; #21; #22; and #24 (classic American flag cover). Overstreet GD 2.0 value for group = $610; FN 6.0 value for group = $1,833. (Total: 4 Comic Books)

Superman Early Mid-Grade Group (DC, 1943-44). Nice lot of three early mid-grade issues of Superman. Includes: #25 VG, classic "artist" cover; #26 VG/FN; and #28 VG/FN, Nazi cover. Overstreet GD 2.0 value for group = $400; FN 6.0 value for group = $1,201. (Total: 3 Comic Books)

Captain America Comics #17 (Timely, 1942). A superb Al Avison cover of Cap punching out some green-skinned monsters sets the tone for this bizarre yet patriotic issue, which features the only appearance of the Fighting Fool. Early Caps are tough to find in good condition, and this is a nice mid-grade copy with tremendous eye appeal. Very slight curl and light discoloration to the left edge of the book, and some dust shadowing on the back. CGC notes a small piece off the corner of page one. CGC FN+ 6.5 Light tan to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $853; NM 9.4 value = $2700.

Sub-Mariner Comics #5 (Timely, 1942). A classic cover, with Sub-Mariner single-handedly protecting America from an Axis invasion. A solid copy, with moderate spine wear, a small split at the top spine, and minor chipping at the right edge. Very attractive and appealing. CGC FN+ 6.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $758.

Don Martin - Original Cover Art for Mad's Don Martin Sails Ahead. Few artists in any medium have a style as instantly recognizable as Don Martin, best known for his hilarious illustrations that graced the pages of Mad magazine for many years. Quirky and stylish, Martin's art was always precise and sharply focused. He had a supreme mastery of facial expressions and a delightful ability to render subtly funny moments. The piece offered here, the cover to one of his many paperback collections, is a magnificent full-color example of the quintessential Don Martin. The image area is approximately 8.5" x 14.5", with an overall size, including frame, of 15" x 21". A fabulous piece by a great American cartoonist. From the Grant Geissman collection.

The Haunt of Fear #28 Gaines File pedigree 1/12 (EC, 1954). Fabulous high-grade copy of one of the seminal horror titles of the 1950s. A "Ghastly" Graham Ingels cover highlights this exceptionally high-grade copy with interior art by Jack Davis, Jack Kamen, and Ingels. Virtually flawless, this is a great addition to any high-grade EC collection. Additionally, this issue bears the distinction of being #1 from the Gaines File pedigree set. #1 File issues are extremely prized by EC collectors, so this is a unique opportunity to acquire this exceptional collectible. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $230.

Suspense Comics #2 Crowley pedigree (Continental Magazines, 1944). Sporting a Nina Albright cover, this copy shows moderate spine wear, a bit of spine roll, some light tanning, and a slight miscut. A nice copy nonetheless of a hard to find issue with L.B. Cole interior art. CGC FN 6.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $885.

Gem Comics #1 (Spotlight/Palace, 1945). The one-and-only issue of Gem Comics, with jungle action galore. This is an outstanding copy with light stress to the top edge of the book, and slight dinginess on the back cover. Virtually no spine stress and a fresh, vibrant cover with excellent clarity. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $120; NM 9.4 value = $350.

Contact Comics #10 Big Apple pedigree (Aviation Press, 1946). Magnificent copy of this comic book has an eye-catching WWII Bomber cover by L.B. Cole. Stunning colors, sharp corners and perfect cover edges with outstanding overall eye appeal. We've never seen a copy that even comes close to this one! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $300.

X-Men #28 (Marvel 1967). X-Men star in "The Wail of the Banshee!" First appearance of the Banshee with art by Dick Ayers. This book has a striking deep blood-red cover, great gloss, no "Marvel chipping" and superb overall eye-appeal. Perfectly flat and very tight with a sharp spine and very fresh white paper. Rare in this top grade. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $180.

Crime SuspenStories #18 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1953). A classic Johnny Craig cover highlights this stunning copy. Deep, rich colors, sharp corners, and exceptional page quality make this an extremely desirable book. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

An Earth Man on Venus (nn) (Avon, 1951). One-shot issue has an attacking Giant Ant cover by Gene Fawcette and story art by Wally Wood. Nice supple book has bright yellow cover and some minor wear at spine edge. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $373; NM 9.4 value = $1,180.

The Incredible Hulk #6 (Marvel, 1963). Steve Ditko provides the stunning cover and interior art to this Silver Age classic as the Hulk battles The Metal Master! A lovely copy, this book shows very little, if any, "Marvel chipping", so common in books from this period. Light tanning on the back cover, very minor spine stress, and a very slight bend at the upper left back cover are the only flaws that keep this from a higher grade. Nice, bright, and appealing. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $462; NM 9.4 value = $2,300.

Marvel High-Grade Silver Age Group (Marvel, 1967-72). High-grade group of Marvels includes Conan the Barbarian #3 CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white to white pages; Marvel Super-Heroes #12 (Origin and 1st appearance Captain Marvel) CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages; Marvel Team-Up #1 (Spider-Man and Human Torch) CGC NM- 9.2 White pages; Silver Surfer #4 (classic Surfer/ Thor battle) CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages; and Sub-Mariner #1 (origin re-told) CGC NM- 9.4 White pages. Very good-looking group of key books. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 total value for group = $1,075. (Total: 5 Comic Books)

Superman Golden Age Group (DC, 1951-54). Lot of 12 Golden Age issues of Superman, in lower grades. Includes: #69 VG, Prankster; #70 GD/VG, Annie Oakley appearance; #71 VG; #72 VG-; #73 VG; #74 GD/VG; #78 VG; #79, 82, 84, 89 GD/VG; and #90 VG. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $680; FN 6.0 value for group = $2,040. (Total: 12 Comic Books)

Aquaman #1 (DC, 1962). The King of the Seven Seas makes a splash in his own title with art by Nick Cardy. High-grade copy has sharp and bright colors with good gloss and an excellent printing cut. Light wear at the top and bottom of the spine keeps this copy from the NM range. CGC notes, "N written on cover in pencil." CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $850.

Captain America Comics #59 (Timely, 1946). Cap's origin is retold in this classic issue, which also features a Human Torch story, and a terrific cover by Syd Shores. Mid-grade copy shows overall wear; left edge is worn and book shows moderate spine stress. Date stamp on cover. Back cover is dingy, with light creasing at corners. Cover still has excellent color and clarity. CGC FN+ 6.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $885; NM 9.4 value = $2,800.

Young Men #28 (Atlas, 1954). The Human Torch by Carl Burgos stars in this scarce issue with back-up stories of Captain America by John Romita and the Sub-Mariner by Bill Everett. Striking black cover accented with rich reds and yellows. Nice mid-grade comic has some wear along spine and top edge of front cover. Beautiful, fully white paper (very uncommon for Atlas books).From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF- 7.5 White pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $316; NM 9.4 value = $1,000.

Picture Stories from the Bible: Complete New Testament Edition Gaines File pedigree (EC, 1946). Incredible copy of this large square-bound issue features the classic life story of Jesus Christ, from his birth in Bethlehem, death at Calvary, and resurrection. Also included is the story of Paul's missionary journeys. Nearly perfect with tremendous color strike, sharp edges and corners and fresh white paper. 6 interior pages are siamese. The finest copy we have ever seen! CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $190.

Picture Stories From American History #2 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1946). Large 52-page comic depicts America's period of colonization and independence. Super killer copy in every way with a perfect spine, no staple stress at all, perfectly clean cover edges and razor sharp corners. Incredible with very supple off-white paper. Most likely the finest copy in existence. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $195.

Valor #1 Gaines File pedigree 12/12 (EC, 1955). The premiere issue of this "New Direction" title, sporting a gorgeous Wally Wood cover showing two knights in mounted combat, was not approved by the newly-formed Comics Code. Clean, sharp and flat, with outstanding page quality. A worthy addition to any collection. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $260.

X-Men Bronze Age Group (Marvel, 1979-81). Here's a huge lot of beautiful CGC NM 9.4 issues from the classic years of the X-Men, including the beginnings of the Dark Phoenix saga. This lot includes: #125 Off-white to white pages; #128 Off-white to white pages; #127 White pages; #129 Off-white to white pages; #131 Cream pages; #132 Off-white pages; #134 Off-white pages; #139 Off-white to white pages; #140 Off-white to white pages; #142 Off-white to white pages. 10 books total. Overstreet NM 9.4 value for group = $357. (Total: 10 Comic Books)

House of Secrets #92 (DC, 1971). A beautiful copy of a classic book, presenting the origin and first appearance of the Swamp Thing! Appearing virtually unread, this is a near-pristine example of this issue, with vibrant colors, sharp corners, and an excellent cut. A very desirable book, made even more so by the superb condition. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $138; NM 9.4 value = $600.

Space Adventures #12 River City (Charlton, 1954). Classic cover by Steve Ditko. This very attractive high-grade copy shows a bit of wear at the upper spine, but is otherwise clean, flat, and beautiful with sharp corners, clean edges and a good cut. A difficult to find book in any condition, this would make an excellent addition to any collection. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $165; NM 9.4 value = $525.

Record Book of Famous Police Cases (St. John, 1949). A very rare square-bound book which features cover art by Matt Baker and interior work by Joe Kubert. This stunning copy has tremendous color inks, very clean edges and sharp corners. Very flat and bright, this copy is, by far, the finest we have ever seen offered at any time. Incredible! CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $300.

The Amazing Spider-Man #13 (Marvel, 1964). In this early issue, Spider-Man comes face-to-face with Mysterio for the first time! Fabulous cover/interior art by Steve Ditko. Very nice copy of this issue (very tough to find in the higher grades; it has super colors, excellent gloss and very clean cover edges (no "Marvel chipping"). Centering is perfect and eye-appeal is excellent. Black area of spine has a few spine stress lines (which is common for this issue) and slight stress at lower staple. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $850.

Shock Illustrated #3 (EC, 1956). Super-rare EC "Picto-Fiction" (text stories with numerous illustrations); only 100 copies of this issue were produced and given out at the EC offices. The rest of the print run was destroyed because Gaines couldn't afford to have them bound! Terrific artwork by EC stalwarts Graham Ingels, Reed Crandall, Jack Kamen, and George Evans with a fabulous cover painting by Rudy Nappi. This is one of the rarest EC books, and the few copies that exist rarely come on to the open market. This is an excellent copy, a solid FN+. It has a 1/2" split at the top of the spine, moderate spine stress, light creasing (does not break ink) in both the top right and bottom right corners, and has a moderately bumped top left corner. The cover still has outstanding clarity and color. Magazine format. From the Grant Geissman Collection. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $268; NM 9.4 value = $850.

Master Comics #55 Mile High pedigree (Fawcett, 1944). A fabulous example of a high-grade treasure. There are two pencil notations at the upper left front cover, but in other regards, an exceptionally beautiful copy. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $175.

Famous Funnies #209 (Eastern Color, 1953). One of the most desirable issues of this long-running title, this is where Buck Rogers makes his Famous Funnies debut, inside a gorgeous Frank Frazetta cover. Never an easy book to find, especially in high grade, this copy is clean and flat with sharp corners and good color. There is a 3" abrasion along the left edge of the front cover, but that is the only visible damage to this very appealing book. CGC VF+ 8.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $309; NM 9.4 value = $975.

Treasure Comics #7 Big Apple pedigree (Prize Publications, 1946). Stunning copy of this esoteric comic has incredible colors, perfect edges, immaculate spine, no staple stress and full paper freshness. Exceptional in every way! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $300.

Strange Tales #115 (Marvel, 1963). Key issue as the Human Torch battles the Uncanny Sandman with a cross-over by everyone's favorite Web-Slinger. Top-grade fresh book with superb color strike and tremendous gloss. Just the smallest hint of wear at the very top and bottom of the spine keeps this beauty from the strict NM 9.4 range. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $500.

Batman #6 (DC, 1941). An outstanding cover of the Dynamic Duo leads off these "brand new whirlwind adventures!" This is a very solid mid-grade copy that shows minor spine roll, slightly rusty staples, and general overall wear. Cover is very crisp, with strong colors and a great look. CGC FN- 5.5 Cream pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $420; FN 6.0 value = $1260.

The Brave and the Bold #30 Bethlehem pedigree (DC, 1960). The third appearance of the Justice League of America as they face Amazo, the Amazing Android! This copy shows minimal spine wear with slightly rounded corners and a date stamp on the back cover. All in all, a very attractive and presentable book. CGC VF- 7.5 Light tan to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $407; NM 9.4 value = $1,900.

Moon Girl #5 (EC, 1948). This copy presents very well, with few visible defects except for some light tanning on the back cover and a 1/2" pencil mark in the upper left front corner. CGC VF+ 8.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $285; NM 9.4 value = $900.

Mad #18 Gaines File 3/12 (EC, 1954). Great Kurtzman cover. Flat, clean copy, with brilliant cover color and sharp corners. An outstanding book from the early run of this landmark title. CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

Crime SuspenStories #17 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1953). Johnny Craig comes through again with another graphic cover. This issue includes an adaptation of Ray Bradbury's "Touch and Go", and a beautiful collaboration between Al Williamson and Frank Frazetta. The upper right corner shows some slight damage, but it barely detracts from the overall appeal of this great book. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $300.

X-Men High-Grade Bronze Age Group (Marvel, 1978-1980). An outstanding lot of high-grade Bronze Age X-Men comics, with covers and art by John Byrne and Dave Cockrum. Includes the first full appearance of Alpha Flight, as well as the first appearances of The Shadow King and The Dazzler, the origin of Professor Xavier, and the disbanding of Alpha Flight. Lot includes: #113 CGC NM 9.4 White pages; #116 CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages; #117 CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages; #118 CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages; #121 CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages; #122 CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages; #130 CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages; and #140 CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value for group = $370. (Total: 8 Comic Books)

Smash Comics #45 Mile High pedigree (Quality, 1943). An exceptional example of a book that is hard to find in high grade. This copy shows only the most minor imperfections, including two small chips at the top front cover, slight spine stress and a small tear at the upper back spine. Overall, a very attractive and desirable book. CGC VF/NM 9.0 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $300.

Captain Flight #10 Big Apple pedigree (Four Star Publications, 1945). Incredible blood-red and bright sun-yellow color inks with clean cover edges and fully fresh white paper. Flat and clean with a small chip at lower corner of spine and a thin line of color loss near center of spine. There is a 2" tear on centerfold. Never seen a finer copy! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $425.

Fight Comics #43 Big Apple pedigree (Fiction House, 1946). Great pagan sacrifice-bondage cover highlights this issue with interior art by the talented Matt Baker. Super-killer copy has an immaculate spine (no staple stress), perfect cover edges and sharp corners. Unreal! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $185.

Tales of Suspense #53 (Marvel, 1964). Magnificent copy of this early issue stars the Invincible Iron Man, includes the origin of the Watcher, and the second appearance of the Black Widow. Cover artwork by Jack "King" Kirby. Gorgeous book with stunning color inks, excellent gloss, sharp corners and perfectly clean cover edges (no "Marvel chipping"). Rare in this top condition, this is one of the very best copies in existence. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $150.

Batman #26 (DC, 1945). Early issue sports a fun Bat-Sled cover by Jerry Robinson. Appealing black cover with great colors and supple paper. Very flat and clean copy has terrific overall eye-appeal and a near flawless spine. Very tiny color flake at upper right corner of front cover and just the slightest bit of wear at top and bottom corners of back cover. This book looks much nicer than its grade would indicate. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $442; NM 9.4 value = $1,400.

Gang Busters #23 Mile High pedigree (DC, 1951). This is a book with everything going for it! Not only is it in beautiful condition, with deep, rich cover colors, sharp corners and a good cut, it's also part of the Mile High pedigree collection, a pedigree known for superlative condition. In addition, this copy also boasts a double cover (CGC has noted that both covers grade 9.6)! Even among Mile High pedigree comics, as rare and desirable as they are, this book is a gem. A unique opportunity to acquire a unique book. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $100.

Rich Buckler and Bob Oksner - Original Cover Art for Action Comics #478 (DC, 1977). A great image of the Man of Steel as he saves the Earth from alien invaders! Bronze Age art is red-hot right now, with exceptional images like this leading the pack. This cover, which measures approximately 11.25" x 17", is in very fine condition, with slight soiling at the edges and two light stains on the right side (There is an arrow pointing to one, with the notation, "Dear Joe - Who was drinking coffee? - Bob"). There is also a notation that reads, "Bob - Pay special attention to Supe's head - Joe". In addition, there is a very soft crease running horizontally across the art. This art is offered in its pure state, without any logo or type, apparently done on a separate overlay, which is not included.

Red Dragon #2 (Street & Smith, 1948). Attractive book has an interesting evil ghouls in clown suits cover and art by Bob Powell. Flat and supple book has great colors and good eye appeal. There is a light surface abrasion in the "R" and "E" on the logo and a minor tear at lower staple. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $189; NM 9.4 value = $600.

Journey into Mystery Group (Marvel, 1964). Good-looking high-grade group of books starring the Mighty Thor includes issues #103 (1st appearance Enchantress) CGC VF/NM 9.0 Light tan to off-white pages; #105 CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages; and #109 CGC VF/NM 9.0 White pages. All have artwork by the prolific Jack "King" Kirby and include classic battles with the Cobra and Mr. Hyde (#105) and the evil Mutant Magneto (#109). Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value for group = $325. (Total: 3 Comic Books)

X-Men #15 (Marvel, 1965). An appearance by the mysterious Sentinels, and the origin of the Beast highlight this fun-filled entry in the X-Men saga, with dandy art by Jack Kirby and Dick Ayers. Very nice copy with white pages; slightly mis-cut at the top, but otherwise pretty spectacular. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $300.

Star Spangled Comics #51 Crowley pedigree (DC, 1945). A killer book that stars The Guardian and Newsboy Legion with a Robot cover by Joe Simon and Jack Kirby. Stunner with superb colors, perfectly clean edges and very tight spine and staples (no stress). Staples are placed about 1/2" in from spine edge. The best copy we have ever seen. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

Superman #6-8 Group (DC, 1940). Three consecutive early issues of Superman, all in GD, except #7, which is GD+. All complete, with no pieces missing, these are excellent low-grade reading copies for the collector looking to complete their Superman run without spending a fortune. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $884. (Total: 3 Comic Books)

Frontline Combat #7 Gaines File pedigree 7/10 (EC, 1952). Classic Harvey Kurtzman cover highlights a special Iwo Jima issue. A fabulous copy with great eye-appeal from a legendary collection. A very desirable book, only 10 copies of this issue were in the Gaines File pedigree collection. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $175.

Mad #1 (EC, 1952). Solid low-grade copy of this key first issue has good colors and light cream pages. Most wear is on back cover with a 2" crease and 1/4" tear at bottom edge. Book has a FN-like appearance from front cover. Condition: VG. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $520; FN 6.0 value = $1,560.

X-Men Bronze Age Group (Marvel, 1979-80). Here is an outstanding lot of eight Bronze Age X-Men comics, all graded an uncanny CGC NM+ 9.6! From that golden period of 1979-1980, these all have incredible artwork by John Byrne! Included are: #126 Off-white to white pages; #135 Off-white to white pages; #136 Off-white to white pages; #137 White pages; #138 Off-white pages; two copies of #140 White pages; and #141 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value for group = $267. (Total: 7 Comic Books)

Jack Kirby - Original Title Splash art for Kamandi #24 (DC, 1974). Pencilled by Jack Kirby, inked by D. Bruce Berry. Dramatic chapter title-splash page by "King" Kirby, from an eerie issue where Kamandi is stranded on a nearly-deserted island haunted by a mysterious force. In this exceptional full-page image, the evil force launches a pillar of flame at the Last Boy On Earth, and his frightened hosts. Vintage '70s Kirby, and a great image from a very cool story. App. 10" x 16" on standard DC board, some white-out in the title lettering, but virtually none in the artwork.

Romance Comics Group (Fox Features, 1949-54). A nice group of 10 romance comic books which includes My Love Affair #1 FN-; #2 VF-; #5 FN; My Private Life #16 FN; My Secret Affair #1 FN+; #3 VG; My Secret Life #24 VF-; and My Love Secret #24 VF- (formerly Phantom Lady); #26 VG; and #28 GD/VG. These comics feature artwork by such legends as Wally Wood, Jack Kamen and Al Feldstein. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $149; FN 6.0 value for group = $510. (Total: 10 Comic Books)

Jungle Comics #79 Big Apple pedigree (Fiction House, 1946). Superior copy of this issue stars Ka'a'nga, Jungle Lord in "Death has a Thousand Fangs". Incredibly high-grade copy approaches perfection with nice interior paper. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $160.

Journey into Mystery Group (Marvel, 1962-64). A gigantic lot of books from the Thor run on Journey Into Mystery, all in mid-low grade. Includes: #84 (FN, second appearance of Thor); #85 (VG/FN); #87 (FN, minor color touch); #88 (FN); #91 (VG, minor color touch); #92 (VG/FN); #93 (VG+); #94 (FN); #95 (FN); #96 (FN); #98 (FN); #99 (FN); #101 (FN); #104 (VG); #107 (FN); #108 (FN); #109 (VG/FN); #110 (FN); #116 (VG); #120 (FN); #121 (FN); #122 (VG/FN); #123 (FN-); #125 (FN-); #126 (VG); #127 (VG/FN, some glue); #128 (VG/FN); #129 (FN); #132 (VG+); #135 (VG); #136 (FN); #137 (VG); #140 (VG); #141(FN); #143 (FN); #147 (FN); #149 (VG/FN); #152 (VG/FN); and #160 (VG/FN). Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $520; FN 6.0 value for group = $1525. (Total: 39 Comic Books)

Tales of Suspense Group (Marvel, 1963-67). This classic title stars the Invincible Iron Man, and, beginning in issue #59, The Star-Spangled Avenger, Captain America. This group includes issues #45 VG/FN; #50 VG; #51-52, 54-56 all FN; #57 VF; #58-59 FN; #60 FN/VF; #61 FN; #62 FN+ color touch; #65 VG+; #66, 68, 69 (2 copies), 70,71 all FN; #73 FN+; #74 FN; #75 VF; #76, 79, 81 all VF-; #82 FN; #83 VF-; #84 FN+; #85, 87 VF; #88 FN; #89 VF; #91, 92 FN; #94 FN/VF; #95 FN; and #97 VF-. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $961; NM 9.4 value for group = $3,560. (Total: 38 Comic Books)

Picture Stories from the Bible: Complete "Life of Christ" Edition Gaines File pedigree (DC / EC, 1944). Large 96 page square-bound edition featuring the complete life of Jesus Christ from his birth in a manger in Bethlehem, to his death on the cross at Calvary, to his resurrection. Phenomenal copy, nearly perfect in every way, with tremendous eye-appeal and very fresh interior paper. Probably the finest copy in the world! CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $190.

Li'l Abner #63 Mile High pedigree (Harvey, 1948). Tight spine, sharp corners, and great eye appeal highlight this classic issue featuring the Hero of Dogpatch. The only certified copy of this book, according to CGC's most recent population report. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-White to White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $160.

Superman Early Issues Group (DC, 1945-46). Big lot of seven early issues of Superman. Includes: #32 GD, classic lightning bolt cover; #33 GD/VG; #34 GD/VG, Red Cross cover; #35 GD/VG; #38 VG-, Superman reading a Batman comic on cover; #39 GD/VG; and #41 VG-, classic "artist" cover. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $679; FN 6.0 value for group = $2,037. (Total: 7 Comic Books)

Alex Ross - Original Pencil Prelim for Adventure #247 Cover Recreation (1999). Tight pencil prelim for one of two covers used for the 1999 Overstreet Comic Book Price Guide. Even in this small format, Ross' photo-realistic style shines, and his use of evocative shading gives this far more eye-appeal than your traditional pencil prelim. Approximately 7" x 10" on thin paper, with an image area of approximately 3 1/2" x 5". Signed by the artist in the lower right corner.

Amazing Mysteries #33 (Marvel, 1949). The very first horror comic from Marvel cover features "The Thing in the Vault!" A beautiful, scarce example of an early Pre-Code horror book, this copy is clean and flat with rich colors, good strike, and sharp corners. There is some spine stress, and slight wear to the top and bottom spine, along with some light tanning on the back cover, but overall this is a very appealing book. A must for any Pre-Code Horror collection! CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $300.

Sheldon "Shelly" Moldoff - Original Cover Art Recreation of Moon Girl #4. Best known as the creator of Hawkman and one of the early Batman artists, Shelly Moldoff was also the creator of Moon Girl, EC's only superhero. Here, Moldoff recreates one of his classic Moon Girl covers in full, vibrant color. With an image area of approximately 15" x 22", this piece is truly stunning: bright, exciting and masterfully rendered. Matted, framed, and ready to hang. From the Grant Geissman collection.

Jack Rickard - Original Cover Art for Mad Blasts. One of the core Mad Artists from 1961 until his death in 1983, Jack Rickard created many of the most memorable Mad covers in that magazine's history. This cover, from the paperback Mad Blasts, has an image area of approximately 14" x 20", and has been professionally matted and framed. It is signed by the artist in the lower right corner. From the Grant Geissman Collection.

Bob Clarke- Mad #128 Cover Art (1969). Humorous Bob Clark artwork for the "blackboard" cover of Mad #128. This lot consists of two separate pieces that were used to create this cover; first the crayon-drawing of Alfred E. Neuman (black on a white background), which was used as a negative-image to create the stat for the blackboard drawing. Also, a second board, on which the only original art is the chalk, erasers, and wooden rail at the bottom of the board; the entire upper section (including all the lettering) is a colored stat, from which the final image was shot. Alfred E. Neuman drawing is app. 7 1/2" x 7 1/2", and the larger layout board is app. 12" x 16".

Crime SuspenStories #23 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1954). Great George Evans cover highlights an issue that was used in the Senate Investigations on Juvenile Delinquency. This issue is marked by a 1" crease in the upper right corner, but otherwise this is an exceptionally nice copy, hard to find in high-grade due to its black cover. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

Valor #2 Gaines File pedigree 12/12 (EC, 1955). Part of EC's "New Direction", this comic presented "Tales of Mortal Combat, and Deeds of Valor!" From the historically important Gaines File pedigree collection, this copy has some very minor bends at the bottom left front corner and some slight tanning to the back cover. Otherwise, a stunning copy with a great gladiator cover by Al Williamson. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $220.

Intimate Confessions #1 (Realistic Comics, 1951). The cover says it all, "Days of Temptation...Nights of Desire..." This highly desired first issue has an alluring party-girl (in a fire-red dress) on a partial photo cover by Everett Raymond Kinstler with a beautiful deep blue background. This issue, seldom seen above the VG range, has superb colors and fully fresh white interior paper. The right edge of the front cover has some light creases and the staple area has minor stress as does the top corner of the spine. The book is flat, clean and tight and represents the finest copy of this issue we have ever seen. CGC FN/VF 7.0 White pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $221; NM 9.4 value = $700.

Strange Terrors #1 Aurora pedigree (St. John, 1952). This issue features "Zombies March by Moonlight" and has a dungeon bondage cover. Good-looking copy has great gloss and colors. Spine is flat with secure staples (no stress) and a very thin reading crease-line that runs parallel with the spine. Nice copy that is scarce above the FN range.From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $150; NM 9.4 value = $450.

Giant Comics Editions #16 (St. John, 1950). Giant square-bound comic features Little Audrey, Abbott and Costello and Casper the Friendly Ghost. Rare in this high grade, this copy has bright colors and good gloss with light cream paper. Condition: VF. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $550.

Fantastic Four #7 (Marvel, 1962). An early issue of "The World's Greatest Comic Magazine" from the legendary team of Stan Lee and Jack Kirby. This copy shows light spine stress and very minor creasing at the top cover. Overall, a very attractive and desirable book. CGC VF+ 8.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $206; NM 9.4 value = $960.

The Silver Surfer #1 (Marvel, 1968). Marvel's space-born superhero flies solo in the first issue of his own magnificent magazine. This is a difficult issue to find in high-grade due to its square binding. Clean and flat, with minimal spine wear, this is a beautiful copy of a classic issue. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $525.

Marvel Silver Age Group (Marvel, 1966-69). Here is a collection of outstanding Silver Age Marvel books, all in gorgeous NM 9.4 condition! Lot includes: Nick Fury, Agent of S.H.E.I.L.D. #1 White pages; Sub-Mariner #6 Off-white to white pages; The Incredible Hulk #111 & #112 Cream to off-white pages; Thor #132 & #156 Off-white to white pages. CGC NM 9.4. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value for group = $270. (Total: 6 Comic Books)

Superman #76 (DC, 1952). Superman and Batman become "The Mightiest Team on Earth" in this landmark issue, which features early art by legendary Superman artist Curt Swan (only his second Superman assignment), as well as a second story showcasing the art of Shuster-studio protégé Wayne Boring. Some light spine stress, a slight tear in the right front edge, and a pencilled "Paid" in the upper left front cover are the only visible flaws in this copy. CGC VF- 7.5 White pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $505; NM 9.4 value = $1,600.

Frontline Combat #9 Gaines File pedigree 7/10 (EC, 1952). Special Civil War issue. This EC gem shows a slight bend at the top spine, but few other visible flaws. Beautiful colors, sharp corners, and an overall attractive appearance make this a very desirable copy of this classic book. Only 10 copies of this issue were included in the Gaines File pedigree collection. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $175.

Artist Unknown - Captain Marvel Adventures #5 Original Artwork (Fawcett, 1941). Page 43, from the story "Captain Marvel Solves the Swamp Mystery." We are not able to confirm the artist of this page (possibly Mark Swayze?), but it is still an outstanding action page from a very early issue of Captain Marvel Adventures, as the Big Red Cheese saves a couple of yokels from the "Swamp Devil." Cap appears in every panel, including some nice flying and action shots. Large art, approximately 15" x 20" on heavy illustration board, with very little white-out and the usual engravers notations written in blue pencil in all panels. Some browning around the edges, and corners are slightly blunted, but none of this affects the art. A superb display piece.

The Amazing Spider-Man #1 (Marvel, 1963). A complete, very low-grade copy of this super-key book. Heavy overall wear on cover and spine, but with decent colors and no large pieces out and no pages missing. Condition: FR-. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $800.

Tales of Suspense #9 (Marvel, 1960). He Lives!! The Demon from the Fifth Dimension!! "Diablo!" High-grade pre-hero issue has art by Jack "King" Kirby and "Sturdy" Steve Ditko and includes an Iron Man prototype. Superb color inks (intense blood-reds and beautiful purples) and great gloss with perfect cut and no "Marvel chipping" whatsoever. Spine is sharp with just the slightest hint of wear at the top corner and along the edge. Scarce this nice! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $112; NM 9.4 value = $450.

Sensation Comics #102 (DC, 1951). Difficult to find in high-grade, this is an exceptionally nice copy from the latter part of this great book's run. A copy with excellent eye-appeal (which features one of comic's few "male bondage" covers), this book shows only the slightest damage to the top and bottom spine, a few points of spine stress, an "M" written on the front cover in pen, and very minor chipping to the right edge of the front cover. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $375.

Superman Patriotic Group (DC, 1941-43). Lot of four early Superman issues, all with patriotic/anti-Axis covers. All are in GD, except as noted: #12; #13 GD/VG, some glue; #17 (classic Hitler cover); #23. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $801. (Total: 4 Comic Books)

The Flash #112 (DC, 1960). Key Silver Age book features the origin and first appearance of Ralph Dibney, the Elongated Man. Nice copy with minor spine wear and very few visible flaws. Flat, sharp and clean. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $475.

Rick Leonardi & Al Milgrom - Original Cover Art for Excalibur #19 (Marvel, 1991). This outstanding cover from Leonardi and Milgrom features a cool image of Wolverine and the X-Men coming to the rescue of the temporarily defeated Excalibur gang. Bold black background really highlights the excellent figure work and energetic inking.

All-Winners Comics #9 (Timely, 1943). When America entered World War II, no one fought harder and with more abandon than the Timely Superheroes. Captain America, The Human Torch, and The Sub-Mariner put aside their differences and waged an all-out war against the Axis Menace! This is a classic example of this great WWII-era comic, with a bright, busy cover by Alex Schomburg. Slight chipping at the top spine, some minor dust shadowing and minor staining/foxing on the back cover don't reduce the overall eye appeal of this great comic. CGC FN+ 6.5 Off-White pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $505.

Picture Stories from the Bible #2: Old Testament Edition Gaines File pedigree (EC, 1943). A stunning copy of this 2nd issue featuring great Old Testament stories of Joseph, Moses and Joshua. Appealing royal-blue cover, immaculate spine (no staple stress) and superb eye appeal. Never seen any copy that even comes close. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $190.

Startling Comics #35 Mile High pedigree (Better Publications, 1945). A classic drug story highlights this Golden Age gem, all wrapped within an incredible Schomburg cover. You'll actually see the Fighting Yank attacked by a hypodermic needle! From the highly-desirable Mile High pedigree collection, this copy has had tape removed from the back cover and the last five pages. This is, however, a very presentable copy with excellent paper quality. CGC FN 6.0 White pages. Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $150.

The Amazing Spider-Man #42 (Marvel, 1966). In this issue, Spidey stars in "The Birth of a Super-Hero!" Key highlight includes the first full appearance of Mary Jane Watson. Classic cover and story art by John Romita. Very attractive top-grade book has superb color strike and fully fresh pages. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $175.

Star Spangled Comics #38 Crowley pedigree (DC, 1944). Stars The Guardian and Newsboy Legion with cover art by Joe Simon and Jack Kirby. Bright and fresh copy has near perfect centering and fantastic overall eye-appeal. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $550.

Superman Low-Grade Group (DC, 1954-60). Large lot of early Superman issues, all in GD/VG. Includes: #92, #94, #96, #107, #108, #110, #111 (extra staple), #112, #113, #114, #116, #118, #121, #123, #129, #132 (stains), #133, and #136. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $587; FN 6.0 value for group = $1,757. (Total: 18 Comic Books)

Strange Terrors #7 (St. John, 1953). A 100-page giant issue with an unusual male bondage cover by Joe Kubert. The square-bound spine is in unusually good condition, although there is a slight crease along the edge and a small ding to the bottom spine. A beautiful book overall, with excellent eye-appeal. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $158; NM 9.4 value = $475.

Ghost Rider (Vol. 1) #4 (Magazine Enterprises, 1951). Once you see this classic Frazetta cover, you'll never forget it! Tough to find in high grade, this book shows very few visible defects. There is a slight bend in the upper left corner, a small color chip out of the lower right front cover corner, and very slight spine wear, but overall this book possesses great eye appeal and presents very nicely. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $575.

The Avengers #1 (Marvel, 1963). Classic first issue features the origin and first appearance of the Avengers (Thor, The Hulk, Iron Man, Ant-Man and The Wasp) as they face the evil Norse god Loki. Cover and story art by Jack "King" Kirby. Solid mid-grade book has excellent colors and gloss with no "Marvel chipping" and off-white pages. There is some minor spine wear and three creases at the lower right corner of front cover. Condition: FN. Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $642; NM 9.4 value = $3,200.

X-Men #21 (Marvel, 1966). Stunning copy of this issue has rich colors, incredible gloss and fully fresh white cover and interior paper. Super killer with outstanding overall eye-appeal. For the most demanding collectors. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $125.

Flash Comics #83 (DC, 1947). This exceptional high-grade copy shows very few defects. There is some moderate spine wear, and a slight crease on the back cover, but overall this book presents very nicely. Great eye-appeal. CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $221; NM 9.4 value = $700.

Superman Golden Age Group (DC, 1952-56). Large lot of Golden Age Superman books, mostly in the GD/GD+ range. Includes: #77 GD+, cover detached, tape repairs; #80 GD+; #81 FR, mildew; #83 FR/GD, spine split; #85 GD+; #86, 87 GD, cover detached; #88 GD, spine split; #91 GD, cover detached; #93 GD; #95, 98 GD+; #99 GD; #102 GD+; #103 GD, one page detached; #104 GD, chunk torn out of cover; #105 GD, tape repairs; and #106 GD+. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 for group = $861. (Total: 18 Comic Books)

Wonder Woman #66 (DC, 1954). Wonder Woman's tiara talks to her in this bizarrely entertaining issue. This is an excellent high-grade copy with light wear at the top and bottom of spine, very light creasing on the right edge near the top, and moderate dinginess on the back cover. Cover image is still very crisp, with bright colors. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $90; NM 9.4 value = $240.

Marvel Mystery Comics #78 (Timely, 1946). A nice high-grade copy from the latter part of this classic book's run. Very slight damage to the top and bottom spine, a pen notation of "10-22-46" in the "L" on the front cover, and a pencilled "30" on the back cover are the only visible defects noticeable on this Golden Age gem. CGC VF 8.0 Cream to light tan pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $300; NM 9.4 value = $950.

Picture Stories from the Bible #4: Old Testament Edition Gaines File pedigree (DC / EC, 1943). Featuring the story of the Prophet Elijah being fed by ravens. This is a stellar high-grade copy of this Pre-Code EC classic. Excellent cover color, sharp corners, and few visible flaws. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $190.

Crime SuspenStories #15 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1953). This issue shows several bends at the lower spine on both front and back, but is otherwise quite attractive. The great Johnny Craig cover and an early appearance by the Old Witch make this a very desirable book. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $66; NM 9.4 value = $240.

Valor #4 Gaines File pedigree 12/12 (EC, 1955). A stunning example of this book with a beautiful royal blue cover depicting a battle scene by the legendary Wally Wood. Interior work by Reed Crandall and Al Williamson. Perfectly flat and very tight with razor sharp corners and pristine cover edges. Near flawless spine and fully fresh white interior paper, a rarity even for Gaines File pedigree copies. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $150.

Aces High #3 Gaines File pedigree 12/12 (EC, 1955). With a stunning George Evans bi-plane cover, this is a true gem from an impressive collection. Very slight spine stress is the only visible flaw in this stunning copy, which displays exceptional page quality. Don't miss this one. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $100.

Aces High #4 Gaines File pedigree 12/12 (EC, 1955). Exciting George Evans aviation cover. A superb book from an important collection. Great color and sharp corners highlight this EC gem. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $100.

X-Men Bronze Age Group (Marvel, 1979). Here is an outstanding lot of four classic issues of X-Men, all with outstanding John Byrne artwork and all graded an unbelievable CGC NM+ 9.6! You just don't see Bronze Age books looking this good very often, and here's a chance to pick up four beauties all for one bid! Lot includes: #119 Off-white to white pages; #120 Off-white to white pages; #121 Off-white pages; #124 White pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value for group = $225. (Total: 4 Comic Books)

Worlds Beyond #1 (Fawcett, 1951). First issue features "Stories of Weird Adventure" with artwork by Sheldon Moldoff, Bob Powell and Bernard Baily. Flat, clean and tight with good eye-appeal, clean cover edges and supple paper. Minor spine wear at staple stress. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 = $125; NM 9.4 value = $375.

Fox Romance Giants Group (Fox Features, 1949-50). A very scarce group of large square-bound Fox Giants includes (#1) 132-page Book of Love FN, featuring "My Story", "My Love Life" and "My Desire"; (#2) 132-page Teen-Age Love FN, featuring "My Secret Story", "My Life", "My Love Story", and "My Experience"; (#3) 132-page All Real Confessions FN+, featuring Zegra, Phantom Lady and "My Love Life"; (#4) 132-page Romantic Thrills FN, featuring "My True Love", "My Great Love", "My Story" and "My Love Memoirs"; and (#5) 132-page Secret Love Stories FN, featuring "My Love Life", "Thrilling Confessions", "My Love Memoirs" and "My Love Story". All these copies are very seldom seen in any condition. Overstreet 2001 value for group = $500. (Total: 5 Comic Books)

WonderWorld Comics #15 (Fox, 1940). Scarce early issue stars The Flame with back-up stories of Yarko the Great, Spark Stevens and Patty O'Day. Bondage cover art by Lou Fine. Full colors and good eye appeal with moderate wear at spine and cover edges. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $332; NM 9.4 value = $1,050.

The Sub-Mariner #1 (Marvel, 1968). Classic John Buscema cover. The first Silver Age solo issue of Prince Namor, the Sub-Mariner. This is an outstanding copy, with a perfect color strike, razor sharp corners, stunning page quality, and a like-new appearance. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $160.

Detective Comics #127 (DC, 1947). A miniaturized Batman and Robin spring into action in this fun-filled issue of Detective. The brilliant red background of this exciting cover has not lost a bit of its intensity over the years, and the book appears very fresh, with excellent color and clarity. Minor problems include light scuffing at the top and bottom of the spine, and a light abrasion just above the tip of Robin's cape. A really nice-looking book all around. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $205; NM 9.4 value = $650.

John Severin - Original Cover Art for Get Me Cracked Paperback (Mega Media, Inc., 1973). Amusing cover artwork to the Cracked paperback collection Get Me Cracked, painted by John Severin, features an excellent image of Sylvester the Janitor. The painting, which is in terrific condition, has been professionally framed, and measures approximately 14" x 20".

Spider-Man Bronze Age Group (Marvel, 1974-1980). An outstanding lot of '70s Spider-Man books, all in amazingly high grade! Bronze Age comics don't usually look this good, but this entire lot is NM+ or better! Included are: #132 CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages; #166 CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages; #167 CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages; #168 CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages; #175 CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages; #181 CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages; #182 CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages; #191 CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages; #192 CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages; #195 CGC NM/MT 9.8 Off-white to white pages; and #200 CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value for group = $126. (Total: 11 Comic Books)

How Stalin Hopes We Will Destroy America (nn) (Joe Lowe Co., 1951). Scarce promotional giveaway in top-grade with nice paper. Seldom seen in any grade. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

Ibis #3 (Fawcett, 1943). Early issue stars Ibis the Invincible and Taia with art by Mac Raboy and Basil Wolverton. Fully flat and very tight comic has full, deep colors and excellent eye-appeal. Tough to find in this high grade. CGC VF+ 8.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $675.

The Amazing Spider-Man #25 (Marvel, 1965). One of Steve Ditko's finest covers leads off this beautiful copy of ASM #25, as Spidey comes up against the Spider-Slayer, and is forced to stare at Jonah Jameson's ugly mug! A classic romp from Lee and Ditko, not to mention this is the very first appearance of Mary Jane Watson (in a cameo, her face doesn't show), and an early Norman Osborne appearance. This copy retains startlingly bright colors and clarity. Aside from very slight rounding of some corners, and a tiny 1/8" tear on the back cover, no other flaws are evident. They don't come much better than this! CGC NM- 9.2. Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001: FN 6.0 value = $87; NM 9.4 value = $320.

Turok #3 Circle 8 pedigree (Dell, 1956). Early issue has an alluring painted cover as Turok comes face-to-face with a Triceratops. Killer, nearly perfect copy has incredible eye-appeal and very supple paper. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $240.

Marvel Mystery Comics #79 (Timely, 1946). Scarce later issue has a Super-Villain cover by Syd Shores and has the last Angel appearance in this title. Flat and tight comic has superbly rich colors and very clean cover edges with no tears, chips, or creasing. CGC VF- 7.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $308; NM 9.4 value = $975.

Marvel Mystery Comics #88 (Timely, 1948). Scarce later issue has a bunch of cross-overs, including appearances by: Captain America, The Blonde Phantom, Golden Girl and Sun Girl and the last appearance of Toro in this title. Book has great colors with some wear at corners and top of spine. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $300; NM 9.4 value = $950.

Marvel Mystery Comics #92 (Timely, 1949). Scarce last issue of this famous title retells the origin of the Human Torch, and features a Captain America appearance and the first appearance of the mysterious Witness. Great color depth with general overall wear and aging. Back cover has some soiling, and the spine has some creasing. This is one of the most popular issues of the entire run. CGC VG 4.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $274; FN 6.0 value = $821.

Miss America Magazine #2 (Timely, 1944). First issue for this title features the first appearance of Patsy Walker and a very attractive photo cover. The first page has the origin of Miss America. Fresh and attractive, this copy has an off-white cover and very supple interior paper. Deep colors, clean cover edges and good eye appeal. The upper right corner has a 3/4" crease with minor spine wear. This book is quite difficult to find in any condition. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $375; NM 9.4 value = $1,190.

Picture Stories From American History #3 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1947). Outstanding copy of this highly educational comic. With striking clarity and uncommonly strong color, it appears as fresh as the day it was printed. Grade seems to derive from two very small chips at either end of the bottom edge, and one small stain on the back cover (which looks like printer's ink). Otherwise outstanding in every way. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $73; NM 9.4 value = $195.

Crime SuspenStories #21 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1954). Presenting "jolting tales of tension", this is a beautiful example of a high-grade book from an important collection. This copy displays very minor spine stress, and slightly rounded corners, but is overall a very attractive and desirable book. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 9.4 value = $135.

Crime SuspenStories #24 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1954). Excellent George Evans ax-murderer cover.
