Top and bottom cover corners are slightly creased, but there is very little to detract from the fabulous eye-appeal of this pedigreed book. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $135.

Amazing Spider-Man Bronze Age Group (Marvel, 1978-79). A terrific lot of super-high-grade Spider-Man books, all in CGC NM/MT 9.8, which includes: #183 Off-white to white pages; #189 Off-white to white pages; #190 White pages; and #195 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value for group = $38. (Total: 4 Comic Books)

Power Comics #2 (Holyoke Publications, 1944). Scarce second issue his a symbolic WW II cover as the Allies smash Hitler and Tojo. Superbly deep blues and rich blood-reds with some moderate wear at spine edge and exact printing cut (uncommon for this book). Very flat and clean copy with no chips, no tears and no creasing. CGC FN+ 6.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $442; NM 9.4 value = $1,400.

Strange Tales #114 (Marvel, 1963). The first Silver Age appearance of Captain America (actually the villainous Acrobat in disguise), pre-dating Avengers #4. Also the third appearance of Dr. Strange and the beginning of his long-running series. A key issue no matter how you slice it! A beautiful high-grade copy, with excellent color and a good cut. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $400.

Batman Group (DC, 1944-60). Group of Batman comics includes issues #21 FN, #31 VG, #130 FN, #131 FN-, #137 FN+ and #139 FN. Featuring the first "skinny" Alfred and a Penguin appearance (#21) and the first appearance of the original Bat-Girl (#139). Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $339; FN 6.0 value for group = $1,017. (Total: 6 Comic Books)

Star Spangled Comics #128 (DC, 1952). Very scarce late issue features the cover story "The Girl Who Lived 5,000 Years!" Stunning black cover with very deep colors and nice eye-appeal. Flat and tight with some minor stress at spine. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $240.

Superman Early Issues Group (DC, 1941-42). Four very early issues of Superman, all in FR, except as noted. #9; #11 (piece out); #14 (coupon clipped); and #15 (FR/GD). Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $1,011. (Total: 4 Comic Books)

X-Men #12 (Marvel, 1965). The Uncanny X-Men face one of their most indestructible foes, The Juggernaut! This key book also includes the origin of the X-Men's founder Professor X with cover art by Jack "King" Kirby. Very high-grade copy has eye-catching blood-red cover color inks and tremendous gloss. One of the finest copies we have seen. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $400.

Moon Girl #6 (EC, 1949). An excellent copy of a hard-to-find book. Good color, straight cut and sharp corners highlight this gem. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

Maxfield Parrish Playing Cards (Edison Mazda, 1918). We present the first of a complete collection of Edison Mazda Parrish decks, each containing 52 unused cards, plus jokers and any additional cards issued. We believe this collection to be the finest offered at public auction in at least a decade. "And Night is Fled" also known as "Dawn" is perhaps the rarest of the series. Condition of this deck is mint, still sealed in the original wax paper. The original box is mint and virtually perfect as well. Although the inner box is removable from the outer box, even the revenue stamp remains fortuitously unbroken.

Startling Comics #28 Okajima pedigree (Better Publications, 1944). Featuring a great Alex Schomburg Nazi cover, this copy is sharp and flat with few noticeable defects. Most notable is the writing on the top front cover, including the word "Okajima", "5-23-44", and "3R". Overall, a very solid book. CGC VF+ 8.5 White pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $142; NM 9.4 value = $425.

Captain America (Vol. 1) #109 (Marvel, 1969). Classic Jack Kirby cover, and the origin of Captain America, make this a key early Marvel issue. A stunning copy with great eye-appeal and outstanding page quality, it would be hard to find a nicer example of this Silver Age favorite. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $65.

Doctor Solar #1 Massachusetts (Gold Key, 1962). A true beauty featuring the origin and first appearance of Dr. Solar (the first original Gold Key character) with artwork by Bob Fujitani. Fully fresh, flat and clean book has superb colors and cover gloss with white paper. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $220.

Conan the Barbarian #1 (Marvel, 1970). Debut issue featuring the origin and first appearance of Robert E. Howard's Conan the Barbarian. This book also includes the first appearance of Kull with exceptional art by Barry Windsor-Smith. Killer copy in more ways than one as Conan cries, "To the Death" in the full battle cover scene. Beautiful, top-grade copy of this popular 70's comic has very fresh paper and great colors. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM value = $240.

X-Men Bronze Age Group (Marvel,1977-78). This is a lot of two consecutive issues of X-Men, the landmark issues where John Byrne took over the art chores from Dave Cockrum. Lot includes: #108 CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages, Byrne's debut, and #109 CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages, the first appearance of Weapon Alpha. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 values for group = $160. (Total: 2 Comic Books)

Suspense Comics #11 (Continental Magazines, 1946). A classic L.B. Cole Devil cover highlights this desirable comic. There is some chipping along the edges, as well as some long creasing at the bottom right front cover. The spine shows some wear and a bit of roll, and there is the notation "P7" written in pencil at the top front cover. A quarter-sized stain shows on the back cover, as well as some general soiling. Overall, very attractive for its grade. CGC VG+ 4.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $314; FN 6.0 value = $943.

Lone Ranger Premium #5 (Gum, Inc., 1940). Picture card premium #5 depicts a great painted image of The Lone Ranger in action thwarting the plans of an outlaw while faithful Silver stands regally by. Picture is a nice VF+ with just the slightest hint of wear at corners and is well centered. Condition: VF+.

X-Men Silver Age Group (Marvel, 1965-67). Three high-grade books include issues #12 CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages with some tiny writing on back cover in pen; #17 CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages; and #38 CGC VF/NM 9.0 White pages. Issue #12 features the first appearance of the powerful Juggernaut and the origin of Professor X. Issue #38 begins the origins of the X-Men back-up series. All are tight, flat and glossy books with nice pages and excellent eye-appeal. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value for group = $670. (Total: 3 Comic Books)

Young Men #25 (Atlas, 1954). With the end of World War II, Captain America, the Human Torch, and the Sub-Mariner were pretty much out of a job. Relegated to publishing limbo by 1949, these three Timely stalwarts were granted one last chance at life to battle the growing Communist Menace. Notable for the quality of the artwork, this issue showcases Bill Everett, in what many consider his finest creative period, on Sub-Mariner, and the early efforts of John Romita, Sr. on Captain America. This copy displays a modicum of spine stress and some minor creasing at the lower corner of the cover with a smudged store stamp and slight discoloration on the back cover. Still and all, a very attractive copy of a hard-to-find book with a classic Human Torch cover. CGC FN+ 6.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $316.

Valor #3 Gaines File pedigree 12/12 (EC, 1955). Beautiful Joe Orlando cover. A slight crease at the lower right back cover is the only significant flaw in this stunning copy from the personal files of William Gaines. Bright crimson cover and razor sharp corners make this a real gem. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $150.

Fox Romance Comic Group (Fox Features, 1949-50). Group of seven scarce comic books includes: My Confession #7 VG; #8 FN-; #9 FN (all have Wally Wood art); #10 VG; My Secret Story #26 VG+; #27 FN-; and My Experience #19 VG (Wally Wood art). Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $119 FN 6.0 value for group = $358. (Total: 7 Comic Books)

My Life Group (Fox Features, 1948-50). Solid group of Romance comics includes issues #5 FN-; #6, 7 VG+; #10 VG; and #15 FN. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $92; FN 6.0 value for group = $276. (Total: 5 Comic Books)

Jack In the Box #11 (Charlton, 1946). This uncommon and unknown issue has a Big Bad Wolf about to chop off the head of a bound little piggy. A quite odd scene, especially for a Funny Animal comic. This issue also has a Yellow-Jacket story. An attractive high-grade flat and tight copy, there is light overall wear on the back cover and minor corner blunting. We have never seen another copy of this book. CGC VF- 7.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $56; NM 9.4 value = $150.

Tales to Astonish #27 (Marvel, 1962). The first appearance of Henry Pym (before he became Ant-Man) as "The Man in the Ant Hill!" A key Silver Age comic, this book shows some spine wear and very slight "Marvel chipping" along the edges, as well as a small crease along the back cover at the spine. CGC notes that the centerfold is detached. Otherwise clean and sharp. CGC VG/FN 5.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet GD 2.0 value = $263; FN 6.0 value = $788.

X-Men #17 (Marvel, 1966). This book has a striking deep blood-red cover, great gloss, no "Marvel chipping" and superb overall eye-appeal. Perfectly flat and very tight with a sharp spine and very fresh white paper. Rare in this top grade. CGC NM 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $180.

All-Star Comics #5 (DC, 1941). Key book features the first appearance of Hawkgirl (the first costumed super-heroine). Solid low-grade copy of this early issue has good eye-appeal and very rich colors. It also has a cover that is nearly detached (but not quite) from the pages. Complete and flat with supple off-white pages. Condition: GD/VG. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $435.

Artist Unknown - Original Art for "The Avenging Ghost" from Journey Into Fear #20 - Complete 6-Page Story (Superior, 1954). This Pre-Code goodie is a gruesome tale of murder and revenge from the legendary Iger Studios. A cold-blooded killer is sentenced to death by hanging, but manages to return from the grave to take his revenge on those he feels wronged him! Dynamic, evocative art measures approximately 12.75" x 20.5" and is in excellent condition with minimal use of white out. Pre-code horror art is incredibly hot right now. Don't miss out on your chance to own a great example! Only the first page is shown here. For the complete story, please go to www.HeritageComics.com.

The Human Torch #29 (Timely, 1947). Later issue has a Syd Shores Human Torch cover and a back-up Sub-Mariner story. Flat and tight book has excellent colors with some wear along spine edge at staple areas. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $316; NM 9.4 value = $1,000.

Picture Stories from the Bible #1: New Testament Edition Gaines File pedigree (EC, 1946). A slight crease on the back cover keeps this copy from a higher grade. Otherwise flat, bright and clean, this is a beautiful copy of a hard-to-find book. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $140.

Picture Stories From American History #1 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1946). First issue of this title depicts the period of discovery and exploration of the new world. Good-looking flat and clean comic has full color inks, very clean edges and sharp corners. There is minor stress at the staples with no other defects of note. Very scarce in the higher grade. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $240.

The Haunt of Fear #23 Gaines File pedigree 8/12 (EC, 1954). An outstanding cover from "Ghastly" Graham Ingels highlights this classic EC horror comic, so gruesome it was singled out by Frederick Wertham in Seduction of the Innocent. Even better, this is the #8 (of 12) copy from the Gaines File, making it one of the best known examples of this particular Haunt. Noticeable flaws include a crease in the lower left corner, and some light spine stress. CGC VF+ 8.5 White pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 values = $57; NM 9.4 value = $210.

Two-Fisted Tales #31 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1953). Special Civil War issue with a great Harvey Kurtzman cover. Some minor damage at the top and bottom spine, slightly rounded corners and some color rubbing along the edge cannot reduce the exceptional eye-appeal of this book. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $175.

Crime SuspenStories #25 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1954). Presenting "Jolting Tales of Tension in the EC Tradition", this book sports a great Jack Kamen cover. Deep colors, sharp corners and an overall excellent appearance make this an outstanding book from an important collection. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $135.

Crime SuspenStories #26 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1955). Classic Kamen subway cover. Two creases to the bottom left front and back cover corner keep this from a higher grade. High eye-appeal and very attractive. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $135.

Crime SuspenStories #27 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1955). Shocking Kamen cover will make you swear off libraries forever! Slight creasing at the top front cover and very minor spine wear. Otherwise very attractive and appealing. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $135.

Realistic Romances Group (Avon, 1951-52). A run that includes issues #1 FN+; #2 VG; #3, 5, 6 FN; and #7, 8 VG. Features artwork by George Evans (#7) and Everett Raymond Kinstler (#1, 6 & 8). Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $80; FN 6.0 value for group = $242. (Total: 7 Comic Books)

White Princess of the Jungle #3 Bethlehem pedigree (Avon, 1952). Cool attacking-natives/ bondage cover as Taanda rushes in to save a bound prisoner on this cover by Bill Everett and Everett Raymond Kinstler. Beautiful high-grade book has bright colors, good gloss and supple paper. CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $290.

Captain Easy #15 Mile High pedigree (Standard, 1949). An impressive copy from a classic pedigree. Aside from the high grade, this comic also features a double cover (outer cover NM- 9.2, interior cover NM+ 9.6). Very desirable. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $65.

Fox Giant Group(Fox, 1950). Two very attractive and rare 132 page Fox square-bound Giants. This lot includes: All Great Jungle Adventure, featuring Rulah, the Jungle Goddess, Jo Jo, Congo King and the Blue Beetle with art by Matt Baker. This is a very glossy,attractive book with great colors and very supple off-white pages. The overall appearance is VF with the paper being separated from the inside spine near the top of the book. So, lets give this copy a strong FN+. Also included is Variety Comics FN+, with adventures of Jungle Lil, the Blue Beetle and My Private Life. We've never seen another copy of either of these comics. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $253. (Total: 2 Comic Books)

The Amazing Spider-Man #24 (Marvel, 1965). Spider-Man "Goes Mad" in this issue with cover and story art by Steve Ditko. Top-grade copy of this book has sharp, bright color inks, excellent gloss and supple pages. Slight wear at corners and top of spine. Tough to find this nice. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $260.

Captain America #100 (Marvel, 1968). The American icon and greatest defender of freedom and liberty rushes forward to lead the mighty Avengers into battle on the cover of his very first Silver Age title issue. "Big Premiere Issue" stars Captain America with classic cover and story art by his co-creator Jack "King" Kirby. Beautiful comic has color inks and gloss so intense that they look as if they could spill right off the cover itself. Perfectly flat, clean and tight with no chips, tears or creasing. This copy is even nicer than its grade would indicate. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $400.

John Cullen Murphy - Original Art for Mad #134 - Back cover (EC, 1970). Entitled "A TV Scene We'd Like To See (Mission Impossible)", this page has the distinction of being the only piece Chevy Chase ever wrote forMad. Fully painted by John Cullen Murphy (better known as Hal Foster's hand-picked successor on Prince Valiant), this piece measures 18.5" x 14.75" and is in excellent condition. The individual panels have been painted and pasted onto artboard, then a mylar sheet has been overlaid to give a framing effect.

Robert Grossman - Original Cover Art for Utterly Mad Paperback. Beautiful airbrushed "yo-yo" cover by Grossman, who illustrated several paperback Mad covers. A pleasant depiction of everyone's favorite "What - Me Worry?" kid. The art, which has been tastefully framed, measures approximately 9" x 12", and is in excellent condition.

Valor #5 Gaines File pedigree 12/12 (EC, 1955). The last issue of this exciting series. This copy shows deep cover colors, sharp corners, and a clean cut. Only minimal spine stress keeps this from a higher grade. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $140.

Maxfield Parrish Playing Cards (Edison Mazda, 1919). The second of a complete collection of Edison Mazda Parrish decks, each containing 52 unused cards, plus jokers and any additional cards issued. "Spirit of the Night" was painted with Sue Lewin, Parrish's muse, posing as the title character. It is undoubtedly the most coveted Edison Mazda image, and a small "Spirit of the Night" Edison Calendar recently fetched five figures at auction. Condition of the deck is mint, with the entire deck still sealed in the original wax paper. Box is mint and virtually perfect as well, with the revenue stamp torn in half but complete.

Jungle Comics Group (Fiction House, 1944-50). Attractive six book group of Jungle Comics includes issues #49 CGC VF 8.0 Cream pages, "1" tear on 3 pages" with faded cover colors; #68 CGC FN+ 6.5 Cream to off-white pages; #78 CGC VG/FN 5.0, "small piece of tape on cover. Name stamp on 1st page" with a cool female bondage cover; #90 CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white pages, "mis-cut" with female bondage cover; #105 CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages; and #127 CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $371; NM 9.4 value for group = $985. (Total: 6 Comic Books)

Three Stooges #1 (Jubilee Publications, 1949). Scarce debut issue has great colors with exact centering, clean cover edges and good eye appeal. This is one of the only copies of this issue that we have seen. CGC FN+ 6.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $332; NM 9.4 value = $1,050.

The Avengers #7 (Marvel, 1964). The Avengers take on the Enchantress, the Executioner and Baron Zemo in "Their Darkest Hour!" Art by Jack "King" Kirby. Great-looking comic with beautiful blue inks and very clean edges (no "Marvel chipping"). Flat and tight with no creasing and no tears. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $215.

Iron Man #1 (Marvel, 1968). Big premiere issue stars the Invincible Iron Man in his own title with story continued from Iron Man and Sub-Mariner #1. Excellent cover and interior art by Gene Colan. Killer, near perfect copy has striking purple cover and very supple pages. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $475.

X-Men #56 Winnipeg pedigree (Marvel, 1969). The Winnipeg copy of this pivotal X-book is marred only by a date stamp in the title portion of the cover, and "10-15-5" written on the first page in pen. Otherwise, this book is sharp and clean as the day it was published. Brilliant colors and an exact cut combine to make this high-grade book a real gem. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $75.

Sensation Comics Group (DC, 1950-51). A solid group of scarce later issues of this title includes: #95, #99, #100, #103, #104, and #105. All are in VG condition and star Wonder Woman. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $265; FN 6.0 value for group = $790. (Total: 6 Comic Books)

Young Allies #2 (Timely, 1941). Second issue has a great action-bondage cover by Joe Simon and Jack Kirby. Attractive mid-grade book has bright, rich color strike and nice off-white pages. Cover has been cleaned and pressed with spine having very slight color touch. Condition: Apparent FN. Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $915.

Jack Kirby - Original Art for Amazing Adventures #6, page 9 (Marvel, 1961). An excellent page from the glory years of Jack Kirby monster books, this features army guys dropping a hydrogen bomb (!) on a big scaly monster called Sserpo! Does it finish him off? If you know Kirby monsters, you know the answer to that. Vintage large art, measuring approximately 13 1/2" x 21", with the only white-out being in the caption in panel one. Signed by Kirby in pencil in the lower right corner.

Superman #2 (DC, 1939). The second issue of this iconic title has scripts by Jerry Siegel and artwork by Joe Shuster. This copy displays amateur restoration including spine reinforcement, a panel replaced with a color photocopy on page 16, and trimming. Condition: FR. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $1,020.

Superman Early Low-Grade Group (DC, 1941-44). Lot of five early Superman issues, all in low grade. Includes: #9 PR/FR, spine completely split, all pages loose; #18 FR, trimmed page replaces a missing page; #19 FR/GD, tape repairs; #20 FR/GD, trimmed back cover; and #27 FR/GD. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $904. (Total: 5 Comic Books)

Alex Ross - Original Pencil Prelim for Avengers #4 Cover Recreation (1999). Very cool tight pencil prelim by the legendary Alex Ross, a recreation of the Jack Kirby cover to Avengers #4, which was used as one of two alternate covers for the 1999 Overstreet Comic Book Price Guide. Features a bold image of Captain America leading the Avengers on a charge. Approximately 7" x 9" on thin art paper, with an image area of approximately 3 1/2" x 5". Signed by the artist in the lower right.

Marvel Mystery Comics #87 (Timely, 1948). Solid mid-grade comic has the first Captain America/Golden Girl team-up with a Syd Shores cover. Minor wear at top corner of spine and corners. Label note reads, "Stains on interior cover." CGC FN+ 6.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $316; NM 9.4 value = $1,000.

Bob Clarke - Original Art Cover Recreation of Mad #1. Bob Clarke was an incredibly prolificMad artist for many years. He was especially valued for his ability to mimic the styles of earlier artists, such as Wood, Elder, or Kurtzman. Here, Clarke recreates the classic cover to America's first satire comic,Mad #1. Rendered in pen and ink, the image area is approximately 14" x 20", and is matted and framed. In excellent condition, this is an amazingly faithful recreation of Kurtzman's original.From the Grant Geissman Collection.

Crime SuspenStories #13 (EC, 1952). Classic Johnny Craig cover. Lizzie Borden takes center stage in this exceptional high-grade copy. Very slight bend at the upper left corner and slight tanning on the back cover are the only visible flaws. Very attractive and desirable. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $275.

Extra! #1 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1955). First issue of this classic "New Direction" title, not approved by the Comics Code. This copy shows a little dust shadowing along the top front cover, but is otherwise tight, flat and clean. An exceptional book. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 9.4 value = $160.

Maxfield Parrish Playing Cards (Edison Mazda, 1922). Presented here, "Egypt" is another rare and underrated deck. Condition of the deck is mint, still sealed in the original wax paper. Box is excellent, but has a few tiny tatters and the stamp is incomplete.

This Magazine Is Haunted Group (Fawcett, 1952). Solid group of comic books from this classic horror title includes (Vol. 1) #17 VG-; #21 FN; (Vol. 2) #12 FN; #14 VG; #15 VG+; and #16 VG. All have Steve Ditko cover and interior art except for #15. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $192; FN 6.0 value for group = $577. (Total: 6 Comic Books)

Fox Giants Group (Fox, 1949). This group of scarce Fox Giant comics includes Album of Love FN-; Love Thrills VG; Revealing Love Stories VG; and Throbbing Love VG. These hard-to-find books are seldom seen in any condition at all. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $154; FN 6.0 value for group = $461. (Total: 4 Comic Books)

Romance Comics Group (St. John, 1949). Group includes Giant Comics Edition #7 FN; #13 FN (Matt Baker and Joe Kubert art); and All-Picture All-True Love Story #1 FN (Canteen Kate by Matt Baker). Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $348 NM 9.4 value for group = $1,185. (Total: 3 Comic Books)

Weird Tales of the Future #7 (Aragon, 1953). Classic hell cover (by Bernard Bailey) as the damned emerge from the devil's mouth, with interior highlighted with art by Basil Wolverton. Glossy book with good colors and gloss. Most of the wear is at spine area with staple stress, dings and parallel creasing. This book is one of the most popular and sought-after of all horror comics in the market. CGC FN- 5.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $300: NM 9.4 value = $950.

Peter Pan Treasure Chest #1 (Dell, 1952). Very large 212 page square-bound comic includes a movie adaptation and other Pan stories. Mickey Mouse and Donald Duck make cross-over appearances in the Pan stories as well. Great black cover displays full images of Peter Pan and Tinker Bell. Believed to be the toughest of all Dell Giants, this copy has striking black color inks with beautiful shades of green and bright yellows. Some wear at spine and cover corners and edges. Supple off-white paper. Condition: VG/FN Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $255.

Red Dragon Group (Street & Smith, 1944-49). This group includes issue #6 (2nd series) CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white pages; and #9 CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white pages. Issue #9 includes the origin of Chuck Magnon and Immortal Man. Overstreet 2001 value for group = $630 (Total: 2 Comic Books)

Action Comics #30 (DC, 1940). Nice early issue stars Superman with Joe Schuster cover and story artwork. Flat and tight comic has good color and supple off-white to light cream pages. Completely unrestored. Condition: VG+. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $221; FN 6.0 value = $663.

Superman #19 (DC, 1942). An early issue of an American icon. A great Jack Burnley cover shows the Man of Steel helping a family in distress. The defects in this book are not terribly pronounced, with some cover soiling and several small tears and a very small chip on the back cover, along with some very small, very light pencil notations by the "S" on the front cover and a moderate amount of spine wear. A nice addition to any collection. CGC FN 6.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $505.

George Perez and Sam Grainger - Original Art for Avengers #143, page 22 (Marvel, 1976). A rare example of the early Marvel art of the great George Perez. Strong and dynamic, this page features Thor and Kang in all-out battle! Perez has been a fan-favorite artist since his earliest days, and this page certainly demonstrates the power that he brings to his work. Bronze Age Marvel pages are red-hot right now, and this is one of the finest examples from that period. 10.75" x 16.25", signed by Perez at the top center.

Sensation Comics Group (DC, 1949-51). Solid mid- to high-grade group includes: #93 CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white pages (good colors, with spine wear and oxidation on back cover) and #101 CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white to white pages (attractive black cover with spine dings and wear at top of spine). Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $228; NM 9.4 value for group = $650. (Total: 2 Comic Books)

This Magazine Is Haunted #4 Aurora pedigree (Fawcett, 1952). Nice glossy copy of this early horror comic book features art by Sheldon Moldoff, Bob Powell and Bernard Bailey. Flat, clean and supple with just a small chip at top area of front cover and light stress at top edge. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF- 7.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $98; NM 9.4 value = $260.

The Amazing Spider-Man #35 (Marvel, 1966). Beautiful condition early Spidey with deep colors, excellent cut, sharp corners and fabulous eye-appeal. A nicer copy of this book would be hard to find. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

Sgt. Fury #13 (Marvel, 1964). Key issue boldly displays on its front cover why this issue is so popular. Classic Captain America and Bucky cross-over with artwork by Jack "King" Kirby and Dick Ayers. Very nice looking copy has wonderful colors and cover gloss, and very fresh paper. Flat, clean and tight with a minor impact dent at top corner of spine. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $360.

Star Spangled Comics #45 (DC, 1945). High-grade copy of this issue stars the Guardian and Newsboy Legion and has a nice black cover and rich color strike. Very flat, clean and tight book has great eye-appeal and secure staples. Label note reads, "1" tear at centerfold". CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $475.

Secret Hearts Group (DC, 1949-57). 13 scarce issues from this popular romance title include issues #1 FN-; #2 VG; #4 FN; #5 VG+; #6-9 VG; #10 FN-; #20 and 38 VG; #41 FN-; and #43 VG. All are very tough comics to find. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $277; FN 6.0 value for group = $828. (Total: 13 Comic Books)

Plastic Man #10 (Quality, 1948). Early issue has a clever Football cover by Jack Cole as Plastic Man "Lines Up Against Crime!" Flat, clean and very supple copy has a nice green cover, clean edges and perfect centering. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $550.

Romance Group (St. John, 1949-51). Three scarce, large square-bound romance books, including: 128-page Giant Comics Editions #9 FN-, with romance and confession stories and art by Joe Kubert and Matt Baker; 128-page Giant Comics Editions "Romances" FN-, with Baker cover art; and 100 page Pictorial Romances #19 FN, with Baker cover art. These issues have proven very difficult to find. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $383. (Total: 3 Comic Books)

The Unseen Group Bethlehem pedigree (Standard, 1952). Attractive group of books from this horror title includes issues #5 (#1) FN, #6 VG, #8 FN,and #9 VF-. All have horror covers and stories with superb color inks, good gloss and supple off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $356; NM 9.4 value = $960. (Total: 4 Comic Books)

The Amazing Spider-Man #29 (Marvel, 1965). Another Ditko classic, as Spidey tangles with his arch-foe, the Scorpion! A clean, flat book with good color and only minor spine wear, this is an excellent high-grade example of this Silver Age classic. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

Mystery in Space Group (DC, 1958-59). Here's a terrific three issue lot of Mystery In Space. Included are: #49 (Gil Kane front cover) CGC FN+ 6.5 Off-white pages; #54 (Bethlehem pedigree) CGC FN/VF 7.0 Tan to off-white pages; and #55 (Bethlehem pedigree) graytone cover, CGC VF- 7.5 Light tan to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $278. (Total: 3 Comic Books)

Superman #3 (DC, 1940). An early issue which stars the Greatest Superhero of all with re-print stories from Action Comics #5 & #6. This low-grade book, which has good colors, shows some amateur restoration, including spine reinforcement. Condition: FR. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $697.

Tales From the Crypt #22 (EC, 1951). Creepy rotting corpse cover by Al Feldstein. Nice copy with slight soiling on the back cover and slightly rounded corners. A very collectible copy. CGC VF+ 8.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $200; NM 9.4 value = $735.

The Amazing Spider-Man #98 (Marvel, 1971). This is the conclusion of the famous drug-themed story that was published without the seal of the Comics Code Authority, as Spidey once again faces off against the malevolent Green Goblin. Gil Kane contributes one of his best covers, a classic image of the Web-Slinger having lost his powers. This is another "amazing" like-new copy, with gorgeous cover color and a crisp, fresh appearance. The only visible flaw is some very, very slight wrinkling in the upper left corner of the front cover. CGC NM 9.4 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $110.

Marvel Bronze Age Group (Marvel, 1974). Two important "firsts" from the Marvel Bronze Age. First up is Dr. Strange #1 CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages, the premiere issue of the legendary Brunner run on this groundbreaking book. Clean and flat, with great color and sharp corners, the eye appeal on this book cannot be overstated. Includes the "Mark Jewelers" insert. Second is Marvel Premiere #15 CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages, the origin and first appearance of Iron Fist, with artwork by Gil Kane and Dick Giordano. Another like-new book with amazing color and crispness. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value for group = $105. (Total: 2 Comic Books)

Thrilling Comics #69 (Nedor Publications, 1948). This exceptional high-grade copy features the "Exciting Adventures of Princess Pantha" and sports a beautiful "Good-Girl" airbrush cover by Alex "Xela" Schomburg and dynamic interior work by Frank Frazetta. Very attractive flat, clean and tight copy has superb colors and light stress at the top corner of the spine and a tiny 1/4" tear at the bottom edge of the back cover. CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

This Magazine Is Haunted #5 Aurora pedigree (Fawcett, 1952). Host Doctor Death oversees "The Slithering Horror of Skontong Swamp" with art by Sheldon Moldoff, Bob Powell and George Evans. Glossy supple book has good eye appeal with some minor wear at spine edge and a few water stains on back cover. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $125; NM 9.4 value = $375.

Love Letters Group (Quality, 1949). Solid run of Love Letters includes issues #1 FN; #2 VG; #5 VG; #6 VG+; #7 FN+; #8 VG/FN; #9 VG; #11, 12 VG/FN; #14 VG; #15 VG/FN; #17 FN+; #19 VF-; #20 & 22 FN-; #25 VG/FN; #27 VG; #28 VG/FN; #29 FN; #31 FN+; #36 VF-; and #41 FN. Key issues include: Bill Ward cover art (#1, 2) and interior art (#9, 11, 17, 31), Paul Gustavson art (#1, 2) and a Jane Russell photo cover (#17). Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $155; NM 9.4 value for group = $463. (Total: 22 Comic Books)

The Amazing Spider-Man #30 (Marvel, 1965). Spidey stars in "The Claws of the Cat" with cover interior art by Steve Ditko. Good-looking copy with deep, dark color strike, fresh white paper, no "Marvel chipping" and just the most minor wear at top and bottom of spine and at top right corner of front cover. Beautiful! CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

Iron Man and Sub-Mariner #1 (Marvel, 1968). Special Once-in-a-Lifetime Issue! First and only issue stars two of Marvel Comics' most successful characters. This comic would "bridge" the gap between Tales of Suspense #99 and Iron Man #1, and Tales to Astonish #101 and Sub-Mariner #1. Super-killer copy with incredible color inks and gloss and fully fresh white pages. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $150.

Tales to Astonish Group (Marvel, 1964). Two high-grade issues starring Giant Man and the Wonderful Wasp with a Spider-Man cross-over (#57). Lot includes #52 CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white pages; and #57 CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value for group = $285. (Total: 2 Comic Books)

X-Men #26 (Marvel, 1966). The X-Men star in "Holocaust!" Superb book with beautiful spine, sharp, rich color strike and full gloss. Exceptional copy! CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $125.

X-Men #27 (Marvel, 1966). Marvel's Mightiest Mutants battle the Mimic in this issue with art by Dick Ayers. Perfectly flat, clean and very tight with exact centering, sharp corners and fresh paper. Killer! CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $125.

More Fun Comics #70 (DC, 1941). Dr. Fate cover with a Spectre back-up story. Solid low-grade book has general overall wear with no large pieces out, no long creases or tears. Completely unrestored with good eye appeal, vivid colors and off-white paper. Condition: VG-. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $226; FN 6.0 value = $679.

Mystic #3 (Atlas, 1951). Nice-looking book has excellent colors and gloss with no chipping, creasing or tears. Flat and supple with some minor spine stress. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $125; NM 9.4 value = $375.

John Byrne - Original art for Marvel Team-Up #54, page 26 (Marvel, 1977). From what many consider to be John Byrne's greatest period in comics comes this page from a classic Marvel title. Featuring Ol' Greenskin in five out of seven panels, this is a magnificent page for any Hulk collector, as the Incredible One stands up to major punishment and just gets madder and madder, finally flattening everything in sight. As an added bonus, Mr. Byrne has added a very lightly pencilled head shot of the Thing on the reverse. Measuring approximately 10.5" x 15.75", there is some white-out in panel three and some blue-pencil throughout, especially in panel seven. Overall, an attractive, desirable Bronze Age gem.

Fox Romance Group (Fox Features, 1949-50). 11 scarce romance comics, including My Great Love #1 VG+; My Intimate Affair #1 FN; My Past #11 FN-, Wood art; My Story #5 FN, Al Feldstein and Jack Kamen art; #7 FN; #9 VG+, Wally Wood art; #12 FN; My Love Story #1 FN; #3 FN-; and Women in Love #1 VG/FN, #2 FN, with art by Jack Kamen and Al Feldstein. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $562. (Total: 11 Comic Books)

Moe Marcus and Rocco Mastroserrio - Original Art for "Hand of Fate" from Tales of Horror #5 - Complete 7-Page Story (Toby Publishing, 1953). A fabulous example of Pre-Code Horror art. This is a great vampire/werewolf story from a period that is becoming more and more collectible. Measuring approximately 15" x 22", this art is in excellent condition. The logo on the first page is a stat, but all other art is original. The use of white-out and blue pencil is minimal, and mostly unobtrusive. A great late-Golden Age find! Only the first page is shown here. To see the complete story, please visit www.HeritageComics.com.

Lone Ranger Premium #1 (Gum, Inc., 1940). The first in a total of five 8" X 10" premiums on heavy paper stock in full color. These wonderfully printed collectible pictures were obtained by sending in wrappers and coins found with Lone Ranger Gum to the manufacturer. Usually found with a bio and ordering instructions on reverse, some came with black backs. Picture #1 depicts Tonto carrying an injured Texas Ranger who, after his recovery, becomes the most famous masked lawman in the history of the Wild West, The Lone Ranger. Picture card appears VF with some very minor fraying at the corners. Bold and fresh colors with excellent centering. These premiums are quite scarce, especially in high-grade. Condition: VF-.

Nick Fury, Agent of S.H.I.E.L.D. #1 (Marvel, 1968). First issue stars the Super-Agent Nick Fury with classic cover and story art by the talented Jim Steranko. Incredible super-high-grade copy has alluring rich blue cover inks, mirror-like gloss and full fresh white paper. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $110.

Leading Comics #6 Crowley Pedigree (DC, 1943). Early issue has a bright yellow cover with some staple stress, spine wear and minor corner blunting. Good overall eye appeal and centering. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $237.

Sub-Mariner Comics #42 (Atlas, 1955). The Sub-Mariner "Fights Commies and Crooks" on the cover and interior of this last issue with nice cover art by Sal Brodsky and great interior work by the legendary Bill Everett. Intense blood-red color inks with good gloss and overall eye appeal. A ver scarce issue, this is of the more popular covers of the later run. CGC FN+ 6.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $253; NM 9.4 value = $800.

Forbidden Love Group (Quality, 1950). Three scarce issues: #1 FN; #3 VG+; and #4 FN+. All have photo covers with artwork by Bill Ward (#4) and Reed Crandall (#1). Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $453. (Total: 3 Comic Books)

Chamber of Chills #19 (Harvey, 1953). Tales of Terror and Suspense with an eye-catching black cover and great colors and gloss. Spine has a few light dings while cover edges are sharp with no chips, tears or creasing. CGC VF+ 8.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $200.

Crime Does Not Pay #43 (Lev Gleason, 1946). High-grade copy of this issue has a classic execution cover by Charles Biro. Good-looking high-grade book has sharp, rich colors and good overall eye appeal. Tough to find in the higher grades. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $53; NM 9.4 value = $140.

Superman #5 (DC, 1940). The fifth issue of this historic title has a classic cover (by Joe Shuster) with Superman tearing through a brick wall to save a female from an attacker. This book, which has good colors, shows a detached cover and tape on the spine. Condition: GD. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $300-400.

Atlas War Group Bethlehem pedigree (Atlas, 1952-57). A very good-looking group of Atlas War comics includes Battle Action #7 VF-; Battle Cry #1 FN+; Battlefield #1 FN+; Commando Adventures #1 VF; and Man Comics #20 VF. Excellent color inks and cover gloss with no chipping and very supple off-white to light cream paper. These issues are very tough to find in high grade. Overstreet 2001 FN 2.0 value for group = $164; NM 9.4 value for group = $413. (Total: 5 Comic Books)

Moon Girl #1 (EC, 1947). The first issue of EC's only foray into the realm of the Superhero. A hard book to find in any grade CGC notes that this copy has a 2" tear on the 17th page and a tiny hole on 5 pages. We also noticed a faint 2" crease along the bottom right corner, but this is a very impressive copy overall. CGC FN+ 6.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $268; NM 9.4 value = $850.

Impact #1 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1955). This landmark "New Direction" title includes the classic story "Master Race" by Bernard Krigstein. A lovely copy, with some slight soiling on the cover and some minor spine stress, this is an excellent book for any high-grade collection. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $150.

This Magazine Is Haunted #10 (Fawcett, 1953). Classic "Severed Head in the Bog" cover. Full deep blue color inks and good gloss with spine wear and a 1 1/2" crease at the lower right corner of the front cover. A very popular book amongst avid horror collectors. CGC FN+ 6.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $113.

Blue Beetle #59 Mile High pedigree (Feature, 1950). A scarce later issue, this copy has minor blunting at the corners and a slight miscut. CGC notes "very minor amount of glue on cover." Overall very attractive. CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $49; NM 9.4 value = $130.

Famous Stars Group (Ziff-Davis, 1950). Complete run of this title featuring the "Exciting Life Stories of Famous Stars" includes issues #1 FN (Jimmy Stewart); #2-3 FN; #4 FN (Robert Mitchum); #5 VG (Liz Taylor); and #6 FN-. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $420. (Total: 6 Comic Books)

Bob Clarke - Original Art for "Karate" from Mad #65 - Complete 5-Page Story (EC, 1961). Signed by Bob Clarke, this is an hilarious send-up of a popular martial art. Unusual forMAD art from this period, some of the dialogue is actually lettered on the board. The rest would have been done on a separate overlay, which is not included. The art, which is in excellent condition, measures approximately 17.5" x 24.5", and is rendered on Craftint Doubletone Drawing Board. Only the first page is shown here. To see the complete story, please visit www.HeritageComics.com.

Superman #290-423 Group (DC, 1975-86). A terrific run of mostly newsstand-fresh comics with many featuring cover art by Neal Adams. There may be a few VFs in the mix, but the dominant grade is NM. Overstreet 2001 value for group = $520. (Total: 133 Comic Books)

Romance Group (Quality, 1950). A solid group of romance books includes Untamed Love #1 FN/VF; #2 VG; #3 VG; #4 VG/FN; and #5 VG; Flaming Love #1 FN+. Some issues feature cover and interior art by Bill Ward (#1's) and Paul Gustavson (#1 & #5). Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $127; FN 6.0 value for group = $380. (Total: 6 Comic Books)

The Amazing Spider-Man #34 (Marvel, 1966). Spider-Man vs. Kraven the Hunter with classic art by Steve Ditko. Superb copy is nearly flawless with very sharp spine, no staple stress at all and just the slightest hint of stress at upper edge of back cover. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

Action Comics #107 (DC, 1947). The Man of Steel bursts through yet another wall on the vibrant cover of this thrill-packed issue, featuring outstanding artwork by Jack Burnley. This is a beautiful mid-grade copy with outstanding clarity and deep, rich cover color. Apparent flaws include a very small chip at the base of the spine and light wear along the bottom edge of the front cover. Cover is slightly off-register, and staples seem to be pulling slightly. "12.00" is written in pencil at the top of the back cover. Very light spine stress. Overall, an outstanding book with tremendous eye-appeal. CGC VF- 7.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $182; NM 9.4 value = $575.

Girls' Love Stories Group (DC, 1949-54). This group includes #1 FN (Alex Toth and Everett Raymond Kinstler art); #2 VG; #3 VF; #4 VG; #9 FN-; #10 VG; #11 FN; #12 VG/FN; #14 FN-; #15 VG; and #29 FN. Scarce DC Romance issues! Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $242; FN 6.0 value for group = $734. (Total: 11 Comic Books)

Paul Chadwick - Original Cover Art for Concrete #7 (Dark Horse, 1988). Very appealing image of Concrete restrained in his cinderblock chair (with dog napping at his feet) while his mysterious "antlers" grow out of control. Chadwick is a superb craftsman with a lush inking style and a keen sense of composition (notice the shadowy figures of supporting characters Maureen and Larry off to the right), and this outstanding cover shows his talents full-force. Approximately 14" x 19" on heavy illustration board; moderate white-out (especially on the dog's back and some of the piping) but a very clean-looking image overall.

Maxfield Parrish Playing Cards (Edison Mazda, 1923). A lovely image, this is the "Lamp Seller of Bagdad". Condition of the cards is perfect mint, but the original wax paper has been removed. Besides the 52 cards, there is one joker and one card entitled "The Picture" with a three-paragraph notice from Edison Lamp Works about the Maxfield Parrish Edison Mazda paintings series. Box is somewhat rough and shows signs of handling.

Three Stooges #3 (St. John, 1953). Early 3-D issue (glasses included) has great gloss and colors. Some stress along spine edge. Very tough to find in the higher grades. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $410.

The Avengers Group (Marvel, 1964-65). This Silver Age group includes: #5 VG/FN with some glue on spine; #6 VF-; #7 FN/VF; #8 VG; #9 FN/VF; #10 FN- some color touch; #14 VF-; #15 FN-; #17 FN/VF; and #19 FN-. Key issues include a Hulk appearance (#5), the first appearance of the original Baron Zemo and his Masters of Evil, and the introduction, first appearance, and death of Wonder Man (#9), and the first appearance of the Swordsman (#19). Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $489. (Total: 10 Comic Books)

The Avengers Silver Age Group (Marvel, 1965-1970). Large group of Avengers includes issues #20-21 FN; #22 FN/VF; #23 VG; #25 FN- (Doc Doom appearance); #26 FN+; #27-28 FN (Giant-Man becomes Goliath); #29 FN; #30 FN+; #31 VG+; #32 FN/VF; #33 FN; #34 FN+; #35 FN; #37 FN+; #38-39 FN; #40 VG/FN; #41 VG+; #42 VF-; #43 VF (1st appearance of the Red Guardian); #44-45 FN+; #46 VG/FN; #47 FN- (Magneto appearance); #48 VF- (Black Knight appearance); #49 FN+; #52 VF (Black Panther joins the team); #56 FN (Baron Zemo appearance and partial origin of Captain America); #60-61 FN-; #66 FN/VF; #67 FN; #69 FN/VF; #70-72 FN; #73 FN/VF; #74-75 FN; 76-77 FN+; and #79 FN/VF. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $438. (Total: 64 Comic Books)

Tales to Astonish #59 (Marvel, 1964). Fantastic battle issue as the Incredible Hulk (first appearance in this title) goes "toe to toe" with Giant-Man, with cover art by Jack "King" Kirby. Very high-grade copy of this issue has full, rich color strike and cover gloss with a fresh off-white to white cover background and interior paper. Razor sharp corners and cover edges with no chipping at all. This book is still quite undervalued in the current market. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $180.

Double Comics #1 (Elliot, 1940). Large 128 page issue has "Double Features", "Double Size" and "Double Value" with a Masked Marvel cover. Condition: VG+. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $221; FN 6.0 value = $663.

Picture Stories from the Bible #1: Old Testament Edition Gaines File pedigree (EC, 1943). High-grade first issue includes such legendary Biblical stories as Adam and Eve in the Garden of Eden, Noah and the Ark, Abraham: the Friend of God and the patriarch Jacob. Bright canary-yellow cover, tight clean spine, no staple stress and supple paper. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $71; NM 9.4 value = $190.

Tales From the Crypt #28 (EC, 1952). A beautiful copy with deep, rich cover colors. Slightly rusted staples and very slightly rounded corners don't detract from the overall exceptional appearance of this EC gem. CGC VF+ 8.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $120; NM 9.4 value = $440.

Tales From the Crypt #35 (EC, 1953). Classic Jack Davis werewolf cover on this high-grade gem. Back cover shows a bit of soiling, but an attractive book overall. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $109; NM 9.4 value = $400.

Extra! #3 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1955). Ripped from the pages of the world's headlines! Very attractive copy with a very important pedigree, this book shows very slight dust shadowing along the top front cover, but is otherwise sharp, flat and clean. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $110.

Maxfield Parrish Playing Cards (Edison Mazda, 1930). The most popular image of the series, "Ecstasy" is actually quite scarce. Condition of the deck is mint, still sealed in the original wax paper. Box is above average, but shows considerable handling.

X-Men Bronze Age Group (Marvel, 1976-78). An outstanding lot of four CGC NM 9.4 books from Dave Cockrum's classic run. Lot includes: #103 Off-white to white pages; #104 Off-white to white pages; #107 Off-white to white pages; and #110 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value for group = $200. (Total: 4 Comic Books)

Weird Horrors Group (St. John, 1952). Group of comics from this horror title includes issues #1 FN with tape on inside cover at lower staple area; #2 FN+; #3 VG; #4 VG; and #8 FN. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $189; FN 6.0 value for group = $564. (Total: 5 Comic Books)

Law Breakers Suspense Stories Group (Capitol Stories, 1953). Three books from this classic horror/crime title include issues #10 VG+; #11 VG-; and #14 FN. Issue #11 has the classic severed tongues cover and story. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $144; FN 6.0 value for group = $433. (Total: 3 Comic Books)

Large Feature Comic #7 (Dell, 1939). Based on the famous radio program, "Hi-Yo Silver The Lone Ranger to the Rescue" is the second Large Feature Book devoted to this Western Hero. Story book with black and white illustrations by Henry Vallely. Flat copy with good color and supple cream pages. Right edge of front cover has a 2" tear and top edge of back cover has 2 1/4" tears. Condition: VG. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $109; FN 6.0 value = $327.

Power Comics #4 (Holyoke Publications, 1945). A stunning L.B. Cole cover highlights this hard-to-find issue. There is a minor degree of soiling on the front cover, slightly rounded corners, a 2.5" stain on the upper back cover, and some chipping at the bottom back cover. CGC FN 6.0 Off white pages. Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $442.

The Amazing Spider-Man #37 (Marvel, 1966). The penultimate Steve Ditko issue of Spider-Man, with lots of robot mayhem! This is an excellent copy with just a couple of very light spine stress lines and a very slight rounding of the top right front cover corner. The back cover has the letter "m" pencilled on it. Very crisp, with tremendous eye-appeal. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $57; NM 9.4 value = $210.

Journey into Mystery Group (Marvel, 1964). Three high-grade copies of this classic title starring the Mighty Thor includes issues #100 CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white pages; #101 CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages; and #112 CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white pages. Features great showdowns with Mr. Hyde (#100), Zarrko (#101) and the Incredible Hulk (#112). Overstreet NM 9.4 value for group = $495. (Total: 3 Comic Books)

X-Men #25 (Marvel, 1966). The X-Men face the menace of El Tigre in this issue, which is highlighted by Jack "King" Kirby's cover art. Very high-grade copy has a beautiful black cover and tremendous gloss. Clean cover edges with no "Marvel chipping". Interior paper is supple and fully fresh. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $125.

X-Men #28 (Marvel, 1967). Featuring the first appearance of the Banshee, this is a tight, solid book with excellent color and good strike. Very minor dings to the top and bottom spine do not decrease the overall attractiveness of this book. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $180.

Leading Comics #8 Crowley pedigree (DC, 1943). Early issue has alluring blue cover with a major mis-cut and very fresh pages. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $237; NM 9.4 value = $750.

Superman #12 (DC, 1941). Classic Fred Ray cover. Moderate spine wear, with a tear by the lower staple, several small tears on cover edges, pencil scribble on "S" in title, and light tanning on cover keep this book from the higher grades. A nice copy for a mid-grade collection. CGC VG+ 4.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $211; FN 6.0 value = $633.

Superman #12 (DC, 1941). This issue is highlighted by a classic Fred Ray cover. Visible flaws include fairly heavy spine wear, some cover creasing, light tanning, and a small tear at the top. A nice mid-grade copy overall. CGC VG 4.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $211; FN 6.0 value = $633.

Uncanny Tales Group, Circle 8 pedigree (Atlas, 1957). Very attractive group includes issues #52, 53 VF-; and #55 VF. All are very flat and supple copies with superb color inks, cover gloss and very clean edges (no chipping) with fresh off-white interior paper. Issue #52 has a Bill Everett cover. Tough to find these issues in the higher grades. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group =$152; NM 9.4 value for group = $400. (Total: 3 Comic Books)

Herbie Group (ACG, 1963-67). Group of 25 comics includes Herbie #1-23 and Forbidden Worlds #114 and #116 both FN. The Herbie run is mostly VF- condition with some VF and a few FN. Herbie #1 is a FN. Issue #54 has appearances by The Beatles, Frank Sinatra and Dean Martin and Jerry Lewis. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $491; NM 9.4 value for group = $1,800. (Total: 25 Comic Books)
Superman #4 (DC, 1940). Low-grade but complete copy of this fourth issue stars Superman, the Man of Steel. The cover has tape. Condition: FR/GD. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $522.

Picture Stories from the Bible #1: Old Testament Edition Gaines File pedigree (DC / EC, 1942). The Old Testament presented "For the First Time in Colored Continuity!" Rich vibrant cover colors make this an extremely attractive book, marred only by a series of creases on the bottom right back cover. Otherwise a beautiful copy from the private collection of Bill Gaines. CGC VF+ 8.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $71; NM 9.4 value = $190.

Mad Rare Hardback Group. This is a group of four rare hardback collections of Mad reprint material. Includes: A Golden Trashery of Mad (1960) Book is VF, dust jacket is FR, with multiple tears and wear (DJ is in a plastic slipcover); Mad Forever (1959) Book is VG (tape stains to inside front and back covers, some damage to interior pages), dust jacket is GD (water damage and stains, no major tears); The Ridiculously Expensive Mad (1969) Book is VG+ with some wear on the edges (all add-ins are present, but the record and stencil pages have been detached), dust jacket is VG+ with minor tears, some foxing, and overall wear; and Mad for Keeps (1958) Book is FN+ (some wear along edges), dust jacket is GD (water damage, chipping, heavy overall wear). (Total: 4 Comic Books)

Extra! #4 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1955). An extremely attractive copy of this pedigreed book. Bright colors, sharp corners, and an excellent cut make this an attractive book for any collection. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $110.

Plastic Man #57 Bethlehem pedigree (Quality, 1955). Very nice copy of this scarce later issue stars the original "Long Arm of the Law" in "The Chemist's Cauldron." Flat, clean and tight book has excellent colors and eye-appeal with supple paper and good gloss. Label note reads: "Light tanning bottom edges interior front and back cover". Very tough to find in this high grade. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $240.

Plastic Man #58 Bethlehem pedigree (Quality, 1956). Plastic Man stars in "The Amazing Duplicating Machine". Vibrant colors and great gloss with clean edges and a very nice spine. Printing cut is near exact and interior paper very supple. Very scarce in this high grade, we have never seen a better copy anywhere. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $240.

Frontier Romances #1 (Avon, 1949). This first issue has "Real Love Stories of the Golden West" and has an eye-catching painted cover. An attractive supple book that has nice fresh paper and two siamese pages. Quite scarce in any grade whatsoever. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $140; NM 9.4 value = $420.

Racket Squad in Action #12 (Charlton, 1954). A high-grade key issue with the classic explosion cover by Steve Ditko and interior art by Joe Shuster. This good-looking book has sharp, deep colors and good gloss with very supple paper. Near perfect centering with some light wear at the lower right corner of the front cover. This book is highly desired among collectors. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $140; NM 9.4 value = $420.

Sensation Comics Group (DC, 1949-50). Later issues of this title, which feature Wonder Woman, can be very difficult to find. This group includes issues #88 FN+; and #97 FN-. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $228. (Total: 2 Comic Books)

Action Comics #220 (DC, 1956). Typical mid-'50s fun with the Man of Steel, as Supes takes part in the Interplanetary Olympics. An excellent copy with moderate spine wear and some printer's creases on the front cover. Rich color and clarity! CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $120; NM 9.4 value = $325.

Picture Stories from the Bible #2: Old Testament Edition Gaines File pedigree (DC / EC, 1942). Large Old Testament Edition includes such legendary stories from the Bible as David and Goliath (cover), Joshua, Samson, King Solomon and the prophet Daniel. This is a very attractive high-grade issue with full, deep colors, clean edges and sharp corners. Tight, clean spine with only the slightest hint of stress at staples. Lots of eye appeal and very tough to find in a grade this high. CGC VF- 7.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $71; NM 9.4 value = $190.

Crime SuspenStories #12 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1952). An early Haunt of Fear story highlights this great issue. This copy shows moderate wear, including some abrading along the spine and at the upper cover, a slight chip out of the lower right corner, and some very slight spine stress. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $66; NM 9.4 value = $240.

Maxfield Parrish Playing Cards (Edison Mazda, 1926). Parrish's painting "Enchantment". The cards are in perfect mint condition, and include an Edison Mazda logo card and an Auction Bridge and Game of Five Hundred rules card as well, plus a smaller paper listing an updated "New Table of Points" for auction bridge, also in perfect condition. (We have never seen one of those before!). Box is in nice condition with only minor signs of handling.

Young Allies #7 (Timely, 1943). Early issue has a great WWII bondage cover (by Alex Schomburg) as Toro and Bucky rush to the rescue. Flat and clean book has deep colors and supple light cream pages with a detached centerfold. Condition: VG+. Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $348.

Crime SuspenStories #6 (EC, 1951). Another outstanding issue of this EC crime classic, which includes artwork by Johnny Craig, Jack Kamen, and Graham Ingels. Very fresh copy has square corners and vivid color. Apparent flaws include a tiny crease in bottom right corner, rust stains in the logo, very minor spine stress, and two very minor stains on the back cover. A really sharp copy. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $87; NM 9.4 value = $320.

Extra! #2 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1955). Great Johnny Craig cover. Deep rich red cover and few visible flaws make this book look much nicer than its grade. Very attractive and desirable. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $110.

Crime SuspenStories #13 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1952). A stunning Johnny Craig cover and an early Old Witch appearance make this a very desirable comic. From the personal collection of Bill Gaines, this issue shows some slight cover bends, a small crease at the upper right back cover, and slight tanning on the back cover. Overall, this one's great-looking, making this an excellent addition to any EC collection. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $75; NM 9.4 value = $275.

Maxfield Parrish Playing Cards (Edison Mazda, 1924). Parrish's famous "Venetian Lamplighter". Condition of the deck is mint, still sealed in the original wax paper, though some of the wax paper is missing. Box is in excellent condition, though not quite mint.

Amazing Spider-Man Group (Marvel, 1970-73). Here's a terrific lot of three high-grade issues, all in CGC NM- 9.2. Lot includes: # 85 (Kingpin story) White pages; #94 (origin retold) Off-white pages; #119 (Hulk appearance) Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value for group = $195. (Total: 3 Comic Books)

Amazing Adventures prototype (Ziff-Davis, 1950). A truly rare and unique item, this is an eight-page prototype of the first issue of AMAZING ADVENTURES which was mailed to subscribers of Ziff-Davis' science-fiction magazines to test the market. It is in black and white throughout, and features a complete story by Alex Schomburg and a back-page feature titled "Amazing Prophecies" by Ogden Whitney. Measuring approximately 8.5" x 11", this booklet is in Very Fine condition with some overall tanning on the front and back. The insides, however, are very nice, displaying off-white to white page quality. Staples are somewhat rusted, and have left small rust marks at the centerfold. There is a barely-visible small stain at the very bottom right corner of the front cover, and there are several bends/creases at the top, but none are extensive enough to disrupt the exceptional eye-appeal of this rarely-seen gem. Think twice before passing this one up, as you may never see another, especially in this condition. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $133; NM 9.4 value = $400.

Beyond #1 Diamond Run pedigree (Ace, 1950). This first issue showcases "Weird, Spooky, Supernatural Stories" with the cover story "The Werewolf Strikes!" An impressive copy, with deep black inks and good gloss. The only flaws of note are some minor cover edge wear and an impact dent at the top left corner of the front cover. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $120; NM 9.4 value = $350.

Fox Giant Group (Fox Features, 1948-49). This lot of four very scarce square-bound comics includes: 132-page Fox Giant "March of Crime" VG+, containing several Rulah and Phantom Lady stories; 132-page Fox Giant "March of Crime" VG+, that features All Top and Jo Jo stories; 130-page Fox Giant "March of Crime" VG, with All Top, Sunny and Junior Comics and Fox Giant "Album of Crime" VG. Each one of these issues are very tough to find in any condition. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $162; FN 6.0 value for group = $485. (Total: 4 Comic Books)

Fox Giant Crime Comic Group (Fox Features, 1949-50) Four very elusive large square-bound crime comics, including: 132-page Fox Giant "All-Famous Crime Stories" #nn FN+; 132-page Fox Giant "Crimes Incorporated" #nn FN; 132-page Fox Giant "Journal of Crime" #nn VG; and 132-page Fox Giant "The Truth about Crime" #nn VG+, which features Rulah, Jungle Goddess. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $166; FN 6.0 value for group = $495. (Total: 4 Comic Books)

Tomb of Terror #12 (Harvey, 1953). Pre-code-horror doesn't get much better than this! With a gruesome cover of a man about to be branded by a decaying corpse, this is a Harvey Publications file copy. There is some slight spine wear, and some minor tanning to the back cover, but overall this book is extremely attractive, with sharp corners, a good cut, and rich, vibrant cover colors. CGC VF+ 8.5 Light tan to off-white pages. Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $69; NM 9.4 value = $185.

Top Secrets Group (Street & Smith, 1949). Five issues from this crime title include #1 CGC FN+ 6.5 Off-white to white pages; #2 CGC FN+ 6.5 Cream to off-white pages; #3 FN- 5.5 Cream to off-white pages; #4 CGC FN+ 6.5 Cream to off-white pages; and #8 CGC FN 6.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $376. (Total: 5 Comic Books)

Camera Comics Group (Charlton, 1944). This lot includes issues #1 and 2 both in FN+ condition. Both are flat, clean and tight copies with supple off-white to light cream paper. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $97. (Total: 2 Comic Books)

Power Comics #3-4 (Holyoke Publications, 1944-45). Solid low-grade copies of these scarce issues have covers by Golden-Age great L.B. Cole. Issue #3 CGC VG 4.0 cream to off-white pages; #4 CGC GD+ 2.5 cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 value for group = $535. (Total: 2 Comic Books)

Doctor Strange #169 (Marvel, 1968). "The Master of the Mystic Arts" in his own book for the first time! A lovely copy with few visible flaws. Sharp, flat and clean, with exceptional page quality. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $160.

X-Men #40 (Marvel, 1968). The mutant team goes up against the legendary Frankenstein Monster in this fun-filled issue with terrific art by Don Heck and George Tuska. As an added bonus, this is only the second issue to feature their spiffy new costumes. A beautiful example of this book, very fresh-looking with square corners and good color. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $85.

Flash Annual #1 (DC, 1963). This must-have book reprints some of the Scarlet Speedster's best stories, including the origins of Kid Flash, the Elongated Man, and Gorilla Grodd, as well as a Golden Age Flash story with Star Sapphire. An 80-page, 25¢ giant, this is a hard issue to find in high-grade. There is slight wear around the edges, including a small crease at the lower right corner of the front cover, and light soiling on the back cover. Otherwise a very good-looking copy. CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $112; NM 9.4 value = $450.

George Woodbridge - Original Art for "Medical Illustrated" from Mad #65 (EC, 1961). Offered here is the first page from Mad's health-care parody, "Medical Illustrated". Rendered in gray tones by George Woodbridge, this piece would look wonderful in any doctor's office. As is common with Mad art from this period, the type was set on a separate overlay, which is not included with this piece. The art measures approximately 16" x 20.75" and is in excellent condition.

Impact #2 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1955). The first issue of this title to carry the seal of the Comics Code Authority, and one of the earliest "New Direction" books from EC, this issue features top-notch work from Reed Crandall, Jack Davis, and "Ghastly" Graham Ingels. Gorgeous copy with vivid colors and a fresh, crisp appearance; no surprise since it's one of the legendary Gaines File pedigree books. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. NM 9.4 value = $100.

Racket Squad in Action Group (Charlton, 1954). This group of books includes issues #2 FN; #3 VG+; #4 GD; #5 GD/VG ("Headlights" cover); #6 VG; #7 FN; #8 FN-; #13 FN; #17 FN+; #22 FN; #25 FN-; and #29 FN (scarce last giant issue). Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $167; FN 6.0 value for group = $465. (Total: 12 Comic Books)

Jumbo Comics #96 (Fiction House, 1947). More fun in the primeval forest, this issue sports an outstanding cover of Sheena the Jungle Queen battling a panther. Also includes the adventures of The Hawk, Sky Girl, "and many others." This is a very nice high-grade copy with incredibly fresh colors. Corners are reasonably sharp, and the book exhibits virtually no spine stress. A real beauty. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $60; NM 9.4 value = $160.

Batman #70 (DC, 1952). Batman meets "The Robot Cop of Gotham City". Excellent color and cut, this copy shows light to moderate spine wear, a pencilled "10.00" on the back cover, and the least amount of dust shadowing on the front cover. Looks much nicer than its grade would suggest. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $182; NM 9.4 value = $575.

Superman #100 (DC, 1955). The spectacular 100th issue of Superman featured some classic cover reprints, but all-new stories, celebrating the Man of Steel's centennial. This is a very good-looking mid-grade copy, flat and clean, with excellent cover color. Some spine stress, light creasing at both bottom corners, and light-to-moderate overall wear are the only problems. There is also one very small amateur tear seal on the back cover. Happy 100, Superman! Condition: VG/FN. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $200; FN 6.0 value = $600.

Girls' Romances Group (DC, 1950-55). Scarce group of DC Romance comics includes issues #2 VG/FN; #4 VG; #5 VG/FN; #6 VG; #7 GD/VG; #8, 10-11 FN; #13 FN-; and #32 GD/VG. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $191; FN 6.0 value = $577. (Total: 10 Comic Books)

Picture Stories from World History #2 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1947). Second issue of this historical title depicts Europe's struggle for civilization. Flat, clean and tight copy has deep colors, clean cover edges and a tight spine. There is a minor impact dent to the lower right corner and minor wear at bottom of spine. Centering is exact and interior paper very fresh. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $79; NM 9.4 value = $210.

Impact #3 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1955). The comic that presents "Tales Designed to Carry an Impact!" A nearly perfect example from an historic collection, this book shows great color, sharp corners, and just the slightest amount of spine stress. An excellent addition to any high-grade collection. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $80.

Terry and the Pirates #7 (Harvey, 1947). An exceptionally beautiful copy of this Caniff classic. Sharp and clean, with only the slightest visible defects. Deep, rich colors provide a high degree of eye appeal. CGC NM 9.4 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $90.

Marvel Super-Heroes #1 (Marvel, 1966). Marvel's first one-shot, this book reprints some of the stories that made Marvel Mighty! Representing such classic features as Daredevil #1, Avengers #2 and the classic Human Torch/Submariner battle from Marvel Mystery #8, this exceptional copy has a beautiful square-bound spine, razor-sharp corners, and a great overall appearance. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $90.

Bob Clarke - Original Art for "Educational Billboards" from Mad #54 (EC, 1960). A great two-page spread that includes not only the art, but the overlay containing the type, which is unusual for Mad art from this era. Written byMad mainstay Tom Koch, this hilarious spread includes Bob Clarke's beautiful renderings of several vintage cars. Total overall dimensions are approximately 22.75" x 36.5". The art is in excellent condition, painted in grey-tones on heavy illustration board, with the overlays attached at the top by masking tape. The left-hand page has a note to the photographer in orange marker, and another notation in blue marker, neither of which affect the art in any way.

Crime SuspenStories #5 (EC, 1951). Still more crime and suspense, and even a terror-tale from the Haunt of Fear - in other words, classic EC! An eerie Johnny Craig cover leads off this fright-filled issue, with sterling work by EC stalwarts Jack Kamen, Graham Ingels, and Jack Davis. Exceptional copy with sharp corners and vivid colors. Only apparent problem is some rust-stains (caused by the clasp of an envelope) in the "I" of the logo. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $108; NM 9.4 value = $400.

Crime SuspenStories #16 (EC, 1953). Classic Johnny Craig cover. A lovely copy of this book, with very slight spine stress and some dust shadowing on the back cover. CGC notes, "manufactured with 1 staple only". Stunning cover colors and sharp corners make this an attractive addition to any collection. CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $75; NM 9.4 value = $275.

Impact #5 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1955). Classic Jack Davis boxing cover. This book shows a very small chip at the top spine, slight to moderate spine wear, and very light tanning on the back cover. An otherwise brilliant copy from the personal files of Bill Gaines. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $80.
Maxfield Parrish Playing Cards (Edison Mazda,1927). This is Parrish's "Reveries". Condition is mint, still sealed in the original wax paper. The box is near mint.
Maxfield Parrish Playing Cards (Edison Mazda, 1928). Parrish favorite "Contentment". Condition of the deck is mint, still sealed in the original wax paper. Box is very nice.

The Amazing Spider-Man #121 (Marvel, 1973). Spider-Man cries, "Someone close to me is about to die!" on the cover of this legendary issue. A landmark issue in the Spider-Man canon, with a dynamite cover from "Jazzy" John Romita, and top-notch interior work by "Gentleman" Gil Kane. This is an incredible high-grade copy of this sought-after book, with sharp corners, and vibrant color. A couple of light smudges at the spine, and in Gwen's hair, are the only apparent flaws. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $40; NM 9.4 value = $150.

The Amazing Spider-Man #121 (Marvel, 1973). A comic that still sparks controversy, almost thirty years after its publication. The death of Gwen Stacy is one of the most important events in the Marvel Universe, and this is a stunning copy of this landmark issue. A bright, vivid yellow cover highlights this impressive gem, with very few flaws to reduce the overwhelming eye-appeal. Sharp corners, excellent cut, with just the slightest evidence of chipping at the bottom of the cover. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $150.

Mac Raboy - Original Art for Master Comics #15(?), Zoro the Magician story (Fawcett, 1940). An outstanding piece of art that shows the early work of a true Master. Before moving to Captain Marvel, Jr., which would become his signature piece, Raboy worked on a number of filler strips for Fawcett, including Zoro the Magician, from which this example comes. Raboy's work would continue to refine itself over the coming years, but one can clearly see the beginnings of a master's technique in this page. Delicate brush strokes and a dynamic graphic sense are hallmarks of Raboy's work, and both can be seen here. On heavy art board measuring approximately 15" x 20", there is a moderate amount of white-out in evidence, as well as the usual blue pencil editor's markings, but this is, overall, a great piece showing the genesis of a true comics legend.

Police Comics #28 Sherwood (Quality, 1944). A classic Jack Cole Plastic Man cover highlights this issue, which also features the Spirit by Will Eisner. This book shows very slight tanning/soiling and some creasing/wrinkling to the front cover, with a light pencil mark in the upper left corner. The spine shows very light wear. A very attractive book. CGC VF- 7.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $213; NM 9.4 value = $675.
