CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $17.75; NM 9.4 value = $65.

Mac Raboy - Original Art for Flash Gordon Sunday Page (King Features Syndicate). Mac Raboy was the artist on Flash Gordon Sundays from 1948 until his death in 1967, so while the exact date of this piece is unknown (the only date in evidence is "11-10". No year is given), it can certainly be placed within that range. This is a beautiful example of Raboy's superb draftsmanship. Unfortunately, it is in rather rough condition. There is significant warpage, obviously from water or humidity damage, and some evidence of mildew on the back. White-out is used throughout, sometimes artistically (as in the clouds in the last panel), more often as correction, and the title stat is somewhat discolored. The piece measures approximately 14.5" x 19.75" on heavy art board and is signed by Raboy in the last panel. The fine, delicate linework of a true master is evident throughout.

Land of the Lost #1 (EC, 1946). This whimsical Max Gaines-era EC humor book was based on the popular Mutual Radio series of the time. This is a solid mid-grade copy with light dust shadowing, moderate blunting to the corners, and some dinginess in the yellow background of the logo. There is also a number penciled just to the left of the logo. Cover image is very crisp with excellent color. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $101; NM 9.4 value = $270.

Crime SuspenStories #9 (EC, 1952). Solid mid-grade book includes art by such greats as Jack Kamen, Jack Davis, Graham "Ghastly" Ingels and Johnny Craig. Flat and supple comic has full color and no tears or creasing. Lower left edge of back cover has a chip. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $87; NM 9.4 value = $320.

Jungle Comics #120 (Fiction House, 1949). A great jungle girl cover graces this terrific issue. This copy has several grease pencil markings on the front cover, including "a", "A-4", and "11-17". Additionally, CGC has noted "'350' written on 1st page in pencil". Still a very nice example of this classic series. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $49; NM 9.4 value = $130.

Marvel Reprint Group (Marvel, 1963-67). A group of nice square-bound comics features the reprinted early adventures of the Fantastic Four, the Amazing Spider-Man, the Incredible Hulk, the X-Men, Iron Man and others. This group includes Marvel Collectors' Item Classics #1-2 VF-; #4 FN; #5 FN/VF; #6 VF/NM; #7 FN; #8 VF+; #9 VF; and Marvel Tales #2, 4.5; #6 VF; #7, FN; and #14 FN/VF. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $161; NM 9.4 value for group = $555. (Total: 5 Comic Books)

Suspense Detective Group (Fawcett, 1952). This group includes issues #1 VG (George Evans and Bernard Baily art); #4 VG/FN; and #5 GD/VG. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $88; FN 6.0 value for group = $261. (Total: 3 Comic Books)

Romance Group (Famous Funnies/Fawcett, 1950). Exceptional copies of these key first issues include Life Story #1 VF; and Personal Love #1 FN/VF. Both have attractive photo covers with Life Story #1 having nice off-white to light cream paper and Personal Love #1 displaying page shaving and light tanning at the edges. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $88; NM 9.4 value for group = $235. (Total: 2 Comic Books)

Jungle Comics #116 (Fiction House, 1949). Tons of fun in the jungle with Camilla the comely Congo Queen, and Ka'a'nga, Lord of the Jungle. This issue features some outstanding work by legendary "good girl" artist Matt Baker. This is a very nice copy with a crisp appearance and exceptionally good color. Bottom of the spine is slightly blunted, there is a small chip out of the bottom right corner of the front cover, some dinginess to vertical box at the top left edge of the spine, and a light dust shadow across the top of the back cover. CGC notes that the number "350" is written on the first page in pencil. CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $49; NM 9.4 value = $130.

Jungle Thrills #16 (Star, 1952). A great L.B. Cole cover with a Sheena-like Jungle Warrior fighting an attacking Gorilla. This comic has excellent colors and light cream paper with some creasing at the lower right corner of the front cover. Condition: FN- Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $150.

Funny Comics Group (St. John, 1950-52) This lot contains two large square-bound comics, including: All-Picture Comedy Carnival #1 FN, with art by Matt Baker; and Giant Comics Editions #14 VG. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $70; FN 6.0 value for group = $210. (Total: 2 Comic Books)

Treasure Comics Group (Prize, 1946). Two mid-grade books. This group includes #3 FN+ Cream pages; and #5 FN+ Cream pages. Both issues show good cover colors and excellent eye-appeal. Issue #5 has artwork by Bernard Krigstein. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $140 (Total: 2 Comic Books)

Superboy #54 (DC, 1956). The Boy of Steel loses his voice in this fun-filled issue. Light spine wear, some very light creasing along the top edge, and slight dust shadowing along the right front cover edge are the only visible problems with this solid high-grade copy. CGC VF 8.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $54; NM 9.4 value = $160.

Crime Patrol #9 (EC, 1948). Cool crime stuff from EC, with a terrific cover. This is a solid low-grade copy, with moderate overall wear and a slight spine roll. CGC VG+ 4.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $55; FN 6.0 value = $165.

Feature Book #46 (Better Publications, 1946). This issue stars Mandrake the Magician in "Fire World". A nice mid-grade copy with full colors and supple off-white pages. Condition: FN. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $124.

Mystery in Space #75 (DC, 1962). A landmark issue of this popular series, featuring a Justice League cross-over. Very minor chipping to the back cover, and slightly rounded corners are about all that keep this book from a higher grade. Clean, sharp and bright. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $75; NM 9.4 value = $275.

Milt Caniff - Original Art for Steve Canyon Daily 10/8/58 (Field Enterprises, Inc., 1958). Caniff has been called the world's most influential cartoonist, and this daily from 1958 certainly supports that claim. Dynamic layouts, sharp use of black and white, and a mastery of facial expression are all evident in this four-panel gem. Measuring approximately 7" x 23", the art is in good condition, with a moderate use of white-out, and glue stains along the bottom of the piece and at the top of panel three. Still, this is a great example of Caniff at the very top of his form.

Romance Comics Group (Quality, 1950). This group includes Love Diary #1 VG+ (Bill Ward cover/interior art); and Love Scandals #2 FN (Paul Gustavson interior art). Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $40; FN 6.0 value for group = $120. (Total: 2 Comic Books)

Shock Detective Cases #20 (Star Publications, 1952). First issue of this title is based on true crime cases and has cover art by L.B. Cole. Good-looking mid-grade book has supple off-white pages. Condition: FN+. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 = $68; NM 9.4 value = $180.

Jungle Comics #128 (Fiction House, 1950). High-grade late issue has fantastic color inks and gloss with very fresh paper. Label note reads: "350 written on 1st page in pencil." CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $130.

The Amazing Spider-Man #64 (Marvel, 1968). It's high-flying action as Spidey becomes "The Vulture's Prey!" From "Jazzy" Johnny Romita's highly acclaimed run on this title, the copy is flat and clean with only the slightest evidence of spine stress. A very attractive copy in superb condition. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $65.

Superboy Annual #1 Green River pedigree (DC, 1964). Solid mid-grade copy of this first Annual issue starring Superboy. Full colors and gloss with supple paper and a date stamp on the front cover. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $52; NM 9.4 value = $190.

Panic #1-#12 Group (EC, 1954). A complete run of Harvey Kurtzman's Panic, all complete, but in low grade. #1 is FR/GD, with tape all along the spine; the rest are GD/GD+, with one or two VGs in the lot. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value for group = $120. (Total: 12 Comic Books)

Captain America Comics #39 Okajima pedigree (Timely, 1944). Captain America rushes into action against the Japanese Army on this action-packed cover by Alex Schomburg. Issue has a Human Torch back-up story. A beauty with superb colors (rich ruby-reds and deep-blues), tight spine and clean cover edges with no chips, no creasing and no tears. Minor hint of wear at top and bottom of spine and the tiniest hint of staple stress keeps this copy from the NM range. The finest copy of Cap #39 we've ever seen. CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $1,700.

Captain America Comics #56 (Timely, 1946). Beautiful high-grade copy of this book stars America's Sentinel of Liberty with cover art by Syd Shores. Great color strike and overall eye-appeal with supple paper and near perfect centering. Minor wear at spine edge and at staple. Tough to get this nice! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,150.

Sub-Mariner Comics #1 (Timely, 1941). Spectacular debut issue has a great action-packed cover by Alex Schomburg as Subby overturns a Nazi transport vessel, spilling a platoon of German soldiers into the drink. Sub-Mariner story art by his legendary creator, Bill Everett, and an Angel back-up feature by Paul Gustavson. Bright, clean and flat comic has great color strike, exact centering and excellent eye appeal. Book has some general overall aging with no main defect of note save for a detached centerfold noted of the label. CGC FN 6.0 Light tan to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $6,480.

Marvel Comics #1 (Timely, 1939). This key issue was the genesis of one of the most successful comic book companies in history: Timely (Marvel) Comics. The first issue for this flagship title has many outstanding features, beginning with the origin and first appearance of its sensational cover star, the Human Torch. Created by Carl Burgos, this is the first cover appearance of the hero as well (oddly illustrated by famous Sci-Fi pulp artist Frank R. Paul). Timely's second powerhouse character begins his series as well, in the first newsstand appearance of Prince Namor, the Sub-Mariner, created and illustrated by Golden-Age legend Bill Everett. Other back-up features include Ka-Zar the Great (the first appearance of the first-known Tarzan clone), the first and only appearance of the Jungle Terror, the first appearance of the Masked Rider and his horse Lightning, and the first appearance of the Angel by Paul Gustavson. This copy of Marvel Comics #1 has good color strike and exact centering (which is uncommon for this book). There has been extensive professional restoration including: color touch, pieces added, tear seals, spine reinforcement and cover cleaned and pressed. Corners have rounding and right edge of front cover has some small chips. The single most important aspect of this book is that it is an October copy, without the black circle printed over the original "OCT" date and "NOV" below. Seldom seen in this state, this is a very rare and highly desirable "variant" of this seminal issue. CGC Apparent VG/FN 5.0 Extensive (P) Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $14,000; FN 6.0 value = $42,000.

Men's Adventures #28 (Atlas, 1954). Scarce last issue for this title has 3 great features including the Human Torch(by Carl Burgos, Captain America by John Romita and the Sub-Mariner by Bill Everett. Very glossy with great black color inks and very supple off-white paper. Cover has a crease down center and spine has some wear at edge. Condition: VG. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $97; FN 6.0 value = $292.

Young Men #25 (Atlas, 1954). The "Human Torch Returns" in this scarce issue starring the three "Pillars" of Atlas: Human Torch by Carl Burgos, Captain America by John Romita, and the Sub-Mariner by Bill Everett. Attractive mid-grade comic has good colors and fresh paper. No chipping or creasing on this copy. Tough in any grade above the VG range. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $316; NM 9.4 value = $1,000.

Young Men #27 (Atlas, 1954). Rare issue has a spooky Ghoul-Head vs. the Human Torch cover and story by Carl Burgos with back-up stories of Captain America by John Romita and the Sub-Mariner by Bill Everett. Superb full color inks with nice gloss and clean edges (no chipping). Spine has some minor wear along edge. Label note reads: "Stain and 1" tear on 2nd page." From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC FN+ 6.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $316; NM 9.4 value = $1,000.

Daring Mystery Comics #4 (Timely, 1940). Hard-to-find early issue has the last Purple Mask appearance with a cool bondage cover by Alex Schomburg. Full colors and light cream pages with the spine having some glue and color touch. Condition: Apparent VG+. Overstreet 2001 GD 2.0 value = $300; FN 6.0 value = $900.

Daring Mystery Comics #7 (Timely, 1941). More costumed superheroes than you can shake a stick at in this classic comic from Timely. This issue features the origin of the Blue Diamond, plus the Fin by Bill Everett, Captain Daring by Simon & Kirby, The Thunderer by Carl Burgos, plus The Challenger, The Silver Scorpion, and Mr. Millions. As an added bonus, this issue also includes an ad for Sub-Mariner Comics #1. Overstreet claims this is a Schomburg cover, but we agree with the Grand Comic Database that it's more likely Joe Simon. A very sturdy-looking copy with moderate overall wear, and a slightly dingy back cover. CGC notes a name stamp on the first page, and there is a number pencilled in the "d" of the logo. CGC FN+ 6.5 Light tan to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $915; NM 9.4 value = $3200.

Printer's Proof of Mad #1 with Harvey Kurtzman's Notations (EC, 1952). An exceptionally rare item, and a true piece of comics history. This is Harvey Kurtzman's proof copy of the legendary Mad #1, the very first copy to come off the engraver's press. Though it is in somewhat delicate and brittle condition, and does not include the cover, it remains a truly historic document. It shows Kurtzman's edits and corrections to stories by Jack Davis, Will Elder, Wally Wood and John Severin (proving that Kurtzman was nothing if not thorough). This piece was originally sold at Sotheby's as part of the Harvey Kurtzman collection.

Mad Cufflinks (EC, 1957). Mad Magazine began offering five styles of jewelry manufactured by Astrahan of New York in June 1957, priced at $2 each or $3 for a pair of cuff links. Mad discontinued selling them by early 1958. Only 1,000 to 2,000 pieces were sold, according to the publisher, and all are excessively rare today with well under 100 pieces surviving. The cuff links were described by Mad as follows: "If you wear these 'What... Me Worry?' Kid cuff links with long-sleeved shirts, you'll amuse your friends. If you pierce your wrists and wear them with short-sleeved shirts, you'll kill your friends!" Excellent condition.From the Grant Geissman collection.

Exciting Comics #52 Big Apple pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1946). The Black Terror, Nemesis of Crime hits crime in the head on this cover by Alex Schomburg. Flat, clean and very fresh book has full colors and very clean edges with no tears, no creasing and no chips. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $475.

Captain Marvel Adventures #50 (Fawcett, 1945). Holy Moley! How come Billy Batson is punching out the bad guys, instead of the Big Red Cheese? Timeless fun and excitement in this Fawcett classic. This is an outstanding copy with excellent color and sharp corners. Only visible faults are some very light creasing on the back cover, minor wear at the top and bottom of spine, and the letter "m" written in pencil on the front cover, just below the logo. Otherwise, simply gorgeous. CGC NM 9.4 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $280.

National Comics #43 Crowley pedigree (Quality, 1944). Nice high grade copy of a hard to find in high grade title. This copy is beautiful; sharp, flat, clean, and terrifically appealing. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $180.

National Comics #53 Crowley pedigree (Quality, 1946). This book is flat and clean, with sharp corners. Slight dust shadowing along the edges and a very minor chip at the top spine cannot detract from the fabulous look of this almost-unread copy. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $150.

Plastic Man #1 (Quality, 1943). Jack Cole's Stretchable Sleuth in his own high-tensile title! This copy is marred by the presence of a fairly dark stain along the right edge and top right corner of the front cover, lighter tanning along the top and a moderate amount of spine wear. There is also some print bleed-through on the front and back cover. All in all, however, a nice copy of a desirable book. CGC VG/FN 5.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet GD 2.0 value = $381; FN 6.0 value = $1,143.

Forbidden Worlds #1 Aurora pedigree (American Comics Group, 1951). Exploring the supernatural with a fantastic double-headed Ghost cover and interior work by the super-talented team of Al Williamson and Frank Frazetta. Extremely fresh copy has great color, near perfect spine and clean cover edges. No tears, no chipping and no creasing. Wow! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,400.

Brenda Starr (Vol. 2), #3 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1948). Top-grade copy of this comic has eye-popping banana-yellow cover colors with rich fire-reds and a near perfect cut. Spine is tight and very sharp (minor nick at top and bottom) with no staple stress, cover edges clean and corners sharp. Comic has very slight professional restoration including "small tear seals on last 3 pages." CGC apparent NM- 9.2 Slight (P) Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $625.

Brenda Starr (Vol. 2), #5 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1948). Very attractive and bright swimsuit cover as Brenda Starr flees for her life from the pool party. Top-grade book with brilliant colors and perfectly white and fresh interior pages. Exceptional in every way, just as you would expect from a Mile High pedigree comic. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $610.

Brenda Starr (Vol. 2), #8 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1949). Masked Terror in the Powder-Mirror cover. White, fresh and very tight with exceptional eye appeal. A good example of why the Mile Highs are so highly desired by veteran collectors. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $610.

Brenda Starr (Vol. 2), #9 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1949). Super-killer copy has intense blood-red colors that seem to leap right off the cover. Perfect centering with an immaculate spine, no staple stress and outstanding eye appeal. Fully flat and tight with the "trademark" Mile High pedigree white paper. This is why books from this pedigree collection are so legendary, being unsurpassed in structure, quality and paper freshness. Incredible book! CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $610.

Brenda Starr (Vol. 2), #11 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1949). Beautiful copy of this scarce issue has brilliant banana-yellow cover colors, accented with deep emerald-green logo. Perfectly flat, clean, bright and tight with sharp corner and cover edges. Small nick at bottom corner of spine keeps this copy from the NM 9.4 range. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $620.

Campus Loves Group (Quality, 1949). A group of four books that includes issues #1 FN+; #2 VG; #4 FN; and #5 FN+. Issues #1 and 2 have cover and interior art by Bill Ward. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value for group = $237. (Total: 4 Comic Books)

Journey Into Fear #9 (Superior, 1952). Grisly pre-code horror in this rare book from Superior Comics. Light overall wear, a slightly bumped upper left corner, and the letter "s" pencilled in the logo are the only noticeable flaws. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $77; NM 9.4 value = $205.

Meet the New Post Gazette Sunday Funnies #1 (Pittsburgh Post, 1949). Scarce giveaway book includes such features as Dick Tracy, Terry and the Pirates, Brenda Starr, Archie and Superman. Attractive copy has excellent color and eye appeal with some slight amateur restoration including: tear seals to cover and interior. CGC Apparent VF+ 8.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $1,200; NM 9.4 value = $2,800.

Yellow Jacket Comics #8 (Farrell, 1946). This high-grade book has great colors and eye appeal with supple paper. There is a light impact dent at the top right corner of the front cover. Otherwise, this is a near perfect copy. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $350.

Amazing Fantasy #15 (Marvel, 1962). One of the most significant comics of the Silver Age, this book features the origin and first appearance of the Amazing Spider-Man. From the often-imitated Jack Kirby cover to the ground-breaking interiors by Steve Ditko, this book is the very definition of "classic", and is listed as #1 on Overstreet's list of Top 10 Silver Age Books. This copy displays some moderate "Marvel chipping" along the right edge of the front cover, but is otherwise very attractive and appealing, with rich cover colors, tight staples, and an excellent cut. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $3,500; VF 8.0 value = $12,825.

The Amazing Spider-Man #8 (Marvel, 1964). Spidey fights "The Living Brain" (as well as Flash Thompson), and the Fantastic Four appear in a back-up story pencilled by Jack Kirby and inked by Steve Ditko. High-energy fun from very early in this classic run. This is an "amazing" copy with square corners and beautiful color. Only visible flaws are a number written in pen on the front cover, and the book is slightly mis-stapled. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1050.

The Amazing Spider-Man #33 (Marvel, 1966). In what has become a legendary sequence, Spider-Man summons all of his strength and casts off a huge load of machinery that had been trapping him, so he can defeat the villain and save Aunt May. This sequence is also foreshadowed by one of Steve Ditko's most evocative -- and atypical -- covers. A classic issue of The Amazing Spider-Man, you'd be hard-pressed to find a better copy than this glorious, near-perfect example, which looks as fresh as the day it was printed. Square corners, vivid colors, and no "Marvel chipping". About the only problem here is a tiny amount of edge wear, and a front cover that is slightly off-register, favoring the right. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

The Amazing Spider-Man Annual #1 (Marvel, 1964). One of the great annuals of all time, this features the incredible 41-page story that introduces the Sinister Six, as well as a re-telling of Spidey's origin, and an early X-Men appearance. As with most square-bound books, this one is tough to find in high grade, but this is truly a great-looking copy with excellent eye-appeal. Spine shows some wear and stress, and the back cover is slightly dingy. There is light wear along the bottom edge of the front cover, but no "Marvel chipping". Color is outstanding, including the large white areas which are still fresh and clean. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $188; NM 9.4 value = $875.

The Amazing Spider-Man #8 (Marvel, 1964). Special "Tribute-to-Teenagers" issue as Spidey takes on The Living Brain and the Human Torch. This comic also has a Fantastic Four back-up story with artwork by "Sturdy" Steve Ditko and Jack "King" Kirby. Book has brilliant banana-yellow cover inks with intense ruby-reds. Fantastic eye-appeal. Perfectly flat and very tight with exact centering and fully fresh paper. Lower half of spine has a few minor dings. There is a small "m" written on the back cover in pen. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,050.

The Amazing Spider-Man #45 (Marvel, 1967). Issue has "One of the Wildest of all Spidey's Battles" as the Web-Slinger takes on the powerful Lizard. Beautiful copy is completely flat and clean with sharp spine and great colors. Terrific cover gloss and eye-appeal. CGC NM- 9.2 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $125.

The Amazing Spider-Man #47 Northland pedigree (Marvel, 1967). Spidey faces his arch-nemesis Kraven the Hunter in this issue with classic cover/interior art by John Romita. Very flat and glossy comic has excellent colors and very sharp edges (no "Marvel chipping"). CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $125.

Jack Kirby and Paul Reinman - Original Art for The Avengers #2, Page 12 (Marvel, 1963). Absolutely incredible page from the second issue of The Avengers features outstanding shots of Iron Man (still in his original yellow armor) and the Hulk, first as the villainous Space Phantom imitating the Hulk's appearance, and then as the genuine Green-Skin. You'd be hard-pressed to find an example of early Marvel art with more action than this beauty! Page is 13 1/2" x 21" in excellent condition, with some pencil lines still visible and no white-out within the art.

Jack Kirby and Paul Reinman - Original Art for The Avengers #3, Page 19 (Marvel, 1963). Avengers Assemble! This action-packed page of original art from Jack "King" Kirby features most of the Mighty Avengers, including Iron Man (one of the earliest appearances of his new, redesigned armor!), the Hulk, Thor, Giant Man, and the Wasp, all in a battle against Namor, the Sub-Mariner himself! That's a lot of Avengers action for one page! Artwork is 13 1/2" x 21" in excellent condition, with some light brown stains in several panels and in the borders. Minor white-out, mainly in captions and word balloons.

The Avengers #4 (Marvel, 1964). A really cool comic with the cover caption saying it all, "Captain America Lives Again!" Re-introduction and first Silver Age appearance of the greatest patriotic super-hero of all: Captain America. Cover and story art by Cap's co-creator Jack "King" Kirby with a classic cover as Cap charges forward, leading the Mighty Avengers into action. High-grade supple book has nice off-white cover and paper with rich color inks and cover gloss. Right edge of front cover has slight wear (tiny stress tears, but no "Marvel chipping") and bottom edge has wear. One of the most important comic books to be published during the Silver Age. CGC VF- 7.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $429; NM 9.4 value = $2,000.

Daredevil #5 (Marvel, 1964). The "Man Without Fear" meets the mysterious masked Matador in this early issue with vintage art by Wally Wood and Jack "King" Kirby. Nearly flawless book has just a bit of wear at upper right corner of front cover. Flat and tight with beautifully fresh interior paper. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $220.

Daredevil Group (Marvel, 1965). The "Man Without Fear" stars in these early issues with art by the legendary Wally Wood. Issue #6 CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages and #8 CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white pages. Both are very nice high-grade copies with sharp and strong colors and cover gloss. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value for group = $300. (Total: 2 Comic Books)

Fantastic Four #9 (Marvel, 1962). Early issue of "The World's Greatest Comic Magazine" starring the Fantastic Four as they face the challenge of the Mighty Sub-Mariner with vintage cover/story art by Jack "King" Kirby. Exceptional copy of this early issue is very bright and glossy with perfect cover edges (no chipping, no tears and no creasing), sharp corners and exact centering. Completely flat and tight with a sharp spine and only the slightest hint of wear beginning to show at top staple and spine edge. Label note reads: "name/address stamp on 1st, 9th and last page." Very scarce in this top-grade! CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $960.

Fantastic Four #48 (Marvel, 1966). One of the great epics of the Marvel Silver Age, this is the issue that introduced the Silver Surfer, and his planet-eating master Galactus. Stan Lee and Jack Kirby were at their finest in this grand, sweepingly cosmic tale. This is a near-perfect copy with no apparent flaws, bright colors, and a sharp, crisp appearance. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,050.

The Avengers #4 (Marvel, 1964). Landmark issue featuring the first Silver Age appearance of Captain America. This is an excellent copy with outstanding clarity and eye-appeal. Apparent flaws include a tiny bit of "Marvel chipping" (including a 1/4" chunk out just below Iron Man's left fist) on front and back covers, a tiny color chip by Ant-Man's foot, light edge wear at top and bottom, and what appear to be two light scratches on Cap's upper tunic, and one on his head. Still, an amazingly fresh-looking book, and a major Marvel Silver Age key. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $429; NM 9.4 value = $2,000.

This remains one of the toughest key Marvel comics to find in high-grade and this copy is one of the finest and freshest we have seen in quite some time. Jack Kirby and Paul Reinman - Original Art for X-Men #2, Page 19 (Marvel, 1963). Jerry Ordway - Original Cover Art for Superman: Exile (DC, 1998). One of the finest artists ever to lend his talents to the fabled Man of Steel, Jerry Ordway brought a powerful realism to the series. Nowhere is that more evident than in this gorgeous cover painting to the Superman: Exile trade paperback. The Man of Steel flies through outer-space, leaving Earth and the moon behind, his face a mask of hurtful disappointment at having to leave his adopted home. Rendered in water-based gauche, this amazing image features startlingly vivid colors, and an incredible attention to detail, including the crater-pitted surface of the moon, and a million stars filling the heavens. This piece is consigned from the artist himself, and those who know Jerry's work know that he rarely sells his original art, let alone a cover painting of this magnitude. So don't miss out on this amazing opportunity to own an iconic image of Superman, rendered by one of his finest creators. Painting is 12" x 17" on standard DC art board, with an image area of roughly 10" x 15 1/2".

Tim & Greg Hildebrandt - Star Wars Trading Card Art (1996). Spectacular Star Wars painting based on the Shadows of the Empire books, depicting the Millenium Falcon chasing the Outrider. The Brothers Hildebrandt produced paintings for several card sets based on Star Wars, and this is a fabulous example of their colorful and evocative style. A simply wonderful painting. 20" x 14", acrylic on board.

Steve Ditko - Original Cover Art for The Many Ghosts of Doctor Graves #55 (Charlton, 1975?). An evocative cover from the Doctor Graves series by the legendary Steve Ditko. A beautiful maiden, a mysterious caped figure, and a malevolent shadow on the wall. Pure Ditko, and reminiscent of his Doctor Strange work for Marvel. Artwork is approximately 10 1/2" x 16" with no white-out. Has some brown staining in the window at the top of the image, a glue stain where one stat is missing, and the logo stat seems to have "slipped" somewhat and does not quite match the background art.

Boris Vallejo - Original Art for Tor Books Edition of Dracula (1991). A stunning example of the dynamic, photo-realistic style that Vallejo made famous, this piece perfectly captures the elegance and the power of Count Dracula, Lord of the Vampires! One can almost hear the music of the creatures of the night as the Count surveys his darkly-lit domain, with a beautiful woman at his feet in typical Boris state of undress. Measuring a big 27" x 17", this piece is rendered in oil on board, tastefully matted and framed, and is in excellent condition. It is signed by the artist in the lower right corner.

Frank Frazetta - Original Oil Painting, Brooklyn Dreams (1962). BROOKLYN DREAMS is an oil painting by the great Frank Frazetta that was executed in 1961 or 1962 for Tower Publications as a possible cover for one of the men's paperback novels that were abundant at that time. It wasn't used at the time because of the financial failure of several of those companies (It was finally published in 1999 as "Pinup", on page 147 of Frank Frazetta: Legends, a copy of which will accompany this lot). It should be noted that Frazetta did a series of pen and brush "girlie" illustrations for a wide assortment of men's magazines and paperbacks during this period. All those originals have been lost or destroyed, with the exception of two pen illustrations and this oil. The piece features an image area of 13" x 20" and is in excellent condition. It is executed on standard illustration board. The reverse contains a series of lively jumping horse studies drawn in pencil. This oil is an excellent example of Frazetta's approach, a rich mix of power and subtlety. One can't help noticing the fascinating counterpoint between the explosiveness of the subject's voluptuous body and her rather pensive pose. The flesh tones feature beautifully blended colors, a Frazetta trademark. The breast area is a kaleidoscope of soft tints. The changing background colors reflect the changing moods in the girl's thoughts. This painting conjures a wonderful and beguiling story that speaks directly to the viewer's imagination. What is this scene? What is happening? Is she waiting for a lover? Is she a common prostitute dreaming of better things? Perhaps she is just trying to get some air on her balcony during a hot Brooklyn night? There is a narrative puzzle here that both enchants and satisfies.

Tomahawk #1 Mile High pedigree (DC, 1950). Debut issue with "Brand-New Adventures of America's Favorite Frontier Hero" with excellent cover/ story art by Fred Ray. This key issue has a wonderful, dark cover with intense deep-blue colors as Tomahawk faces down a group of hostile Indians. A beautiful book in every way with exceptional color strike, perfectly clean cover edges, sharp corners, near flawless spine and the "trademark" Mile High pedigree white paper. CGC notes: "Manufactured with large piece out margin of 2nd page, slightly affects story." This copy is unequalled in structure quality and freshness, as are most comic books from this most legendary of all pedigree collections.From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,500.

Vault of Horror #13 Gaines File pedigree 6/9 (EC, 1950). Remember those comics that terrified you as a kid? This was one of them. Despite the issue number, this is the true second issue of this title (it was WAR AGAINST CRIME for the first 11 issues). It's also memorable for the infamous Morphine story contained within its pages. Additionally, this is one of the scarcest Gaines File pedigree books, as there were only nine copies of this issue in the collection, instead of the usual twelve. According to CGC's most recent census report, a finer copy of this desirable book may not exist. Virtually perfect, this book is a pleasure to look at. CGC NM/MT 9.8 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,015.

Mad #3 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1953). One of the most trend-setting comics ever published, MAD has become a cultural icon. This is a beautiful example of the beginnings of MAD, before it went to magazine size. From the personal stash of MAD's publisher comes this copy, one of the nicest in existence according to CGC's current population report. A lovely copy that, except for a little tanning on the back cover, looks like it did when it came right off the presses. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $900.

Strange Adventures #10 White Mountain pedigree (DC, 1951). Great book featuring "Amazing Science-Fiction Tales", starring Captain Comet with a wonderful giant Space-Alien cover and art by Murphy Anderson, Carmine Infantino and Gil Kane. Gorgeous comic has color inks that seem to leap right off the cover with intense fire-reds and brilliant banana-yellows. Completely flat, very tight and fresh book has outstanding eye appeal. A beauty with a date stamp on first page (this date stamp is quite common on White Mountain books, and is one of the prime identifiers for this pedigree). One of the finest copies on planet Earth! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $900.

Mad #5 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1953). Low distribution, high grade and the Gaines File pedigree pedigree make this an incredibly desirable book! A nearly perfect copy with a tight spine, sharp corners, and just the most minor chipping at the top of the cover. You'll have to search long and hard for a nicer copy of this hard-to-find issue. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,600.

Mad #2 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1952). An excellent example of this rare book. Flat, tight spine, sharp corners, fabulous presentation. This is copy #3 of 12 from the Gaines File pedigree Collection. Classic Jack Davis baseball cover. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $1,500.

Mad #4 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1953). From the personal collection of William Gaines comes this exceptional copy of a classic comic. Good luck finding a nicer copy of this book. According to CGC, this is as nice as they've ever seen. Beautiful eye appeal, flat, tight spine, nice gloss...it doesn't get better than this. All wrapped up in a classic Kurtzman cover. CGC NM/MT 9.8 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $900.

Brenda Starr (Vol. 2), #10 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1949). Featuring "Romantic Adventures for Teen-agers" as Brenda Starr catches a would-be-assassin in her dressing room. Absolutely stellar book! As near to perfection as you can get with super sharp, rich colors, and completely fresh white "Mile High" paper. Most likely the very best copy in existence, bar none! CGC NM/MT 9.8 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value: $610.

War Against Crime #11 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1950). One of the nicest copies of this book in existence. This copy comes from the legendary Gaines File pedigree collection, and presents a nearly flawless appearance. Flat and clean, with razor sharp corners. CG NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $1,155.

Exciting Comics #26 Mile High pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1943). A truly exciting Schomburg WWII cover highlights this Golden Age beauty. According to CGC, this is by far the nicest copy in existence. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $525.

Mad #10 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). The #3 copy from the Gaines File pedigree, this is an outstanding example of this classic laffer. Only apparent problems are a small chip out of the top right corner, and a small color rub on the left edge of the cover, next to the "u" in "Jugular." CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $650.

Phantom Lady #15 Bethlehem pedigree (Fox Features Syndicate, 1947). Phantom Lady uses her unique Black Ray to fight crime on the cover of this great issue, which also includes excellent interior art by Matt Baker. Superb copy has rich, sharp colors, very supple white cover background and supple interior paper. Perfectly flat and tight with great eye appeal, this is the best copy we have seen at any time. Rare in this top-grade. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $2,250.

Detective Comics #121 Mile High pedigree (DC, 1947). Absolutely stunning copy! Extremely fresh appearance, with crisp colors. Only visible problems are a very slight amount of wear at the base of the spine, and a slight mis-cut. Truly outstanding. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $650.

Mad #8 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1953). A truly exceptional copy of a truly exceptional comic. The only visible flaws include a very slight spine abrasion and very slight tanning on the back cover. Otherwise, rich colors, sharp corners and excellent page quality make this book a real treasure. CGC's census shows that a nicer copy has not been found. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 9.4 value = $650.

House of Mystery #1 Northford pedigree (DC, 1952). DC's first horror comic features the cover story "Wanda Was a Werewolf". This is a beautiful copy of this tough issue, which has sharp, deep color strike, exact centering, perfectly clean edges and very sharp spine with no staple stress at all. Flat, clean and very tight with some light tanning on interior front and back cover. Very seldom seen above the VG to FN range, this is, without a doubt, one of the top copies of this key issue in existence.From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $2,000.

Brenda Starr(Vol. 2), #4 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1948). Used in the Senate Investigations on Juvenile Delinquency and featured in Seduction of the Innocent, this comic has a very attractive bondage cover by Jack Kamen with stunning deep sea-blue color inks and fully fresh white paper. Completely flat and tight with very sharp spine, cover edges, corners and near exact centering. Very scarce in this high grade, this is the finest copy we have ever seen. CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $740.

Fighting Yank #13 Mile High pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1945). The Fighting Yank to the rescue on this WWII bondage cover by Alex Schomburg. Near flawless book has an immaculate spine with no staple stress and incredibly deep color strike. An ideal example of why books from this legendary collection are so highly desired, unsurpassed in structure quality and paper freshness. Label note reads: "4 sets of Siamese pages." CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

Mad #6 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1953). Classic Kurtzman cover. An excellent comic from Bill Gaines' personal collection, this book shows very minor chipping at the bottom right corner, and a small ink stain on the back cover. Otherwise flat, clean, and sharp with exceptional paper quality. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $650.

Mad #7 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1953). More MADness from EC regulars Wood, Elder, and Severin, and six pages of early cartoons from Kurtzman. This is from the legendary Gaines File, the #3 (of 12) copy. The only apparent flaws are a slightly blunted upper left corner, and staples approximately 1/4" to the right of the spine. Very crisp, with vivid colors. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $650.

Brenda Starr #13 (#1) Mile High pedigree (Four Star, 1947). Vibrant sun-yellow cover accented with a rich ruby-red logo. Fully flat, tight and fresh with the "trademark" Mile High pedigree white pages. One of the finest copies of this book in existence! CGC VF+ 8.5 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $740.

The Black Terror #8 Pennsylvania pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1944). A classic Schomburg WWII cover highlights this high-grade beauty. Nearly perfect, this copy shows only some very minor spine stress. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $525.

Mad #11 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). A memorable Basil Wolverton cover leads off the #3 Gaines File pedigree copy of Mad #11, an outstanding example of this issue. Only obvious problems are a very lightly bumped lower left corner, very light wear along the top edge of the cover, and staples rolled very slightly onto the front cover. Exceptional freshness and clarity, the usual hallmarks of a Gaines File pedigree book, are readily evident here. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $650.

All-Flash #22 Mile High pedigree (DC, 1946). This book could have come right off the press. So nice it looks virtually unread, the spine is stress-free, the colors are deep and strong, and the corners are nice and sharp. There is an arrival date notation in grease pencil at the top of the front cover and a slight mis-cut, but this really doesn't reduce the overall eye appeal of this gorgeous book. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $550.

Captain America Comics #32 Crowley pedigree (Timely, 1943). This exceptional high-grade copy has a wonderful WWII cover by Syd Shores as Captain America dives to save young Bucky from certain death. Also included is a Human Torch back-up story. This copy offers superb colors with an excellent cut, clean cover edges and great eye-appeal. One of the finest copies of this book we have ever seen! CGC VF+ 8.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $537; NM 9.4 value = $1700.

Dark Mysteries #1 Aurora pedigree (Magazine Enterprises, 1951). Debut issue delivering "Thrilling Tales of Horror and Suspense" is high-lighted with cover and story art by the legendary Wally Wood. Very attractive flat, clean and supple book has bright, rich colors and nice fresh interior paper which is quite uncommon for these books. Spine is beautiful with secure staples and no stress marks of note. Bottom edge of front cover has the smallest chip in the black area. Very tough to find in the higher grades; one of the finest we have seen. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $975.

Speed Comics #38 Mile High pedigree (Harvey, 1945). Super-killer copy of this classic WW II patriotic cover that re-enacts the classic US Marine victory scene on the island of Iwo Jima. This comic approaches perfection in every sense of the word and is the finest copy known in existence! CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $400.

Brenda Starr (Vol. 2), #12 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1949). Great-looking top-grade book has wonderful deep sea-blue color inks with rich reds and bright yellows. Completely flat and very tight with a sharp spine, no staple stress and perfect centering. Truly, a superior book. The finest copy known! CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $620.

America's Best Comics #10 Mile High pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1944). Classic patriotic cover. Beautiful copy of a very desirable book. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

Strange Tales #115 Bethlehem pedigree (Marvel, 1963). The senses-shattering origin of Dr. Strange, Master of the Mystic Arts, by the one and only "Sturdy" Steve Ditko! Difficult to find in high-grade, this copy shows a date stamp on the cover in red ink, and slight evidence of wear at the top and bottom spine. None of this mars the incredible eye-appeal of this Silver Age classic. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $500.

Vault of Horror #39 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1954). Why settle for less when you can have the best? According to CGC's latest census report, there's not a finer copy of this book in existence. A gem of a comic, with a fabulous look. A stunningly subtle Johnny Craig bondage cover highlights this wonderful book. CGC NM/MT 9.8 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $240.

Mad #9 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). A hilarious Kurtzman cover leads off this classic issue of MAD. This is the #3 Gaines File pedigree copy, and displays bright, luminous colors and an exceptionally fresh appearance. The only visible flaws include a slightly bumped upper left corner and light spine stress. CGC VF/NM 9.0 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $650.

America's Best Comics #14 Mile High pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1945). An unbelievably nice copy from the pedigree that set the standard for quality. This fabulous Golden Age find is near-perfect, its only defect being a slight mis-cut. According to CGC, a finer copy does not exist. CGC NM/MT 9.8 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

Startling Comics #41 Mile High pedigree (Better Publications, 1946). This issue is highlighted by a classic Atom Bomb cover by Alex Schomburg. An exceptional copy, this book's only visible flaw is some light dust shadowing along the top cover. Otherwise, flat, clean and sharp, with incredible eye-appeal. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $360.

Mad #15 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). A rich turquoise background and a John Tenniel illustration from "Alice in Wonderland" make up the cover of this early issue of America's first satire comic. Sharp, flat, and clean, with outstanding page quality, CGC has not graded a nicer copy of this issue. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $525.

Crime SuspenStories #2 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1950). You'll find "Startling Tales of Tension" in this pristine example of a classic EC crime comic. A slight impact mark at the upper spine and some very minor dust shadows on the back cover are the only marks of age on this tight, flat copy. Gorgeous and eye appealing, this is a worthy addition to any collection. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $560.

Crime SuspenStories #20 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1953). Classic Johnny Craig hanging cover used in Seduction of the Innocent. From the collection of William Gaines, the front cover shows slight creasing at the bottom right corner and minor damage at the top right corner, but an attractive book overall. Vastly underpriced and extremely desirable. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $260.

Crime SuspenStories #22 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1954). Probably the most infamous of all of EC's gory images, this classic Johnny Craig ax decapitation cover was used in the Senate Investigations on Juvenile Delinquency and lead to that event's foremost sound bite. When asked by Senator Estes Kefauver if he thought this cover was in good taste, publisher Bill Gaines responded, "Yes, sir: I do. For the cover of a horror comic." Good taste or not, this copy is in excellent condition, with slight crimping at the upper spine and moderate spine stress. Great cover colors and an excellent cut combine to make this an extremely attractive and desirable copy. CGC VF/NM 9.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

Captain Marvel Adventures #29 Mile High pedigree (Fawcett, 1943). The "Big Red Cheese" stars in "Battle at the China Wall". This near flawless copy has very fresh interior paper, deep color strike and razor sharp corners. There is some minor stress at the very top of the spine. A truly beautiful book! CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $480.

Crypt of Terror #19 Bethlehem pedigree (EC, 1950). Last issue before title change has a spooky female Voodoo-Zombie cover by Johnny Craig with interior work by Al Feldstein and Graham "Ghastly" Ingels. Very high-grade flat, clean and tight book with full color depth and nice spine. Tough to find in this high grade! CGC VF/NM 9.0 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $2,800.

Mad #12 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). Another batch of merriment calculated to drive you Mad, this is the #3 Gaines File pedigree copy of this issue, and you'd be hard-pressed to find a better copy on the market. Square corners and outstanding crispness make this a sterling example of what GF books are all about. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $525.

Star Spangled Comics #17 Mile High pedigree (DC, 1943). A star-studded classic from the Golden Age wrapped in an exciting Simon & Kirby cover! This beautiful high-grade copy has a double cover (the first cover is CGC graded 7.5, the interior cover is CGC graded 8.5). The book shows only the most minor flaws, including a pencil notation of "D 11-20" in the upper left corner of the front cover and a pencilled "6" in the upper center, a very little bit of spine stress, a 2" crease in the upper left front cover and some slight creasing on the back cover. Stunning eye-appeal makes this an extremely desirable book. CGC VF+ 8.5 White pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $379; NM 9.4 value = $1,200.

Mad #17 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). The famous upside-down issue of Mad is well-represented here in this near-perfect example. This is the #3 Gaines File pedigree copy, and a very light crease at the top of the spine is the only apparent flaw. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

Vault of Horror #19 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1951). Johnny Craig's cover is an homage to true love. Flat, sharp and clean, this is a nearly-perfect copy of a very collectible book. According to CGC, this is equal to the finest copy they've seen. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

Vault of Horror #21 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1951). Super high grade Gaines File pedigree Copy of this classic comic. A beautiful book...the grade says it all. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $365.

Mad #14 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). A suitably classical cover (who knew Mona readsMad?) for a classic humor comic. This is the #3 Gaines File pedigree copy, and it's a beauty, with a crisp, fresh appearance and outstanding color. Only apparent flaws are a very small chip at the base of the spine, some very light spine stress, slight color rub at the top staple, and light diagonal creases at the top left edge. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $525.

Mad #16 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). Kurtzman's hilarious "newspaper" cover leads off this near-perfect copy of Mad. This is #3 from the Gaines File; razor-sharp corners and amazing clarity make this look as fresh as if it were printed yesterday. Only apparent flaws are the bottom staple rolled 1/4" on to the back cover, and both covers are slightly off-register. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

All-Flash #21 Mile High pedigree (DC, 1945). An exceptionally beautiful copy of this Golden Age gem. Clean and flat with rich colors and sharp corners, this would be a welcome addition to any high-grade collection. The fact that the book is slightly miscut is the only discernible flaw in an otherwise stunning copy. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $550.

Thrilling Comics #22 Mile High pedigree (Better Publications, 1941). The Mile High pedigree collection, well-known for high-quality, is superbly represented in this classic wartime book. CGC notes very minor color touch on cover, but this book has been graded as unrestored. There is a pencilled arrival date on the cover, but this is otherwise a clean, flat, and beautiful book. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $425.

Mad #19 Gaines File pedigree 3/9 (EC, 1955). Beautiful copy with an important pedigree. Sharp and clean, this book shows slight tanning along the edges, but very little else to detract from its outstanding look. Oddly, there were only nine copies of this issue in the Gaines File pedigree collection, as opposed to all other issues of Mad, of which there were 12, making this the scarcest of the Gaines File pedigree Mad's. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

Crime SuspenStories #3 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1951). Classic EC crime/suspense tales (including an amazing story by Harvey Kurtzman), and you can't get much better than a Gaines File pedigree copy. Absolutely no apparent flaws, with razor-sharp corners, vivid color, and amazing crispness. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $400.

The Haunt of Fear #10 Gaines File pedigree 2/12 (EC, 1951). Includes a stunning bullfight cover by Al Feldstein and a biography of "Ghastly" Graham Ingels. An exceptional copy of this classic issue from the files of William Gaines. A very small chip at the bottom spine and a slight bend at the upper spine do not detract from the exceptional eye-appeal of this beautiful book. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $415.

Vault of Horror #30 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1953). A gory severed arm cover highlights this high grade beauty. Part of the Gaines File pedigree set, this is a great looking copy that will enhance any collection. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $285.

Hit Comics #1 Cosmic Aeroplane pedigree (Quality, 1940). The Red Bee opens fire on this cover, illustrated by the talented Lou Fine. Debut issue also includes such back-up series as Hercules, Blaze Barton and Neon the Unknown. Attractive mid-grade copy has superbly deep color strike and exact centering. Flat and supple with some stress along spine edges and at left edge of back cover. No chips, no large tears and no large creases. CGC FN+ 6.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $1,775; NM 9.4 value = $6,800.

The Amazing Spider-Man #32 Slabodian pedigree (Marvel, 1966). Spidey stars in "Man on a Rampage!" Cover and story art by Steve Ditko. Super-killer copy of this classic issue has bright, sharp colors, no "Marvel chipping", and an immaculate spine. Rare in this top-grade and a must see comic! CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

Fighting Yank #12 Big Apple pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1945). America's Bravest Defender has the Imperial Japanese Army subdued on this Hirohito bondage cover by Alex Schomburg. Top-grade book has vibrant color strike and a near flawless spine with full white paper. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $500.

Journey into Mystery #1 Aurora pedigree (Atlas, 1952). Very good-looking copy of this scarce first issue has Russ Heath cover art. Flat, clean and very glossy book has tremendous color inks and excellent overall eye appeal. No chipping with some wear along spine including a 1/2" split at bottom and a 1" split at top. One of the best copies of this very tough-to-find issue we have seen. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC FN+ 6.5 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $900; NM 9.4 value = $3,100.

Mad #21 Gaines File pedigree 3/11 (EC, 1955). This issue features the very first cover appearance of Alfred E. Neuman in a fake ad. This copy shows slight tanning along the edges, but is otherwise very attractive and desirable. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $420.

Crime SuspenStories #8 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1951). Issue features artwork by Jack Kamen, Graham Ingels and Johnny Craig with a chilling cover by Craig as well. Incredible copy of this issue is nearly perfect in every way possible. Full color strike, perfectly clean edges and razor sharp corners. Spine is immaculate, centering exact with very fresh interior paper. One of the very finest copies in the world. Absolutely stunning! CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $320.

Brenda Starr(Vol. 2), #6 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1949). Here's a high-grade fresh, clean and flat book from the most famous of all pedigree comic collections. Minor stress at top left corner of back cover but no chips or tears. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $610.

Wonder Comics #20 Diamond Run pedigree (Better Comics, 1948). Stunning copy of this last issue has a great "Good-Girl" airbrush cover by Alex "Xela" Schomburg and interior story art by Frank Frazetta on The Silver Knight. This near flawless book is superb in every way with full colors, sharp spine and perfectly clean cover edges. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $655.

Exciting Comics #51 Big Apple pedigree (Nedor Publications, 1946). The Black Terror has crime "In Hand" and on the run on this cover by the legendary Alex Schomburg. This issue also features the first appearance of Miss Masque. Killer, near flawless copy has supple off-white cover and interior paper. Exceptional comic!From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $575.

X-Men #8 Massachusetts pedigree (Marvel, 1964). The X-Men face the uncanny threat of "Unus the Untouchable" (first appearance), with cover and story artwork by Jack "King" Kirby. Top-grade, supple copy is perfectly flat, clean and fully glossy with superb eye-appeal. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $375.

Mad #22 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1955). With a faux-Picasso cover, this is a great example of a nearly perfect book from an historically important collection. A great addition to any collection. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

The Haunt of Fear #11 Gaines File pedigree 2/12 (EC, 1952). The gruesome cover by "Ghastly" Graham Ingels made this book a best "cellar"! A near-perfect copy, with great color, sharp corners and exceptional page quality. A real gem. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $310.

Vault of Horror #31 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1953). A beautiful, high grade copy from the classic Gaines File pedigree collection, contains an adaptation of a story by, and a biography of, Ray Bradbury. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $285.

Brenda Starr (Vol 2), #7 Mile High pedigree (Superior, 1949). Good-looking book has rich color strike, fully fresh white pages and good eye appeal. Bottom corner of spine has an impact dent. No other defects of note. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $610.

Mad #23 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1955). The very last comic issue of this landmark title. Starting with the next issue,Mad switched to magazine format to escape the restrictions of the newly-formed Comics Code Authority. This copy, from the legendary Gaines File pedigree collection, is in excellent condition with few visible flaws. A key issue of this classic title. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

Mad #20 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1955). A comic designed to be snuck into class! From the Gaines File pedigree collection, this copy has few visible flaws, save for a small crease in the upper left back cover. One word: Beautiful. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $420.

Crime SuspenStories #11 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1952). An eerie cover from EC mainstay Johnny Craig leads off this classic crime book from Entertaining Comics. Another beautiful Gaines File pedigree copy the only apparent flaws are a tiny crease in the top right corner, and a slight mis-fold (cover and staples are slightly off-register). CGC NM+ 9.6 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.6 value = $240.

Tales From the Crypt #43 Gaines File pedigree 2/12 (EC, 1954). An outstanding Jack Davis cover leads off this superb entry into the annals of EC horror. Davis, Jack Kamen, and Graham Ingels all lend their talents to tales of terror. Not to mention the fact that, not only is this copy from the legendary Gaines Files , but it's the #2 (of 12) copy to boot! A beautiful example with square corners and excellent clarity. Only visible problems are a bit of spine stress and very light wear along the top edge of the front cover. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $105; NM 9.4 value = $385.

Mad #13 Gaines File pedigree 3/12 (EC, 1954). Harvey Kurtzman's humorous "smallest title in the world" cover is even more striking in such high grade, with a velvety field of dark red lending incredible eye appeal to this outstanding book. This is the #3 Gaines File pedigree copy, and has all the crispness and clarity you would normally associate with that elite grouping. Slightly lower CGC grade is likely due to a series of very small flaws, including a small chip in the lower left edge of the back cover, and some very, very light spine stress, also on the back. There is a very light color scuff at the bottom left corner of the front cover and a very light color break at the top left corner. Overall, this is a tremendously appealing example of this classic comic. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $525.

Startling Comics #46 Okajima pedigree (Better Publications, 1947). A classic Graham Ingels bondage cover highlights this high-grade beauty. Slight discoloration of the back cover spine, some light pencil notations on the front cover, and two date stamps on the back cover do not detract from the overwhelming appeal of this Golden Age gem. CGC VF+ 8.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $285; NM 9.4 value = $900.

Tales From the Crypt #42 Gaines File pedigree 2/12 (EC, 1954). A gorgeous copy with a classic Jack Davis vampire cover. Stunning color, flat and clean (as one would expect from a Gaines File pedigree copy), the only noted flaw is a slight mis-cut. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $385.

Journey into Mystery #2 Aurora pedigree (Atlas, 1952). Very attractive copy has stellar color inks and gloss with clean edges (only 1 small chip at the bottom edge of the front cover) and very secure staples (no stress). Spine is flat and tight with only a few soft stress lines. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC FN/VF 7.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $300; NM 9.4 value = $950.

Crime SuspenStories #10 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1952). Books from the Gaines File pedigree tend to be the Holy Grail for most EC collectors, and here's an amazing example of this classic EC crime comic. This is truly gorgeous! One tiny (1/8") nick at the base of the spine is the only evident flaw. Cover is still crisp and vivid. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $320.

Startling Terror Tales #11 Northford pedigree (Star Publications, 1952). One of the most sought-after of all horror comics featuring the classic "Ghost Spider of Death" cover by L.B. Cole. Incredible colors with fire-reds and bright yellows make this scene leap right off the cover. Spine is tight with no staple stress. Back cover has some chipping and tears at bottom and top edges. Label note reads: "heavy tanning interior front and back cover". One look at this cover and you will see why this issue is always in such high demand. CGC FN+ 6.5 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $316; NM 9.4 value = $1,000.

Teen-Age Dope Slaves #1 Aurora pedigree (Harvey, 1952). This book presents a "Shocking Dope Expose"! Popular first and only issue depicts the reality of drug abuse among teen-agers, which was used in the infamous book Seduction of the Innocent. This is a very attractive copy of an issue that is almost non-existent in the higher grades. Great colors and gloss with supple paper and very clean edges. Spine has a few light dings. From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $285; NM 9.4 value = $900.

Vault of Horror #26 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1952). Classic Johnny Craig cover. Illustrations from this issue were used in Parade of Pleasure. Beautiful copy with a very small chip at the bottom spine, otherwise sharp, flat and clean. A great addition to any high-grade collection. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $365.

Crime SuspenStories #19 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1953). Rich Al Feldstein cover. Great color and sharp corners on a nearly perfect copy from the private collection of Bill Gaines. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

Crime SuspenStories #14 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1952). Brutal Johnny Craig cover. An excellent example of a stunning Gaines File pedigree book. Clean and crisp, with only the most minor flaws. According to CGC, there is not a nicer copy of this book. CGC NM+ 9.6 Cream to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $240.

Picture Stories from the Bible #3: Old Testament Gaines File pedigree (DC / EC, 1943). An outstanding copy of this hard-to-find issue. Deep greens, bright yellows and vivid reds on the cover make this an extremely attractive book. A nicer copy would be hard to find. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $190.

Contact Comics #10 Big Apple pedigree (Aviation Press, 1946). Magnificent copy of this comic book has an eye-catching WWII Bomber cover by L.B. Cole. Stunning colors, sharp corners and perfect cover edges with outstanding overall eye appeal. We've never seen a copy that even comes close to this one! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $300.

The Haunt of Fear #28 Gaines File pedigree 1/12 (EC, 1954). Fabulous high-grade copy of one of the seminal horror titles of the 1950s. A "Ghastly" Graham Ingels cover highlights this exceptionally high-grade copy with interior art by Jack Davis, Jack Kamen, and Ingels. Virtually flawless, this is a great addition to any high-grade EC collection. Additionally, this issue bears the distinction of being #1 from the Gaines File pedigree set. #1 File issues are extremely prized by EC collectors, so this is a unique opportunity to acquire this exceptional collectible. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $230.

Suspense Comics #2 Crowley pedigree (Continental Magazines, 1944). Sporting a Nina Albright cover, this copy shows moderate spine wear, a bit of spine roll, some light tanning, and a slight miscut. A nice copy nonetheless of a hard to find issue with L.B. Cole interior art. CGC FN 6.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $885.

Crime SuspenStories #18 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1953). A classic Johnny Craig cover highlights this stunning copy. Deep, rich colors, sharp corners, and exceptional page quality make this an extremely desirable book. CGC NM 9.4 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

Picture Stories From American History #2 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1946). Large 52-page comic depicts America's period of colonization and independence. Super killer copy in every way with a perfect spine, no staple stress at all, perfectly clean cover edges and razor sharp corners. Incredible with very supple off-white paper. Most likely the finest copy in existence. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $195.

Valor #1 Gaines File pedigree 12/12 (EC, 1955). The premiere issue of this "New Direction" title, sporting a gorgeous Wally Wood cover showing two knights in mounted combat, was not approved by the newly-formed Comics Code. Clean, sharp and flat, with outstanding page quality. A worthy addition to any collection. CGC NM- 9.2 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $260.

Picture Stories from the Bible: Complete New Testament Edition Gaines File pedigree (EC, 1946). Incredible copy of this large square-bound issue features the classic life story of Jesus Christ, from his birth in Bethlehem, death at Calvary, and resurrection. Also included is the story of Paul's missionary journeys. Nearly perfect with tremendous color strike, sharp edges and corners and fresh white paper. 6 interior pages are siamese. The finest copy we have ever seen! CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $190.

Treasure Comics #7 Big Apple pedigree (Prize Publications, 1946). Stunning copy of this esoteric comic has incredible colors, perfect edges, immaculate spine, no staple stress and full paper freshness. Exceptional in every way! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $300.

The Brave and the Bold #30 Bethlehem pedigree (DC, 1960). The third appearance of the Justice League of America as they face Amazo, the Amazing Android! This copy shows minimal spine wear with slightly rounded corners and a date stamp on the back cover. All in all, a very attractive and presentable book. CGC VF- 7.5 Light tan to off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 FN 6.0 value = $407; NM 9.4 value = $1,900.

Master Comics #55 Mile High pedigree (Fawcett, 1944). A fabulous example of a high-grade treasure. There are two pencil notations at the upper left front cover, but in other regards, an exceptionally beautiful copy. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $175.

Captain Flight #10 Big Apple pedigree (Four Star Publications, 1945). Incredible blood-red and bright sun-yellow color inks with clean cover edges and fully fresh white paper. Flat and clean with a small chip at lower corner of spine and a thin line of color loss near center of spine. There is a 2" tear on centerfold. Never seen a finer copy! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF/NM 9.0 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $425.

Fight Comics #43 Big Apple pedigree (Fiction House, 1946). Great pagan sacrifice-bondage cover highlights this issue with interior art by the talented Matt Baker. Super-killer copy has an immaculate spine (no staple stress), perfect cover edges and sharp corners. Unreal! From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC NM+ 9.6 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $185.

Gang Busters #23 Mile High pedigree (DC, 1951). This is a book with everything going for it! Not only is it in beautiful condition, with deep, rich cover colors, sharp corners and a good cut, it's also part of the Mile High pedigree collection, a pedigree known for superlative condition. In addition, this copy also boasts a double cover (CGC has noted that both covers grade 9.6)! Even among Mile High pedigree comics, as rare and desirable as they are, this book is a gem. A unique opportunity to acquire a unique book. CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $100.

Smash Comics #45 Mile High pedigree (Quality, 1943). An exceptional example of a book that is hard to find in high grade. This copy shows only the most minor imperfections, including two small chips at the top front cover, slight spine stress and a small tear at the upper back spine. Overall, a very attractive and desirable book. CGC VF/NM 9.0 White pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $300.

Crime SuspenStories #17 Gaines File pedigree 8/11 (EC, 1953). Johnny Craig comes through again with another graphic cover. This issue includes an adaptation of Ray Bradbury's "Touch and Go", and a beautiful collaboration between Al Williamson and Frank Frazetta. The upper right corner shows some slight damage, but it barely detracts from the overall appeal of this great book. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white pages. Overstreet NM 9.4 value = $300.

Star Spangled Comics #51 Crowley pedigree (DC, 1945). A killer book that stars The Guardian and Newsboy Legion with a Robot cover by Joe Simon and Jack Kirby. Stunner with superb colors, perfectly clean edges and very tight spine and staples (no stress). Staples are placed about 1/2" in from spine edge. The best copy we have ever seen. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $450.

Jungle Comics #79 Big Apple pedigree (Fiction House, 1946). Superior copy of this issue stars Ka'a'nga, Jungle Lord in "Death has a Thousand Fangs". Frontline Combat #7 Gaines File pedigree 7/10 (EC, 1952). Classic Harvey Kurtzman cover highlights a special Iwo Jima issue. A fabulous copy with great eye-appeal from a legendary collection. Picture Stories from the Bible: Complete "Life of Christ" Edition Gaines File pedigree (DC / EC, 1944). Large 96 page square-bound edition featuring the complete life of Jesus Christ from his birth in a manger in Bethlehem, to his death on the cross at Calvary, to his resurrection. Phenomenal copy, nearly perfect in every way, with tremendous eye-appeal and very fresh interior paper. Probably the finest copy in the world! CGC NM+ 9.6 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $190.

Li'l Abner #63 Mile High pedigree (Harvey, 1948). Tight spine, sharp corners, and great eye appeal highlight this classic issue featuring the Hero of Dogpatch. Strange Terrors #1 Aurora pedigree (St. John, 1952). This issue features "Zombies March by Moonlight" and has a dungeon bondage cover. Good-looking copy has great gloss and colors. Nice copy that is scarce above the FN range.From the private collection of Marnin Rosenberg. CGC VF 8.0 Off-white pages. Overstreet FN 6.0 value = $150; NM 9.4 value = $450.

Valor #2 Gaines File pedigree 12/12 (EC, 1955). Part of EC's "New Direction", this comic presented "Tales of Mortal Combat, and Deeds of Valor!" Otherwise, a stunning copy with a great gladiator cover by Al Williamson. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $220.

Crime SuspenStories #23 Gaines File pedigree 9/12 (EC, 1954). Great George Evans cover highlights an issue that was used in the Senate Investigations on Juvenile Delinquency. CGC NM- 9.2 Off-white to white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $210.

Frontline Combat #9 Gaines File pedigree 7/10 (EC, 1952). Special Civil War issue. This EC gem shows a slight bend at the top spine, but few other visible flaws. Only 10 copies of this issue were included in the Gaines File pedigree collection. CGC NM 9.4 Off-white pages. Overstreet 2001 NM 9.4 value = $175.

Startling Comics #35 Mile High pedigree (Better Publications, 1945). A classic drug story highlights this Golden Age gem, all wrapped within an incredible Schomburg cover.
